  iatf_certified boolean [note: 'IATF 16949 certified']
  is_active boolean [note: 'Active flag']
  plant_code varchar [note: 'Plant code']
  plant_name varchar [note: 'Plant name']

  Note: 'Represents the specific manufacturing or distribution plant/site of a supplier that ships parts to an OEM assembly plant. Captures plant address, plant code, production capabilities, capacity constraints, dock-to-dock lead time, customs zone, and plant-level quality certifications. Enables JIT/JIS scheduling at the plant-to-plant level and supports CKD/SKD kit origin tracking.'
}

Table supply.inbound_part {
  inbound_part_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the inbound part record.']
  commodity_position_id varchar [note: 'FK to the commodity position covering this inbound part material.']
  compliance_document_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.compliance_document. Business justification: Part Certification Management process stores the compliance document (e.g., type‑approval) associated with each inbound part.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Material receipt cost is allocated to a Cost Center for inventory valuation and variance analysis.']
  part_master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to engineering.part_master. Business justification: Inbound receipt process matches each received part to the engineering part master for quality verification and BOM reconciliation.']
  regulatory_requirement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_requirement. Business justification: Regulatory Requirement Mapping report links each inbound part to the regulatory requirement it must satisfy for emissions and safety compliance.']
  sku_master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.sku_master. Business justification: Receiving process matches inbound parts to SKU master to create accurate stock records; essential for inventory posting.']
  supply_supplier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.supply_supplier. Business justification: Connect inbound part to internal supplier master for analytics']
  average_cost decimal(12,4) [note: 'Weighted average purchase cost per unit of the part.']
  commodity_group varchar [note: 'High‑level classification of the part for procurement and reporting.']
  country_of_origin varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO code of the country where the part was manufactured.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the inbound part record was first created.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for the part\'s cost.']
  customs_tariff_code varchar [note: 'HS‑8 tariff classification code used for import duties.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date from which the part information is considered valid.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date after which the part information is no longer valid (nullable).']
  engineering_change_level varchar [note: 'Level of engineering change applied to the part (e.g., A‑E).']
  hazardous_material_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the part is classified as hazardous under regulatory rules.']
  height_mm decimal(10,2) [note: 'Physical height of the part in millimetres.']
  last_received_date date [note: 'Date when the part was most recently received at the plant.']
  last_received_quantity varchar [note: 'Quantity of the part received on the most recent receipt.']
  lead_time_days varchar [note: 'Planned number of days from order placement to receipt.']
  length_mm decimal(10,2) [note: 'Physical length of the part in millimetres.']
  lifecycle_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the part within the supply chain.']
  lot_size varchar [note: 'Standard production batch size for the part.']
  material_type varchar [note: 'Category indicating whether the part is raw material, sub‑assembly, CKD kit, finished good, or service item.']
  minimum_order_quantity varchar [note: 'Smallest quantity that can be ordered from the supplier.']
  oem_part_number varchar [note: 'Original Equipment Manufacturer part number used internally for cross‑reference.']
  part_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name or description of the purchased part.']
  ppap_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the Production Part Approval Process for the part.']
  price_uom varchar [note: 'Unit of measure used for the price (e.g., each, kilogram).']
  reorder_point_quantity varchar [note: 'Inventory level that triggers a new purchase order.']
  safety_stock_quantity varchar [note: 'Buffer inventory quantity maintained to protect against supply variability.']
  supplier_part_number varchar [note: 'Identifier assigned by the external supplier to the part.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Standard unit used for ordering and inventory management.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time of the most recent modification to the record.']
  weight_kg decimal(12,3) [note: 'Net weight of the part in kilograms.']
  width_mm decimal(10,2) [note: 'Physical width of the part in millimetres.']

  Note: 'Master record for every purchased part number sourced from external suppliers. Captures OEM part number, supplier part number cross-reference, commodity group, material type (raw, sub-assembly, CKD kit), unit of measure, PPAP approval status, engineering change level, hazardous material flag, country of origin, and customs tariff code. Bridges SAP MM material master and PTC Windchill parts classification for supply-domain-owned purchased parts.'
}

Table supply.sourcing_nomination {
  sourcing_nomination_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for each sourcing nomination record.']
  sourcing_buyer_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the internal commodity buyer responsible for the nomination.']
  sourcing_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the internal commodity buyer responsible for the nomination.']
  supply_supplier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.supply_supplier. Business justification: Link sourcing nomination to internal supplier master']
  comments varchar [note: 'Additional notes or remarks entered by the buyer or sourcing team.']
  commodity varchar [note: 'High‑level classification of the part or commodity being nominated (e.g., ENGINE, HVAC, ELECTRICAL).']
  commodity_index_linked_flag boolean [note: 'Whether the sourcing nomination pricing is linked to a commodity index.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the nomination record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for the target piece price.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Optional date when the nomination expires or is superseded.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date from which the nomination becomes effective for planning purposes.']
  is_jis boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the nominated part is required under a JIS delivery strategy.']
  is_jit boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the nominated part is required under a JIT delivery strategy.']
  kit_type varchar [note: 'Specifies whether the part is supplied as a Completely Knocked Down (CKD) or Semi‑Knocked Down (SKD) kit, or not a kit.']
  model_year varchar [note: 'Model year for which the part nomination applies.']
  nominated_volume decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total quantity of the part the supplier is expected to deliver for the program.']
  nomination_date timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the OEM formally recorded the nomination.']
  nomination_number varchar [note: 'Human‑readable business code assigned to the nomination for tracking and reference.']
  nomination_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the nomination.']
  part_number varchar [note: 'Manufacturer part number or PLM identifier for the component subject to nomination.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Business priority assigned to the nomination for execution planning.']
  program_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the vehicle program or platform associated with the nomination (e.g., SUV2025, EVX).']
  region varchar [note: 'Region code indicating the primary supply geography for the nominated part.']
  risk_rating varchar [note: 'Qualitative assessment of supply risk for the nominated part.']
  sor_reference varchar [note: 'Reference code linking to the SOR document that defines functional and performance requirements.']
  target_piece_price decimal(12,4) [note: 'Target unit price (per piece) the OEM aims to achieve with the nominated supplier.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the nomination record.']

  Note: 'Records the formal OEM decision to nominate a specific supplier for a given part or commodity within a model year program. Captures nomination date, program/platform code, nominated supplier, awarded annual volume, target piece price, SOR (Statement of Requirements) reference, nomination status (nominated, confirmed, withdrawn), and the responsible commodity buyer. Precedes the RFQ and PPAP process. SSOT for sourcing award decisions; distinct from procurement domain\'s strategic sourcing strategy — this is the operational award record.'
}

Table supply.rfq {
  rfq_id varchar [pk, note: 'System‑generated unique identifier for the RFQ record.']
  part_master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to engineering.part_master. Business justification: RFQ generation is driven by engineering part definitions; linking to part_master enables accurate pricing and specification alignment.']
  rfq_buyer_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the internal buying organization or department originating the RFQ.']
  rfq_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the internal buying organization or department originating the RFQ.']
  supply_supplier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.supply_supplier. Business justification: Associate RFQ with internal supplier master']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current status of internal approval workflow.']
  attachment_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether supporting documents are attached to the RFQ.']
  commodity_code varchar [note: 'Standard industry code classifying the type of material or service requested.']
  commodity_index_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to commodity price index for material cost escalation.']
  compliance_requirements varchar [note: 'Regulatory or quality standards the supplier must meet (e.g., IATF 16949, ISO 14001).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the RFQ record was first created in the system.']
  delivery_schedule_type varchar [note: 'Preferred delivery methodology (Just‑In‑Time, Just‑In‑Sequence, or standard).']
  discount_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Any discount the supplier proposes relative to the target price.']
  eprocurement_portal_code varchar [note: 'FK to the e-procurement portal through which this RFQ was issued.']
  issue_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the RFQ was issued to suppliers.']
  last_status_change_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent change to the RFQ status.']
  net_price_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Net price after discounts, taxes, and fees.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes entered by the buyer.']
  part_description varchar [note: 'Free‑text description of the part or service requested.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Business priority assigned to the RFQ.']
  program_model_year varchar [note: 'Vehicle program or model year for which the part is required.']
  quantity_estimate varchar [note: 'Projected annual volume the buyer expects to purchase.']
  regulatory_approval_required boolean [note: 'True if the part requires regulatory sign‑off before purchase.']
  required_delivery_date date [note: 'Date by which the part must be delivered to the plant.']
  response_due_date date [note: 'Last date on which suppliers may submit their quotations.']
  rfq_number varchar [note: 'Business‑visible RFQ number assigned by the sourcing system.']
  rfq_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the RFQ.']
  rfq_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the RFQ request.']
  target_price_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Maximum price the buyer is willing to pay for the quoted item (gross).']
  target_price_currency varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 currency code for the target price.']
  tooling_description varchar [note: 'Details of the tooling requirements, if any.']
  tooling_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether new tooling is needed for the part.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Unit in which the quantity is expressed.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the RFQ record.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User identifier of the employee who created the RFQ.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of a Request for Quotation event issued to one or more suppliers for a specific part, assembly, or service. Captures RFQ number, issue date, response due date, commodity, target price, annual volume estimate, program/model year, tooling requirements, and RFQ status (open, closed, awarded, cancelled). Integrates with SAP MM RFQ (ME41) and PTC Windchill supplier collaboration portal.'
}

Table supply.rfq_response {
  rfq_response_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the RFQ response record.']
  rfq_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the original Request for Quotation to which this response belongs.']
  supply_supplier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.supply_supplier. Business justification: Associate RFQ response with internal supplier master']
  amortization_term_months varchar [note: 'Number of months over which the tooling cost is amortized.']
  capacity_commitment decimal(18,4) [note: 'Maximum production capacity the supplier commits to deliver per period.']
  capacity_unit varchar [note: 'Unit for the capacity commitment (e.g., units_per_month).']
  commodity_surcharge_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Commodity surcharge amount included in the quoted price.']
  compliance_certifications varchar [note: 'List of regulatory or industry certifications attached to the quotation.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the RFQ response record was first created in the system.']
  delivery_address varchar [note: 'Physical address where the supplier will deliver the goods.']
  discount_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage discount offered on the quoted unit price.']
  exceptions_to_sor varchar [note: 'Supplier‑provided deviations or exceptions to the original SOR.']
  freight_included boolean [note: 'True if freight costs are included in the total price.']
  is_preferred_supplier boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the supplier is marked as a preferred source for this part.']
  lead_time_days varchar [note: 'Number of calendar days the supplier needs to deliver the quoted items after order confirmation.']
  payment_terms varchar [note: 'Standard payment condition offered by the supplier.']
  portal_submission_flag boolean [note: 'Whether this response was submitted via e-procurement portal.']
  quoted_currency varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 currency code of the quoted price.']
  quoted_quantity decimal(18,4) [note: 'Quantity of items the supplier is quoting for.']
  quoted_unit_price decimal(18,4) [note: 'Price per unit quoted by the supplier.']
  regulatory_approval_status varchar [note: 'Status of any required regulatory approvals for the quoted part.']
  remarks varchar [note: 'Additional free‑form comments from the supplier.']
  response_number varchar [note: 'External reference number assigned to the supplier\'s quotation response.']
  response_source varchar [note: 'Channel through which the supplier submitted the quotation.']
  rfq_response_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the RFQ response.']
  shipping_method varchar [note: 'Mode of transportation for delivering the goods.']
  submission_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the supplier submitted the quotation.']
  tax_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Estimated tax component included in the quotation.']
  tooling_cost decimal(18,4) [note: 'One‑time cost for tooling or molds required to produce the part.']
  total_price decimal(18,4) [note: 'Total amount for the quoted quantity (unit price multiplied by quantity).']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Measurement unit for the quoted quantity.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the RFQ response record.']
  validity_end_date date [note: 'Last date on which the quoted terms are valid.']
  validity_start_date date [note: 'First date on which the quoted terms are valid.']
  warranty_period_months varchar [note: 'Length of warranty coverage offered by the supplier.']
  warranty_type varchar [note: 'Type of warranty provided.']

  Note: 'Captures a supplier\'s formal quotation response to an RFQ. Records quoted unit price, tooling cost, amortization terms, lead time, capacity commitment, exceptions to SOR, validity period, and response status (submitted, under review, accepted, rejected). Supports competitive bid analysis and supplier selection decisions. One RFQ may have multiple responses from competing suppliers.'
}

Table supply.supply_purchase_order {
  supply_purchase_order_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'FK to cost center']
  supply_supplier_id varchar [note: 'FK to supplier']
  vehicle_order_id varchar [note: 'FK to vehicle order']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Created timestamp']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Currency code']
  delivery_date date [note: 'Expected delivery date']
  incoterms varchar [note: 'Incoterms']
  order_date date [note: 'Order date']
  payment_terms varchar [note: 'Payment terms']
  po_number varchar [note: 'PO number']
  po_status varchar [note: 'PO status']
  po_type varchar [note: 'PO type']
  total_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total PO amount']

  Note: 'Legally binding procurement document issued to a supplier authorizing delivery of parts or materials at agreed price and schedule. Captures PO number, PO type (standard, blanket, scheduling agreement), supplier, plant, delivery terms (Incoterms), payment terms, total value, currency, and PO status (open, partially delivered, closed, cancelled). SSOT for purchase commitments; sourced from SAP MM (ME21N/ME22N).'
}

Table supply.supply_po_line {
  supply_po_line_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'FK to GL account']
  order_line_id varchar [note: 'FK to order line']
  sku_master_id varchar [note: 'FK to SKU master']
  supply_purchase_order_id varchar [note: 'FK to PO']
  supply_supplier_id varchar [note: 'FK to supplier']

  goods_receipt_quantity decimal(12,4) [note: 'GR quantity']
  line_number varchar [note: 'Line number']
  line_status varchar [note: 'Line status']
  net_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Net amount']

  quantity decimal(12,4) [note: 'Order quantity']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Unit of measure']
  unit_price decimal(18,4) [note: 'Unit price']

  Note: 'Individual line item within a purchase order representing a specific part number, quantity, unit price, delivery date, and plant destination. Captures line number, material number, ordered quantity, confirmed quantity, net price, delivery date, goods receipt quantity, and invoice quantity. Enables line-level tracking of delivery performance and invoice matching (3-way match) in SAP MM.'
}

Table supply.scheduling_agreement {
  scheduling_agreement_id varchar [pk, note: 'System generated unique identifier for the scheduling agreement record.']
  approved_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Approval of scheduling agreements requires a designated employee; used in Scheduling Agreement Approval Log report.']
  part_master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to engineering.part_master. Business justification: Scheduling agreements are created per engineering part to lock volume, price, and delivery cadence with the supplier.']
  supply_supplier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.supply_supplier. Business justification: Link scheduling agreement to internal supplier master']
  actual_otd_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Measured on‑time delivery performance for the agreement period.']
  actual_ppm decimal(5,2) [note: 'Measured defect rate for parts delivered under the agreement.']
  agreement_number varchar [note: 'External business identifier assigned to the scheduling agreement (e.g., contract number).']
  agreement_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the scheduling agreement (e.g., framework, spot, consignment).']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date on which the agreement received formal approval.']
  commodity_escalation_clause_flag boolean [note: 'Whether the scheduling agreement includes a commodity price escalation clause.']
  compliance_approval_status varchar [note: 'Regulatory or quality compliance status of the agreement.']
  compliance_document_ref varchar [note: 'Reference number of the attached compliance documentation.']
  contract_scope varchar [note: 'High‑level description of the parts, services, or components covered.']
  contract_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier for the agreement when amendments are made.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the scheduling agreement record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 code of the currency used for pricing.']
  delivery_rhythm varchar [note: 'Scheduled frequency of deliveries defined in the agreement.']
  early_termination_allowed boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether the agreement can be terminated before the end date.']
  end_date date [note: 'Date on which the agreement expires or is terminated (nullable for open‑ended contracts).']
  kanban_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the agreement uses a Kanban pull system for JIT deliveries.']
  part_description varchar [note: 'Human‑readable description of the part covered by the agreement.']
  payment_terms varchar [note: 'Standard payment condition agreed with the supplier.']
  penalty_clause varchar [note: 'Text describing penalties for missed deliveries or quality breaches.']
  portal_managed_flag boolean [note: 'Whether this scheduling agreement is managed via e-procurement portal.']
  price_per_unit decimal(18,4) [note: 'Agreed price for one unit of the supplied part.']
  release_horizon_weeks varchar [note: 'Number of weeks in advance that delivery forecasts must be submitted.']
  renewal_notice_period_days varchar [note: 'Notice period required to exercise renewal option.']
  renewal_option boolean [note: 'Indicates if the agreement includes an automatic renewal provision.']
  scheduling_agreement_description varchar [note: 'Free‑text description of the purpose, scope and key terms of the agreement.']
  scheduling_agreement_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the scheduling agreement.']
  start_date date [note: 'Date on which the agreement becomes binding.']
  target_otd_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Target percentage for on‑time deliveries defined in the agreement.']
  target_ppm decimal(5,2) [note: 'Maximum allowable defect rate for supplied parts.']
  termination_notice_period_days varchar [note: 'Number of days notice required to terminate the agreement early.']
  total_annual_volume decimal(18,2) [note: 'Planned total quantity of parts to be supplied over the contract year.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Measurement unit for the volume (e.g., pieces, kilograms).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the scheduling agreement record.']

  Note: 'Long-term supply agreement with a supplier defining the framework for JIT/JIS delivery of parts over a model year or contract period. Captures agreement number, validity start/end dates, target annual volume, release horizon (firm/forecast weeks), delivery rhythm (daily, weekly), Kanban flag, and agreement status. The scheduling agreement is the backbone of JIT supply in SAP MM (ME31L/ME32L).'
}

Table supply.supply_delivery_schedule {
  supply_delivery_schedule_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key']
  supply_purchase_order_id varchar [note: 'FK to PO']
  supply_supplier_id varchar [note: 'FK to supplier']
  actual_delivery_date date [note: 'Actual delivery date']
  actual_quantity decimal(12,4) [note: 'Actual quantity delivered']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Created timestamp']
  delivery_status varchar [note: 'Delivery status']
  part_number varchar [note: 'Part number']
  schedule_type varchar [note: 'Schedule type']
  scheduled_date date [note: 'Scheduled delivery date']
  scheduled_quantity decimal(12,4) [note: 'Scheduled quantity']

  Note: 'JIT/JIS delivery schedule line issued against a scheduling agreement, specifying exact quantities and delivery dates/times for a part to a plant dock. Captures schedule line date, time, required quantity, cumulative quantity, schedule type (firm, forecast, JIS sequence), dock door, and transmission status (sent, acknowledged, revised). Drives supplier production and logistics planning. Sourced from SAP MM schedule lines (EKET).'
}

Table supply.supply_ppap_submission {
  supply_ppap_submission_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key']
  supply_supplier_id varchar [note: 'FK to supplier']
  approval_date date [note: 'Approval date']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Created timestamp']
  disposition varchar [note: 'Disposition']
  part_number varchar [note: 'Part number']
  reason_for_submission varchar [note: 'Reason for submission']
  reviewer_name varchar [note: 'Reviewer name']
  submission_date date [note: 'Submission date']
  submission_level varchar [note: 'PPAP submission level']
  submission_status varchar [note: 'Submission status']

  Note: 'Production Part Approval Process submission record tracking the formal approval of a supplier\'s manufacturing process for a specific part. Captures PPAP level (1–5), submission date, part number, supplier, engineering change level, submission reason (new part, engineering change, tooling move), PPAP elements checklist status, PSW (Part Submission Warrant) status, and approval/rejection date. Integrates with PTC Windchill and SAP QM. SSOT for PPAP compliance.'
}

Table supply.ppap_element {
  ppap_element_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the PPAP element record.']
  commodity_group_id varchar [note: 'FK to the commodity group relevant to this PPAP element material.']
  procurement_supplier_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the supplier responsible for the part associated with this PPAP element.']
  supply_ppap_submission_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the parent PPAP submission that aggregates multiple elements.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the element was approved by the quality reviewer.']
  compliance_standard varchar [note: 'Regulatory or quality standard that the element satisfies (e.g., IATF 16949, AIAG PPAP).']
  created_by_user varchar [note: 'User identifier of the person who created the PPAP element record.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the PPAP element record was first created in the system.']
  defect_rate_ppm decimal(10,2) [note: 'Measured parts‑per‑million defect rate associated with the element, if applicable.']
  document_format varchar [note: 'File format of the stored PPAP element document.']
  document_reference varchar [note: 'Path or URL to the stored document that fulfills the PPAP element.']
  due_date date [note: 'Target date by which the element must be submitted for the PPAP package.']
  element_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code identifying the PPAP element type as defined by AIAG.']
  element_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the PPAP element (e.g., Design Records, PFMEA).']
  element_status varchar
  element_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the PPAP element according to AIAG PPAP element taxonomy.']
  element_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the element document (e.g., v1.0, revA).']
  file_size_bytes varchar [note: 'Size of the document file in bytes.']
  is_confidential boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the element contains confidential information requiring restricted handling.']
  last_reviewed_by varchar [note: 'User identifier of the most recent reviewer of the element.']
  part_number varchar [note: 'Manufacturer part number to which the PPAP element applies.']
  part_revision varchar [note: 'Revision identifier of the part version covered by the PPAP element.']
  quality_metric varchar [note: 'Outcome of the quality assessment for the element.']
  required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the element is mandatory for the current PPAP submission.']
  review_date date [note: 'Date on which the element was last reviewed.']
  reviewer_comments varchar [note: 'Free‑text comments entered by the reviewer during evaluation.']
  submission_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the element within the PPAP workflow.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time of the most recent modification to the PPAP element record.']

  Note: 'Individual PPAP element or deliverable within a PPAP submission, such as Design Records, PFMEA, Control Plan, MSA, SPC studies, or PSW. Captures element type (per AIAG PPAP standard), required flag, submission status (not started, submitted, approved, rejected), reviewer comments, and document reference. Enables granular tracking of PPAP completeness and compliance against IATF 16949 requirements.'
}

Table supply.inbound_shipment {
  inbound_shipment_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the inbound shipment record.']
  carrier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to logistics.carrier. Business justification: REQUIRED: Carrier performance & cost reporting uses inbound shipment data; linking enables carrier KPI aggregation.']
  compliance_document_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.compliance_document. Business justification: Shipment Documentation process records the customs/compliance document attached to each inbound shipment.']
  procurement_purchase_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.purchase_order. Business justification: REQUIRED: Shipment‑to‑PO matching process uses PO ID to verify that inbound shipments correspond to the correct purchase order.']
  receiving_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Receiving clerk records inbound shipment receipt; required for Receiving Activity Log.']
  receiving_plant_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the OEM plant receiving the shipment.']
  regulatory_requirement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_requirement. Business justification: Shipment Regulatory Check links inbound shipments to the regulatory_requirement governing hazardous material or import/export rules.']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.sku. Business justification: Shipment tracking per SKU is needed for logistics, ETA monitoring, and traceability of components to finished vehicles.']
  supply_supplier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.supply_supplier. Business justification: Associate inbound shipment with internal supplier master']
  actual_arrival_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Real date and time when the shipment was received at the dock.']
  asn_number varchar [note: 'Unique identifier supplied by the supplier to announce the shipment in advance.']
  carrier_scac varchar [note: 'Standard Carrier Alpha Code (four‑letter identifier) for the carrier.']
  container_count varchar [note: 'Number of containers included in the shipment.']
  created_timestamp timestamp
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for the freight cost.']
  customs_declaration_number varchar [note: 'Identifier assigned by customs for the import declaration.']
  delivery_window_end timestamp [note: 'End of the agreed delivery time window at the receiving dock.']
  delivery_window_start timestamp [note: 'Start of the agreed delivery time window at the receiving dock.']
  departure_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the shipment left the supplier\'s dock.']
  estimated_arrival_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Planned date and time of arrival at the receiving dock (ETA).']
  freight_cost decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total cost charged by the carrier for transporting the shipment.']
  hazardous_class varchar [note: 'Classification of hazardous material according to UN/ADR standards.']
  incoterm varchar [note: 'International commercial term defining responsibility and cost allocation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EXW|FCA|CPT|CIP|DAT|DAP|DDP — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  is_expedited boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the shipment is expedited (true) or standard (false).']
  is_hazardous boolean [note: 'True if the shipment contains hazardous materials.']
  last_status_update_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent change to the shipment status.']
  material_group varchar [note: 'Classification of the materials contained in the shipment.']
  mode_of_transport varchar [note: 'Primary mode used to move the shipment from supplier to plant.']
  pallet_count varchar [note: 'Number of pallets used to load the shipment.']
  record_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the shipment record was first created in the system.']
  record_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the shipment record.']
  remarks varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or notes entered by logistics personnel.']
  ship_date date
  shipment_number varchar
  shipment_status varchar [note: 'Current processing state of the inbound shipment.']
  temperature_control_required boolean [note: 'True if the shipment must be kept within a temperature range.']
  temperature_max_c decimal(5,2) [note: 'Upper bound of the required temperature range for the shipment.']
  temperature_min_c decimal(5,2) [note: 'Lower bound of the required temperature range for the shipment.']
  total_volume_m3 decimal(12,3) [note: 'Aggregate volume of the shipment, expressed in cubic meters.']
  total_weight_kg decimal(12,3) [note: 'Aggregate weight of all items in the shipment, expressed in kilograms.']

  Note: 'Tracks an inbound shipment of parts from a supplier plant to an OEM receiving dock. Captures ASN (Advance Shipping Notice) number, carrier, mode of transport (road, rail, air, sea), departure date/time, estimated arrival date/time, actual arrival date/time, total weight, total volume, number of containers/pallets, customs declaration number, and shipment status (in transit, arrived, cleared, received). Integrates with SAP MM inbound delivery (VL31N).'
}

Table supply.supply_goods_receipt {
  supply_goods_receipt_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key']
  supply_purchase_order_id varchar [note: 'FK to PO']
  supply_supplier_id varchar [note: 'FK to supplier']
  accepted_quantity decimal(12,4) [note: 'Accepted quantity']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Created timestamp']
  inspection_status varchar [note: 'Inspection status']
  receipt_date date [note: 'Receipt date']
  receipt_number varchar [note: 'Receipt number']
  received_quantity decimal(12,4) [note: 'Received quantity']
  rejected_quantity decimal(12,4) [note: 'Rejected quantity']
  storage_location varchar [note: 'Storage location']

  Note: 'Records the physical receipt and system confirmation of parts delivered by a supplier to an OEM plant. Captures GR document number, posting date, received quantity, accepted quantity, rejected quantity, storage location, batch number, GR type (standard, return, subsequent delivery), and posting status. Triggers inventory update and initiates 3-way invoice matching in SAP MM (MIGO). SSOT for inbound goods confirmation.'
}

Table supply.ckd_kit {
  ckd_kit_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the CKD/SKD kit record.']
  commodity_group_id varchar [note: 'FK to the primary commodity group for materials in this CKD kit.']
  compliance_document_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.compliance_document. Business justification: CKD Kit Certification process stores the compliance_document (type approval) linked to each kit.']
  configuration_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to vehicle.vehicle_configuration. Business justification: INCLUDE: CKD Kit Allocation also specifies the exact vehicle configuration (options, trim) the kit supports, requiring a FK to vehicle_configuration.']
  model_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to vehicle.model. Business justification: INCLUDE: CKD Kit Allocation process links each kit to the vehicle model it builds, so a FK model_id replaces the denormalized vehicle_model string.']
  supplier_contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.supplier_contract. Business justification: REQUIRED: Kit shipment compliance audit ties each CKD kit to the governing supplier contract that defines pricing and delivery terms.']
  supply_supplier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.supply_supplier. Business justification: Link CKD kit to internal supplier master']
  actual_arrival_date date [note: 'Actual date the kit was received at the target plant.']
  compliance_certifications varchar [note: 'List of certifications (e.g., CE, EPA) applicable to the kit.']
  created_timestamp timestamp
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for the kit value.']
  dispatch_date date [note: 'Date the kit was dispatched from the supplier or warehouse.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date on which the kit record ceases to be effective (nullable for open‑ended).']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date from which the kit record becomes effective for planning and execution.']
  expected_arrival_date date [note: 'Planned date of arrival at the target assembly plant.']
  export_customs_classification varchar [note: 'Harmonized System code used for customs clearance of the exported kit.']
  hazardous_material_description varchar [note: 'Description of hazardous substances contained in the kit, if any.']
  hazardous_material_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the kit contains hazardous materials requiring special handling.']
  incoterms varchar [note: 'International commercial terms governing shipment responsibilities. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EXW|FCA|CPT|CIP|DAT|DAP|DDP — promote to reference product]']
  inspection_result varchar [note: 'Outcome of the last inspection.']
  kit_content_description varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the parts and sub‑assemblies included in the kit.']
  kit_number varchar [note: 'Human‑readable identifier assigned to the kit for tracking and reference.']
  kit_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the kit within its lifecycle.']
  kit_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the kit is Completely Knocked Down (CKD) or Semi Knocked Down (SKD).']
  kit_value_usd decimal(15,2) [note: 'Monetary value of the kit in US dollars.']
  last_inspection_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent inspection performed on the kit.']
  model_year varchar [note: 'Calendar year of the vehicle model for which the kit is intended.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form field for additional remarks or observations.']
  packing_specification varchar [note: 'Details of the packing method, materials, and dimensions used for the kit.']
  ppap_document_reference varchar [note: 'Reference or identifier of the PPAP documentation associated with the kit.']
  ppap_status varchar [note: 'Status of the Production Part Approval Process for the kit.']
  quality_status varchar [note: 'Result of the final quality inspection for the kit.']
  record_audit_created timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the kit record was first created in the system.']
  record_audit_updated timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the kit record.']
  regulatory_approval_date date [note: 'Date on which the required regulatory approval was granted.']
  regulatory_approval_status varchar [note: 'Current status of regulatory approvals required for the kit.']
  regulatory_body varchar [note: 'Regulatory authority overseeing the kit\'s compliance.']
  resource_lifecycle_status varchar [note: 'Overall lifecycle state of the kit as a master resource.']
  seal_number varchar [note: 'Security seal number applied to the container.']
  shipping_container_number varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the shipping container carrying the kit.']
  shipping_method varchar [note: 'Mode of transport used to move the kit to the destination plant.']
  special_instructions varchar [note: 'Specific handling or assembly instructions associated with the kit.']
  target_plant_code varchar [note: 'Code of the assembly plant where the kit will be assembled.']
  target_plant_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the target assembly plant.']
  total_parts_count varchar [note: 'Number of individual parts contained in the kit.']
  total_volume_cbm decimal(12,3) [note: 'Aggregate volume of the kit in cubic meters.']
  total_weight_kg decimal(12,3) [note: 'Aggregate weight of the kit in kilograms.']
  tracking_number varchar [note: 'Carrier‑provided tracking identifier for the shipment.']
  warranty_period_months varchar [note: 'Duration of the warranty coverage for the assembled vehicle derived from this kit.']
  warranty_terms varchar [note: 'Textual description of warranty conditions and exclusions.']

  Note: 'Master record for a Completely Knocked Down (CKD) or Semi Knocked Down (SKD) kit assembled for export to a global assembly operation. Captures kit number, kit type (CKD/SKD), target assembly plant, model year, vehicle configuration, kit content list, packing specification, export customs classification, and kit lifecycle status. Supports global assembly operations where vehicles are shipped as kits for local assembly.'
}

Table supply.ckd_shipment {
  ckd_shipment_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the CKD/SKD kit shipment record.']
  ckd_kit_id varchar
  ckd_kit_shipment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to logistics.ckd_kit_shipment. Business justification: REQUIRED: CKD kit planning and execution need a single linked record; linking eliminates duplicate identifiers and aligns logistics execution.']
  commodity_group_id varchar [note: 'FK to the primary commodity group for this CKD shipment.']
  supply_supplier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.supply_supplier. Business justification: Link CKD shipment to internal supplier master']
  actual_arrival_date date [note: 'Real calendar date when the shipment was received at the destination plant.']
  bill_of_lading varchar [note: 'Document number that serves as a receipt for the shipped goods.']
  ckd_shipment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the shipment.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the shipment record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 code of the currency used for total_value.']
  customs_clearance_status varchar [note: 'Current status of customs clearance for the shipment.']
  departure_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the shipment departed the origin plant.']
  departure_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the shipment left the origin plant.']
  destination_plant_code varchar [note: 'Code of the assembly plant receiving the CKD/SKD kits.']
  estimated_arrival_date date [note: 'Planned calendar date for arrival at the destination plant.']
  export_declaration_number varchar [note: 'Customs export declaration identifier for the shipment.']
  freight_cost decimal(18,2) [note: 'Cost charged by the carrier for transporting the shipment.']
  freight_currency varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 currency code for freight_cost.']
  freight_terms varchar [note: 'Incoterm‑related freight payment terms.']
  hazardous_material_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the shipment contains hazardous materials.']
  incoterms varchar [note: 'International commercial terms governing the shipment responsibilities. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EXW|FCA|CPT|CIP|DAP|DDP|FAS|FOB|CFR|CIF|DAT|DDU — promote to reference product]']
  kit_count varchar [note: 'Number of CKD/SKD kits included in the shipment.']
  origin_plant_code varchar [note: 'Code of the OEM source plant where the kits are shipped from.']
  seal_number varchar [note: 'Security seal identifier applied to the container.']
  ship_date date
  shipment_number varchar
  shipment_status varchar
  temperature_control_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates if temperature control is required for the shipment.']
  total_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary value of the shipment before any adjustments.']
  tracking_number varchar [note: 'Unique identifier used to track the shipment in carrier systems.']
  transport_mode varchar [note: 'Mode of transportation used for the shipment.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the shipment record.']
  volume_m3 decimal(12,3) [note: 'Total cubic meter volume of the shipment.']
  weight_kg decimal(12,3) [note: 'Gross weight of the shipment in kilograms.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of a CKD/SKD kit shipment from the OEM source plant to a global assembly destination. Captures shipment number, origin plant, destination assembly plant, vessel/flight number, bill of lading, export declaration number, departure date, estimated arrival date, kit count, total value, currency, and customs clearance status. Enables end-to-end tracking of global CKD/SKD supply flows.'
}

Table supply.supplier_scorecard {
  supplier_scorecard_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for each supplier scorecard record.']
  supply_supplier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.supply_supplier. Business justification: Connect supplier scorecard to internal supplier master']
  commodity_price_competitiveness_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Score measuring supplier commodity price competitiveness vs market indices.']
  compliance_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Score measuring adherence to regulatory and internal compliance requirements.']
  corrective_action_description varchar [note: 'Free‑text description of actions the supplier must take to address deficiencies.']
  corrective_action_flag boolean [note: 'True when the supplier must undertake corrective actions.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the scorecard record was first created in the system.']
  delivery_quantity_accuracy_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Ratio of delivered quantity to ordered quantity, expressed as a percent.']
  evaluation_date timestamp [note: 'Exact timestamp when the scorecard was completed.']
  evaluation_period_end date [note: 'Last calendar day of the period covered by the scorecard.']
  evaluation_period_start date [note: 'First calendar day of the period covered by the scorecard.']
  evaluator_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the internal evaluator responsible for the scorecard.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional comments or observations captured during the evaluation.']
  otd_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of deliveries that arrived on or before the agreed delivery date.']
  overall_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Aggregated weighted score combining all KPI values for the evaluation period.']
  performance_tier varchar [note: 'Categorical tier assigned based on the overall score.']
  ppap_on_time_completion_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of PPAP submissions completed within the agreed timeframe.']
  ppm_defect_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Defect rate expressed in parts per million for supplied parts during the period.']
  quality_ppm decimal(10,2)
  responsiveness_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Score reflecting the supplier\'s responsiveness to inquiries and change requests.']
  review_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the scorecard is still being drafted, has been finalized, or has been reviewed by management.']
  risk_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Composite risk rating derived from financial, operational, and compliance factors.']
  scorecard_number varchar [note: 'Human‑readable identifier assigned to the scorecard, used for reporting and audit trails.']
  scorecard_period varchar
  scoring_methodology_version varchar [note: 'Identifier of the scoring model version used for this evaluation.']
  supplier_scorecard_status varchar [note: 'Current state of the scorecard in its lifecycle.']
  sustainability_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Score reflecting the supplier\'s environmental and sustainability initiatives.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time of the most recent modification to the scorecard.']

  Note: 'Periodic (monthly/quarterly) performance evaluation record for a supplier across key KPIs including PPM (Parts Per Million defect rate), OTD (On-Time Delivery percentage), delivery quantity accuracy, PPAP on-time completion rate, responsiveness score, and overall supplier rating. Captures evaluation period, scoring methodology version, individual KPI values, weighted total score, performance tier (preferred, approved, conditional, disqualified), and corrective action flag.'
}

Table supply.supplier_deviation {
  supplier_deviation_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for each supplier deviation record.']
  obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.compliance_obligation. Business justification: Deviation Management report ties a supplier deviation to the specific compliance_obligation it breaches.']
  procurement_purchase_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.purchase_order. Business justification: REQUIRED: Deviation tracking report links a supplier deviation directly to the purchase order that triggered the deviation for root‑cause analysis.']
  sku_master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.sku_master. Business justification: Supplier deviations are part‑specific; associating with SKU allows impact analysis on stocked inventory quality.']
  supply_supplier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.supply_supplier. Business justification: Link supplier deviation to internal supplier master']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the deviation was approved or rejected by engineering.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the engineering or quality manager who approved the deviation.']
  authorized_cost decimal(12,2) [note: 'Monetary value of the authorized quantity for the deviation.']
  comments varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes entered by users regarding the deviation.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the deviation record was first created in the data lake.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 currency code for the authorized cost.']
  deviation_number varchar [note: 'External reference number assigned to the deviation request, used for tracking and communication with suppliers.']
  deviation_status varchar
  deviation_type varchar [note: 'Category of the deviation (e.g., dimensional, material, process, documentation).']
  disposition varchar [note: 'Final handling instruction for the deviated parts.']
  effective_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the deviation became effective for production.']
  engineering_approval_status varchar [note: 'Current status of engineering review for the deviation.']
  expiration_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the deviation expires and must no longer be used.']
  is_temporary_flag boolean [note: 'True if the deviation is a short‑term waiver; false if permanent.']
  part_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name or description of the part.']
  part_number varchar [note: 'Identifier of the part to which the deviation applies (OEM or supplier part number).']
  portal_notification_sent_flag boolean [note: 'Whether a notification was sent to the supplier via the collaboration portal.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Business priority assigned to the deviation for handling urgency.']
  quality_hold_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the part is placed on quality hold pending deviation resolution.']
  quantity_authorized decimal(12,2) [note: 'Number of units or amount of material approved for the deviation.']
  related_ecn_number varchar [note: 'Engineering Change Notice (ECN) identifier associated with the deviation, if any.']
  related_ppap_status varchar [note: 'Status of the Production Part Approval Process linked to this deviation.']
  request_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact date‑time when the supplier submitted the deviation request.']
  requested_date date
  risk_rating varchar [note: 'Risk assessment of the deviation impact on quality and delivery.']
  supplier_deviation_description varchar [note: 'Detailed explanation of the reason for the deviation and any special instructions.']
  supplier_deviation_status varchar [note: 'Overall lifecycle state of the deviation record.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Measurement unit for the authorized quantity.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the deviation record.']
  validity_end_date date [note: 'Date after which the deviation is no longer valid.']
  validity_start_date date [note: 'Date from which the deviation is permitted to be used.']

  Note: 'Formal record of a supplier request for deviation or waiver to ship non-conforming parts or use an alternative process temporarily. Captures deviation number, part number, deviation type (dimensional, material, process), quantity authorized, validity period, engineering approval status, quality hold flag, and disposition (use-as-is, rework, scrap). Supports IATF 16949 non-conformance management and traceability.'
}

  supply_corrective_action_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key']
