  due_date date [note: 'Due date']
  issue_description varchar [note: 'Issue description']
  root_cause varchar [note: 'Root cause']
  severity varchar [note: 'Severity']
  supply_corrective_action_status varchar [note: 'Status']

  Note: 'Supplier corrective action request (SCAR) issued when a supplier fails to meet quality, delivery, or compliance requirements. Captures SCAR number, triggering event (PPM breach, OTD failure, audit finding), root cause category, 8D report reference, containment action, permanent corrective action, verification date, and closure status. Drives supplier development and continuous improvement programs.'
}

Table supply.supplier_audit {
  supplier_audit_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for each supplier audit.']
  auditor_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Internal audits are performed by specific auditors; audit reports reference auditor employee.']
  supply_supplier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.supply_supplier. Business justification: Associate supplier audit with internal supplier master']
  audit_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the audit took place.']
  audit_location varchar [note: 'Site, plant, or facility name where the audit took place.']
  audit_method varchar [note: 'Delivery method of the audit (on‑site, remote, or virtual).']
  audit_number varchar [note: 'External audit reference code assigned by the audit team.']
  audit_report_document varchar [note: 'Location of the detailed audit report (file path, URL, or document ID).']
  audit_result varchar
  audit_standard varchar [note: 'Quality or environmental standard against which the audit is assessed.']
  audit_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the audit (system, process, product, or layered process).']
  auditor_email varchar [note: 'Primary email contact for the auditor.']
  auditor_name varchar [note: 'Full legal name of the auditor conducting the audit.']
  auditor_phone varchar [note: 'Contact telephone number for the auditor.']
  closure_date date [note: 'Date on which the audit record was marked closed.']
  closure_status varchar [note: 'State of audit closure (open, closed, or pending).']
  comments varchar [note: 'Additional observations, recommendations, or notes recorded by the auditor.']
  created_timestamp timestamp
  findings_major_count varchar [note: 'Number of major non‑conformities discovered during the audit.']
  findings_minor_count varchar [note: 'Number of minor non‑conformities discovered during the audit.']
  findings_observation_count varchar [note: 'Number of observation‑type findings (no immediate non‑conformance).']
  lifecycle_status varchar [note: 'Overall lifecycle stage of the audit (planned, in progress, completed, closed, or cancelled).']
  overall_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Aggregated audit score expressed as a percentage.']
  portal_scheduled_flag boolean [note: 'Whether the audit was scheduled via the supplier collaboration portal.']
  re_audit_required boolean [note: 'True if a subsequent audit must be scheduled to verify corrective actions.']
  record_audit_created timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the audit record was initially captured.']
  record_audit_updated timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the latest modification to the audit record.']
  risk_rating varchar [note: 'Overall risk classification derived from audit findings.']
  scope_description varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the audit scope and boundaries.']

  Note: 'Record of a formal quality or process audit conducted at a supplier facility. Captures audit type (system audit, process audit, product audit, layered process audit), audit date, auditor name, scope, findings count by severity (major, minor, observation), overall audit score, re-audit required flag, and closure status. Supports IATF 16949 supplier monitoring and development obligations.'
}

Table supply.inbound_inspection {
  inbound_inspection_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for each inbound inspection record.']
  commodity_group_id varchar [note: 'FK to the commodity group of the inspected material.']
  inbound_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employee who performed the inspection.']
  inspector_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employee who performed the inspection.']
  procurement_supplier_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the supplier that provided the inspected part.']
  sku_master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.sku_master. Business justification: Inspection results are recorded per SKU to update quality status and determine stock availability.']
  supply_goods_receipt_id varchar
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the inspection record was first created in the system.']
  defect_count varchar [note: 'Total number of defects identified in the inspected sample.']
  defect_rate_ppm decimal(12,2) [note: 'Defects per million units calculated for the inspected lot.']
  defect_type_codes varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of defect classification codes (e.g., surface, dimensional, functional).']
  disposition varchar [note: 'Action taken after inspection (accept, return to supplier, sort and use, or rework).']
  inspection_date date
  inspection_location varchar [note: 'Plant or line code where the inspection was carried out.']
  inspection_lot_number varchar [note: 'External identifier assigned by the plant for the inspection lot.']
  inspection_method varchar [note: 'Method used for the inspection (AQL sampling, 100% check, or skip‑lot).']
  inspection_number varchar
  inspection_result varchar [note: 'Overall outcome of the inspection based on defect criteria.']
  inspection_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the inspection record.']
  inspection_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the inspection was performed at the plant.']
  part_number varchar [note: 'OEM-assigned part number of the component being inspected.']
  part_revision varchar [note: 'Engineering change revision identifier for the part.']
  sample_size varchar [note: 'Number of units selected for inspection according to the sampling plan.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the inspection record.']

  Note: 'Incoming quality inspection record for parts received from a supplier at an OEM plant. Captures inspection lot number, inspection date, part number, supplier, sample size, inspection method (AQL sampling, 100% check, skip-lot), number of defects found, defect type codes, inspection result (pass, fail, conditional release), and disposition (accept, return to supplier, sort and use). Integrates with SAP QM inspection lots.'
}

Table supply.disruption {
  disruption_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key']
  supply_supplier_id varchar [note: 'FK to supplier']
  affected_parts varchar [note: 'Affected parts']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Created timestamp']
  disruption_description varchar [note: 'Description']
  disruption_status varchar [note: 'Status']
  disruption_type varchar [note: 'Disruption type']
  end_date date [note: 'End date']
  impact_description varchar [note: 'Impact description']
  mitigation_plan varchar [note: 'Mitigation plan']
  severity varchar [note: 'Severity']
  start_date date [note: 'Start date']

  Note: 'Records a supply disruption event where a supplier is unable to meet scheduled delivery commitments due to capacity issues, natural disasters, labor actions, logistics failures, or quality holds. Captures disruption type, affected parts, impacted plants, start date, expected resolution date, severity (critical, major, minor), mitigation actions taken (alternative sourcing, expedite, safety stock draw), and financial impact estimate. Supports supply risk management and business continuity planning.'
}

Table supply.tooling_asset {
  tooling_asset_id varchar [pk, note: 'Surrogate primary key uniquely identifying each tooling asset record.']
  compliance_document_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.compliance_document. Business justification: Tooling Certification process records the compliance_document (e.g., safety standard) for each tooling_asset.']
  fixed_asset_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.fixed_asset. Business justification: Tooling is capitalized as a Fixed Asset for depreciation and asset management in finance.']
  supply_supplier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.supply_supplier. Business justification: Link tooling asset to internal supplier master']
  acquisition_cost decimal(15,2) [note: 'Capital cost incurred to acquire the tooling asset.']
  acquisition_currency varchar [note: 'Currency in which the acquisition cost is recorded.']
  acquisition_date date [note: 'Date the tooling asset was purchased or received.']
  commissioning_date date [note: 'Date the tooling asset entered production service.']
  compliance_standard varchar [note: 'Regulatory or quality standard applicable to the tooling.']
  condition varchar [note: 'Current physical condition of the tooling asset.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO country code of the tooling location.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the tooling asset record was first created in the system.']
  depreciation_method varchar [note: 'Accounting method used to depreciate the tooling asset.']
  inspection_status varchar [note: 'Result of the latest inspection.']
  last_inspection_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent condition inspection.']
  maintenance_cost_total decimal(15,2) [note: 'Cumulative cost incurred for maintaining the tooling asset.']
  maintenance_last_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent maintenance activity.']
  maintenance_next_date date [note: 'Planned date for the next maintenance event.']
  maintenance_type varchar [note: 'Category of maintenance performed on the tooling.']
  next_inspection_due date [note: 'Planned date for the next scheduled inspection.']
  ownership_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the tooling is owned by the OEM, the supplier, jointly owned, or leased.']
  part_number varchar [note: 'OEM part number that this tooling is used to manufacture.']
  plant_code varchar [note: 'Code of the manufacturing plant where the tooling is deployed.']
  portal_visibility_flag boolean [note: 'Whether the tooling asset is visible to the supplier on the collaboration portal.']
  regulatory_approval_date date [note: 'Date when regulatory approval was granted.']
  regulatory_approval_required boolean [note: 'Indicates if the tooling must obtain regulatory approval before use.']
  regulatory_approval_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the required regulatory approval.']
  status_change_date date [note: 'Date when the current status became effective.']
  supplier_location varchar [note: 'Physical site or plant where the tooling asset is located at the supplier.']
  tool_description varchar [note: 'Free‑text description of the tooling asset, including dimensions and capabilities.']
  tool_life_remaining varchar [note: 'Remaining shots or cycles before the tool reaches end‑of‑life.']
  tool_life_total varchar [note: 'Maximum allowable shots or cycles before tool retirement.']
  tool_life_type varchar [note: 'Metric used to track tool life – either number of shots or operational cycles.']
  tool_life_used varchar [note: 'Cumulative shots or cycles the tool has already performed.']
  tool_number varchar [note: 'Manufacturer-assigned identifier for the tooling item (die, mold, fixture, gauge).']
  tool_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the tooling asset by its functional form.']
  tooling_asset_status varchar [note: 'Operational status of the tooling asset.']
  tooling_number varchar
  tooling_status varchar
  tooling_type varchar
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the tooling asset record.']
  warranty_end_date date [note: 'Date when the tooling warranty expires.']
  warranty_provider varchar [note: 'Entity that provides the warranty (OEM, supplier, third‑party).']
  warranty_start_date date [note: 'Date when the tooling warranty becomes effective.']

  Note: 'Master record for OEM-owned production tooling (dies, molds, jigs, fixtures, checking gauges) physically located at supplier facilities. Captures tool number, tool type, part number produced, supplier plant location, ownership classification (OEM-owned per IATF 16949 §8.5.3), acquisition cost, net book value, tool life capacity (shots/cycles), current utilization, last maintenance/inspection date, and lifecycle status (active, under repair, awaiting transfer, end-of-life). Supports tooling investment tracking, PPAP tooling records, and supplier exit/transition planning.'
}

Table supply.price_agreement {
  price_agreement_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the price agreement record.']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Price agreements tie to a GL account to record purchase price variances and contract liabilities.']
  hedging_contract_id varchar [note: 'FK to the associated hedging contract if commodity-linked.']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.sku. Business justification: Price agreements are negotiated per SKU, supporting cost management and contract compliance.']
  sku_master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.sku_master. Business justification: Price agreements are negotiated per part/SKU; linking provides pricing data for inventory valuation and costing.']
  supply_supplier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.supply_supplier. Business justification: Associate price agreement with internal supplier master']
  actual_otd_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Measured on‑time delivery performance of the supplier for the current period.']
  actual_ppm decimal(5,2) [note: 'Measured defect rate of supplied parts for the current period.']
  agreement_number varchar [note: 'External business identifier assigned to the price agreement by the OEM.']
  agreement_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the agreement indicating its primary purpose (e.g., price, cost, service, tooling).']
  annual_price_reduction_commitment decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage reduction in price committed by the supplier each year.']
  commodity_index_linked_flag boolean [note: 'Whether the price agreement is linked to a commodity price index.']
  commodity_index_name varchar [note: 'Name of the commodity index used for price escalation (e.g., LME Aluminum).']
  compliance_approval_status varchar [note: 'Status of internal compliance review for the agreement.']
  compliance_document_ref varchar [note: 'Reference identifier for the compliance documentation attached to the agreement.']
  contract_scope varchar [note: 'Narrative defining the parts, volumes, and geographic regions covered by the agreement.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the price agreement record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 code of the currency used for the unit price.']
  early_termination_allowed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the agreement may be terminated before the end date without penalty.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date on which the price agreement expires or is terminated; null if open‑ended.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date on which the price agreement becomes binding.']
  escalation_formula varchar [note: 'Formula for commodity-based price escalation/de-escalation.']
  part_number varchar [note: 'OEM part number to which the price agreement applies.']
  payment_terms varchar [note: 'Standard payment condition agreed for invoices under this price agreement.']
  penalty_clause varchar [note: 'Text describing penalties for non‑compliance such as late delivery or quality breaches.']
  price_adjustment_trigger varchar [note: 'Condition that can trigger a price change during the agreement (material index, foreign exchange, or none).']
  price_agreement_description varchar [note: 'Free‑form text describing the purpose, scope, and any special conditions of the agreement.']
  price_agreement_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the price agreement.']
  renewal_notice_period_days varchar [note: 'Number of days notice required to exercise a renewal option.']
  renewal_option varchar [note: 'Specifies if the agreement auto‑renews, requires manual renewal, or does not renew.']
  target_otd_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Contractual target for supplier on‑time delivery performance.']
  target_ppm decimal(5,2) [note: 'Maximum acceptable defect rate for supplied parts under the agreement.']
  termination_notice_period_days varchar [note: 'Number of days notice required to terminate the agreement early.']
  tooling_amortization_terms varchar [note: 'Terms describing how tooling costs are amortized over the agreement period.']
  total_annual_volume decimal(18,2) [note: 'Maximum total quantity of the part that may be purchased under the agreement each year.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Measurement unit used for the agreed price and volume (e.g., piece, kilogram).']
  unit_price decimal(18,6) [note: 'Negotiated price per unit of the part for the agreement period.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time of the most recent modification to the price agreement record.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Sequential version number of the agreement reflecting amendments.']

  Note: 'Formally agreed piece price and commercial terms between the OEM and a supplier for a specific part over a defined period. Captures agreement number, part number, supplier, effective date, expiry date, agreed unit price, currency, annual price reduction commitment (APR), tooling amortization terms, price adjustment triggers (material index, FX), and agreement status (active, expired, under negotiation). Distinct from the purchase order — this is the commercial price framework.'
}

Table supply.inbound_event {
  inbound_event_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key']
  supply_supplier_id varchar [note: 'FK to supplier']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Created timestamp']
  event_description varchar [note: 'Event description']
  event_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Event timestamp']
  event_type varchar [note: 'Event type']
  part_number varchar [note: 'Part number']
  resolution_status varchar [note: 'Resolution status']
  severity varchar [note: 'Severity']

  Note: 'Milestone and exception event log for inbound supply chain activities, capturing key status transitions such as ASN received, customs cleared, dock arrival, goods receipt posted, quality hold placed, or delivery rejected. Captures event type, event timestamp, location, part number, shipment reference, responsible party, and event notes. Enables end-to-end supply chain visibility and exception management across the inbound supply network.'
}

Table supply.commodity_group {
  commodity_group_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the commodity group. _canonical_skip_reason: Entity is a reference lookup used for classification of purchased parts.']
  parent_commodity_group_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the parent commodity group for hierarchical classification.']
  supply_supplier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.supply_supplier. Business justification: Associate commodity group with responsible supplier for governance']
  applicable_regions varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of ISO 3166‑1 alpha‑3 country codes where the commodity group is used.']
  average_cost_usd decimal(12,2) [note: 'Average unit cost in US dollars for items within the commodity group.']
  commodity_category varchar [note: 'High‑level category grouping similar commodity groups.']
  commodity_code varchar [note: 'Unique alphanumeric code identifying the commodity group as defined in SAP MM.']
  commodity_group_category varchar
  commodity_group_code varchar [note: 'Business code used to identify the commodity group in source systems.']
  commodity_group_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the commodity family, its typical applications and characteristics.']
  commodity_group_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the commodity family (e.g., Electronics, Castings).']
  commodity_group_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the commodity group.']
  commodity_group_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the commodity group by its role in the supply chain.']
  commodity_manager varchar [note: 'Name of the internal manager responsible for the commodity group.']
  commodity_name varchar
  compliance_standards varchar [note: 'List of regulatory standards applicable to the commodity group. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: REACH|RoHS|ISO9001|IATF16949|EPA|CARB — promote to reference product]']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the commodity group record was first created in the source system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Default currency used for cost references in this commodity group.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when the commodity group is retired or no longer valid.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date from which the commodity group definition is valid.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when the commodity group becomes effective for use.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date after which the commodity group definition is no longer valid (null if indefinite).']
  hazardous_material_description varchar [note: 'Description of the hazardous characteristics or classifications for the commodity group.']
  hazardous_material_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the commodity group contains hazardous materials.']
  hedge_coverage_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of annual consumption currently hedged.']
  hedging_strategy varchar [note: 'Commodity hedging strategy: none, partial, full, collar.']
  is_confidential boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the commodity group contains confidential pricing or strategic information.']
  is_global boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the commodity group is applicable across all plants and regions.']
  lead_time_max_days varchar [note: 'Longest typical supplier lead time for items in this commodity group.']
  lead_time_min_days varchar [note: 'Shortest typical supplier lead time for items in this commodity group.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or additional information about the commodity group.']
  price_volatility_index decimal(8,4) [note: 'Historical price volatility index for this commodity group.']
  regulatory_compliance_flag boolean [note: 'True if the commodity group is subject to regulatory compliance requirements (e.g., REACH, RoHS).']
  risk_factor varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of primary supply‑chain risks (e.g., single source, geopolitical, regulatory).']
  risk_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numerical risk rating (0‑100) for the commodity group based on supplier performance and material hazards.']
  strategic_classification varchar [note: 'Kraljic matrix classification indicating the strategic importance of the commodity.']
  sustainability_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Score reflecting environmental impact and sustainability of the commodity group.']
  typical_price_range_high decimal(12,2) [note: 'Upper bound of typical unit price for items in this commodity group.']
  typical_price_range_low decimal(12,2) [note: 'Lower bound of typical unit price for items in this commodity group.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Standard unit of measure used for the commodity group.']
  updated_by varchar [note: 'User or system that performed the latest update to the commodity group record.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time of the most recent update to the commodity group record.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Version identifier for changes to the commodity group definition.']
  volume_per_unit_cbm decimal(10,3) [note: 'Typical volume of a single unit within the commodity group, expressed in cubic meters.']
  weight_per_unit_kg decimal(10,3) [note: 'Typical weight of a single unit within the commodity group, expressed in kilograms.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User or system that initially created the commodity group record.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for commodity_group. '
}

Table supply.supply_agreement {
  supply_agreement_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key']
  supply_supplier_id varchar [note: 'FK to supplier']
  agreement_number varchar [note: 'Agreement number']
  agreement_type varchar [note: 'Agreement type']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Created timestamp']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Currency code']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Effective end']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Effective start']
  payment_terms varchar [note: 'Payment terms']
  scope_description varchar [note: 'Scope description']
  supply_agreement_status varchar [note: 'Status']
  total_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total value']

  Note: 'Represents a contractual relationship between a supply_supplier and a vehicle SKU. Each record captures the terms under which a supplier provides parts for a specific SKU, including lead time, cost, pricing unit, lifecycle status, and the effective date range.. Existence Justification: A tier‑1 or tier‑2 supplier can provide parts for many vehicle SKUs, and a given SKU can be sourced from multiple suppliers. The business actively manages each supplier‑SKU pairing as a supply agreement, tracking lead times, costs, pricing units, lifecycle status, and effective dates.'
}

Table supply.supplier_nomination {
  supplier_nomination_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key']
  supply_supplier_id varchar [note: 'FK to supplier']
  annual_volume varchar [note: 'Annual volume']
  approval_date date [note: 'Approval date']
  commodity_code varchar [note: 'Commodity code']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Created timestamp']
  nomination_date date [note: 'Nomination date']
  nomination_number varchar [note: 'Nomination number']
  nomination_status varchar [note: 'Nomination status']
  part_number varchar [note: 'Part number']
  program_code varchar [note: 'Program code']
  target_price decimal(18,4) [note: 'Target price']

  Note: 'This association captures the contractual nomination of a supply_supplier to a model_year_program, including the volume, price, currency, status, and risk rating agreed for that program.. Existence Justification: A supplier can be nominated for multiple model year programs, providing volume and price terms for each. Conversely, each model year program sources parts from many suppliers. The nomination itself is managed as a contract with its own attributes (volume, price, status, risk).'
}

Table supply.supplier_compliance_assignment {
  supplier_compliance_assignment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key']
  regulatory_requirement_id varchar [note: 'FK to regulatory requirement']
  supply_supplier_id varchar [note: 'FK to supplier']
  audit_date date [note: 'Last audit date']
  certification_number varchar [note: 'Certification number']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Compliance status']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Created timestamp']
  effective_date date [note: 'Effective date']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Expiry date']
  notes varchar [note: 'Notes']

  Note: 'Represents the assignment of a compliance obligation to a supplier, capturing the status, effective period, and priority of the responsibility. Each record links one supplier to one compliance obligation and stores attributes that exist only in the context of this relationship.. Existence Justification: A supplier may be responsible for multiple regulatory compliance obligations, and a single compliance obligation often requires participation from multiple suppliers. The business actively tracks each supplier‑obligation link with status, effective dates, and priority, making the relationship a managed entity rather than a simple lookup.'
}

Table procurement.procurement_supplier {
  procurement_supplier_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the supplier master record.']

  address_line1 varchar [note: 'First line of the supplier\'s primary business address.']
  bank_account_number varchar [note: 'Supplier\'s bank account number for payments.']
  bank_name varchar [note: 'Name of the financial institution holding the supplier\'s account.']
  certification_status varchar [note: 'Current overall status of the supplier\'s certifications.']
  city varchar [note: 'City component of the supplier\'s primary address.']
  commodity_specialization varchar [note: 'Specific commodity or material the supplier specializes in providing.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO country code of the supplier\'s primary location.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the supplier record was first created.']
  credit_limit decimal(15,2) [note: 'Maximum credit amount approved for the supplier.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Default currency used for transactions with the supplier.']
  data_governance_ref varchar [note: 'Reference to data governance catalog entry for lineage and ownership tracking']
  deactivation_date date [note: 'Date when the supplier record was marked inactive or blocked.']
  duns_number varchar [note: 'Dun & Bradstreet unique identifier for the supplier organization.']
  iatf16949_cert_expiry date [note: 'Expiration date of the supplier\'s IATF 16949 certification.']

  incoterms varchar [note: 'International commercial terms defining delivery responsibilities.']
  iso14001_cert_expiry date [note: 'Expiration date of the ISO 14001 certification.']
  iso14001_certified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the supplier holds an ISO 14001 environmental certification.']
  iso9001_cert_expiry date [note: 'Expiration date of the ISO 9001 certification.']
  iso9001_certified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the supplier is ISO 9001 certified.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time of the most recent update to the supplier record.']
  lead_time_days varchar [note: 'Typical number of days from order placement to delivery.']
  legal_name varchar [note: 'Full legal registered name of the supplier entity.']
  max_order_quantity varchar [note: 'Largest quantity the supplier can deliver in a single order.']
  min_order_quantity varchar [note: 'Smallest quantity the supplier accepts per purchase order.']
  onboarding_date date [note: 'Date when the supplier was first approved for procurement.']
  payment_terms varchar [note: 'Standard payment condition agreed with the supplier.']
  postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal or ZIP code of the supplier\'s primary address.']
  preferred_language varchar [note: 'Language used for communications with the supplier.']
  primary_contact_email varchar [note: 'Email address of the primary procurement contact.']
  primary_contact_name varchar [note: 'Name of the main contact person for procurement communications.']
  primary_contact_phone varchar [note: 'Telephone number of the primary procurement contact.']
  procurement_supplier_name varchar [note: 'Primary display name of the supplier used in procurement processes.']
  procurement_supplier_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the supplier record.']
  rating_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Overall performance rating assigned by the procurement team.']
  risk_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Risk assessment score based on financial, compliance, and operational factors.']
  state_province varchar [note: 'State or province of the supplier\'s primary address.']
  supplier_category varchar [note: 'Broad business category describing the goods or services supplied.']
  supplier_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the supplier based on its position in the supply chain.']
  sustainability_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Score reflecting the supplier\'s environmental and social sustainability performance.']
  swift_code varchar [note: 'International bank identifier for cross‑border payments.']
  tax_identification_number varchar [note: 'Government‑issued tax identifier for the supplier.']
  vat_number varchar [note: 'Value‑Added Tax registration number for the supplier.']
  website_url varchar [note: 'Public website address of the supplier.']

  Note: 'Master record for all suppliers and vendors providing direct materials (production parts, raw materials) and indirect materials (MRO, tooling, services) to Automotive. Captures supplier identity, classification (tier-1, tier-2, tier-3), business registration details, DUNS number, tax identifiers, payment terms, currency, incoterms, preferred language, supplier status (active, blocked, under-development), IATF 16949 certification status, ISO 9001/14001 certification flags, geographic footprint, commodity specialization, and strategic sourcing category. SSOT for supplier identity within the procurement domain; integrates with SAP MM vendor master.'
}

Table procurement.procurement_supplier_plant {
  procurement_supplier_plant_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for the supplier-plant relationship record.']
  packaging_specification_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the packaging specification that the supplier must follow for deliveries to this plant.']
  plant_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the manufacturing plant approved for the supplier.']
  procurement_supplier_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the supplier (vendor) linked to the plant.']
  address_line1 varchar [note: 'First line of the supplier\'s delivery address for the plant.']
  address_line2 varchar [note: 'Second line of the supplier\'s delivery address for the plant, if applicable.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the supplier received approval for the plant.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Lifecycle status of the supplier\'s approval for this plant.']
  city varchar [note: 'City component of the supplier\'s delivery address for the plant.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO country code of the delivery location.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the supplier‑plant record was first created.']
  data_governance_ref varchar [note: 'Reference to data governance catalog entry for lineage and ownership tracking']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Termination date of the supplier\'s authorization for the plant; null if open‑ended.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'First date the supplier is authorized to supply the plant.']
  freight_terms varchar [note: 'Incoterm that defines cost and risk responsibilities for the shipment.']
  is_preferred_supplier boolean [note: 'True if the supplier is designated as a preferred source for the plant.']
  jis_delivery_flag boolean [note: 'True if the supplier delivers parts in the production sequence (JIS) for this plant.']
  jit_delivery_flag boolean [note: 'True if the supplier delivers on a Just‑In‑Time basis for this plant.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the most recent change to this record.']
  lead_time_days varchar [note: 'Typical number of days from purchase order issuance to receipt at the plant.']
  minimum_order_quantity varchar [note: 'Smallest quantity the plant will accept for a single delivery from this supplier.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional comments or special instructions related to the supplier‑plant relationship.']
  postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal/ZIP code of the supplier\'s delivery address.']
  procurement_supplier_plant_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the supplier‑plant association.']
  quality_rating varchar [note: 'Internal quality score assigned to the supplier for deliveries to this plant.']
  shipping_method varchar [note: 'Standard mode of transportation used for deliveries to the plant.']
  state_province varchar [note: 'State or province component of the supplier\'s delivery address.']
  supplier_contact_email varchar [note: 'Email address of the supplier\'s plant liaison.']
  supplier_contact_name varchar [note: 'Name of the supplier representative responsible for deliveries to the plant.']
  supplier_contact_phone varchar [note: 'Telephone number for the supplier\'s plant liaison.']
  unloading_point varchar [note: 'Designated dock or bay where the supplier\'s goods are received at the plant.']

  Note: 'Association between a supplier and the Automotive manufacturing plants it is approved to supply. Captures plant-specific supplier data including delivery address, unloading point, JIT/JIS delivery flag, lead time, minimum order quantity, packaging specification reference, and plant-level approval status. Enables plant-level sourcing decisions and inbound logistics planning. Reflects SAP MM vendor-plant extension (LFM1/LFM2).'
}

Table procurement.sourcing_event {
  sourcing_event_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the sourcing event.']
  eprocurement_portal_id varchar [note: 'FK to the e-procurement portal through which this sourcing event was conducted.']
  primary_sourcing_procurement_supplier_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the primary supplier nominated in the event.']
  sourcing_buyer_employee_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the internal buyer responsible for the event.']
  sourcing_employee_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the internal buyer responsible for the event.']
  spend_category_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.spend_category. Business justification: Sourcing events are organized by commodity/spend category. The existing commodity_group (STRING) is a denormalized text field. Linking sourcing_event to spend_category enables structured sourcing pipe']
  supplier_contract_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the contract generated from the awarded event.']
  vehicle_program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to engineering.vehicle_program. Business justification: Sourcing events are launched per vehicle program to source components; required for program‑level sourcing reports.']
  award_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Final contract value awarded to the selected supplier.']
  award_currency_code varchar [note: 'Currency of the award amount.']
  award_decision_date date [note: 'Date on which the supplier award was formally decided.']
  award_supplier_name varchar [note: 'Legal name of the awarded supplier.']
  buyer_department varchar [note: 'Organizational department to which the buyer belongs.']
  buyer_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the internal buyer or procurement team.']
  commodity_group varchar [note: 'High-level classification of the goods or services being sourced (e.g., Powertrain, Electronics, MRO, Raw_Materials, Software, Services) [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: Powertrain|Electronics|MRO|Raw_Materials|Software|Services]']
  confidentiality_level varchar [note: 'Data classification level for the event record.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the sourcing event record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO currency code for monetary amounts.']
  data_governance_ref varchar [note: 'Reference to data governance catalog entry for lineage and ownership tracking']
  evaluation_method varchar [note: 'Method used to rank and select suppliers.']
  evaluation_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numeric score resulting from the supplier evaluation process.']
  event_number varchar [note: 'External reference number assigned to the sourcing event, used in communications and reporting.']
  event_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the sourcing event was officially launched.']
  event_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the event is a Request for Quotation, Request for Information, or Request for Proposal.']
  is_multi_source boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the event is intended to select multiple suppliers.']
  is_nda_required boolean [note: 'True if a non-disclosure agreement is required from participants.']
  sor_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier for the SOR document linked to the event.']
  sor_version varchar [note: 'Version number of the SOR document used for this event.']
  sourcing_event_description varchar [note: 'Free-text description providing context and objectives of the sourcing event.']
  sourcing_event_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the sourcing event.']
  sourcing_strategy varchar [note: 'Chosen approach for supplier selection.']
  submission_deadline date [note: 'Last date suppliers may submit their proposals.']
  target_spend_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Estimated total spend the organization aims to achieve through this event.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the sourcing event record.']

  Note: 'Represents a formal strategic sourcing event initiated by Automotive procurement teams, including RFQ (Request for Quotation), RFI (Request for Information), and RFP (Request for Proposal) events. Captures event type, commodity group, target spend, SOR (Statement of Requirements) reference, sourcing strategy (single-source, dual-source, competitive bid), event status, award decision date, nominated suppliers, and responsible buyer. Supports global sourcing strategy execution and Ariba Sourcing integration.'
}

Table procurement.supplier_quote {
  supplier_quote_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for each supplier quote.']
  compliance_document_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.compliance_document. Business justification: REQUIRED: Quotes for regulated components include a link to the supplier\'s compliance certificate, needed for part approval and downstream validation.']
  created_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Supplier Quote Creation – created_by_employee_id identifies the employee who entered the quote, needed for quote provenance and compliance with pricing approval processes.']
  eprocurement_portal_id varchar [note: 'FK to the e-procurement portal through which this quote was submitted.']
  procurement_supplier_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the supplier who submitted the quote.']
  sku_master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.sku_master. Business justification: REQUIRED: Quote evaluation compares quoted price against SKU master price list; linking quote to SKU enables price variance analysis.']
  sourcing_event_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the original sourcing request that this quote answers.']
  vehicle_program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to engineering.vehicle_program. Business justification: Supplier quotes are evaluated against a specific vehicle program\'s requirements; needed for quote comparison dashboards.']
  amortization_rate_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage rate applied to amortize tooling cost over the term.']
  amortization_term_months varchar [note: 'Number of months over which tooling cost is amortized.']
  comments varchar [note: 'Additional remarks or conditions supplied by the vendor.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the quote record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter currency identifier for the quoted amounts.']
  discount_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Monetary discount the supplier is offering on the base price.']
  e_sourcing_platform varchar [note: 'E-procurement platform used (Ariba, SupplyOn, Covisint)']
  is_preferred boolean [note: 'True if the supplier is marked as preferred for this part in the sourcing strategy.']
  lead_time_days varchar [note: 'Estimated number of days from order receipt to delivery.']
  minimum_order_quantity varchar [note: 'Smallest quantity the supplier will accept for this part.']
  net_price decimal(18,4) [note: 'Final price per unit after applying discounts, excluding tax.']
  payment_terms varchar [note: 'Conditions under which payment must be made for the quoted items.']
  ppap_commitment_date date [note: 'Date by which the supplier must complete Production Part Approval Process.']
  quote_date date [note: 'Quote date']
  quote_number varchar [note: 'Human‑readable reference number assigned to the quote by the procurement system.']
  quote_status varchar [note: 'Indicates the processing stage of the quote within the sourcing workflow.']
  quote_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the quote is a response to an RFQ, RFP, RFI, etc.']
  quoted_amount decimal(18,2)
  quoted_price decimal(18,4) [note: 'Base price per unit offered by the supplier.']
  submission_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time the supplier formally submitted the quote.']
  tax_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Estimated tax to be added to the net price.']
  tooling_cost decimal(18,4) [note: 'One‑time cost for tooling or molds required to produce the part.']
  total_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total amount']
  total_price decimal(18,4) [note: 'Final amount payable per unit after tax.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the quote record.']
  valid_until_date date
  validity_end_date date [note: 'Date after which the quoted prices expire.']
  validity_start_date date [note: 'Date from which the quoted prices are considered valid.']

  Note: 'Supplier response to a sourcing event (RFQ/RFP), capturing quoted unit price, tooling cost, amortization terms, lead time, MOQ (minimum order quantity), payment terms, validity period, PPAP (Production Part Approval Process) commitment date, and quote status (submitted, under-review, awarded, rejected). Enables competitive bid analysis and total cost of ownership (TCO) evaluation. Linked to sourcing events and specific part numbers.'
}

Table procurement.purchase_requisition {
  purchase_requisition_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the purchase requisition.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Requisition cost allocation to cost center is required for pre‑PO budgeting and approval.']
  eprocurement_portal_id varchar [note: 'FK to the e-procurement portal used for this requisition.']
  equipment_registry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to asset.equipment_registry. Business justification: Equipment Requisition initiates creation of a new equipment asset; linking requisition to equipment registry enables traceability from request to asset.']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Requisition GL account enables automatic posting of approved requisitions to the ledger.']
  opportunity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.opportunity. Business justification: Required for \'Procurement requisition generation from a won sales opportunity\' – experts expect a direct link to track which opportunity triggered the requisition.']
  plant_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to manufacturing.plant. Business justification: Required for Plant Requisition Allocation Report linking each requisition to its manufacturing plant.']
  primary_purchase_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employee who created the requisition.']
  procurement_supplier_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the selected vendor for the requisition (if known).']
  purchase_approved_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employee who approved the requisition.']
  purchase_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employee who created the requisition.']
  sku_master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.sku_master. Business justification: REQUIRED: Requisition planning and allocation reports need the SKU master to forecast demand and allocate inventory.']
  spend_category_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.spend_category. Business justification: Purchase requisitions should be classified by spend category for spend analytics and sourcing strategy alignment. Linking purchase_requisition to spend_category enables category-level demand visibilit']
  supplier_contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.supplier_contract. Business justification: In SAP MM, a purchase requisition can reference a source of supply (outline agreement/contract) to indicate which contract should be used for conversion to a PO. The existing source_of_supply field is']
  vehicle_program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to engineering.vehicle_program. Business justification: Program‑specific purchase requisition; finance and engineering track spend per vehicle program.']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the selected vendor for the requisition (if known).']
  account_assignment_category varchar [note: 'Category indicating how the cost will be allocated (e.g., cost center, project).']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current approval state of the requisition.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the requisition was approved.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 currency code for the estimated value.']
  data_governance_ref varchar [note: 'Reference to data governance catalog entry for lineage and ownership tracking']
  estimated_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Estimated monetary value of the requisition before approval.']
  is_converted_to_po boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the requisition has been converted into a purchase order.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑text field for additional comments or special instructions.']
  payment_terms varchar [note: 'Standard payment terms (e.g., NET30) associated with the requisition.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Priority level assigned to the requisition for processing urgency.']
  procurement_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the requisition is for direct materials, indirect, services, or capital assets.']
  purchase_group varchar [note: 'Organizational group responsible for processing the requisition.']
  purchase_order_number varchar [note: 'Reference number of the purchase order generated from this requisition.']
  purchase_requisition_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the requisition.']
  quantity decimal(15,3) [note: 'Quantity of the material or service requested.']
  record_audit_created timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the record was first captured in the system.']
  record_audit_updated timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the record.']
  required_delivery_date date [note: 'Date by which the material or service is needed.']
  requisition_date date [note: 'Date when the requisition was initially created.']
  requisition_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier assigned to the purchase requisition.']
  source_of_supply varchar [note: 'Specifies if the supply is internal, external vendor, or consignment.']
  tax_code varchar [note: 'Tax classification code applicable to the requisition.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Unit in which the quantity is expressed (e.g., EA, KG, L).']

  Note: 'Internal request to procure direct or indirect materials, tooling, or services. Captures requisition number, requestor, cost center, plant, material/service description, quantity, required delivery date, estimated value, account assignment category (cost center, project, asset), approval status, and conversion-to-PO status. Represents the demand signal that initiates the procure-to-pay cycle. Sourced from SAP MM MRP-generated or manually created purchase requisitions (BANF/EBAN).'
}

Table procurement.procurement_purchase_order {
  procurement_purchase_order_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the purchase order record.']
  compliance_document_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.compliance_document. Business justification: REQUIRED: PO must reference the compliance certification document for regulated parts (emissions, safety) to satisfy audit and legal reporting.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Required for PO cost allocation report; finance tracks spend by cost center for each purchase order.']
  created_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Purchase Order Creation – created_by_employee_id logs the employee who created the PO, a mandatory field for PO creation audit and responsibility tracking.']
  eprocurement_portal_id varchar [note: 'FK to the e-procurement portal used for this purchase order.']
  equipment_registry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to asset.equipment_registry. Business justification: Capital Equipment Procurement process requires linking PO to the registered equipment asset for depreciation, warranty, and maintenance tracking.']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: GL account needed for posting PO amounts to the general ledger; mandatory for financial statements.']
  inspection_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.inspection_plan. Business justification: Incoming Inspection Planning: each PO is assigned an inspection plan used by quality to inspect received parts; standard practice in automotive manufacturing.']
  plant_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the manufacturing plant or site receiving the goods/services.']
  procurement_supplier_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the supplier (vendor) to which the purchase order is issued.']
  production_line_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to manufacturing.production_line. Business justification: Needed for Line Delivery Schedule to allocate PO deliveries to specific production lines.']
  supplier_contract_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the underlying procurement contract or framework agreement, if applicable.']
  vehicle_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.vehicle_order. Business justification: Needed for \'Production scheduling report\' that ties each PO of parts to the specific vehicle order it supplies.']
  vehicle_program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to engineering.vehicle_program. Business justification: Purchase orders are linked to vehicle programs for cost allocation and program profitability analysis.']
  account_assignment varchar [note: 'Cost object (e.g., cost center, internal order) to which the PO costs are charged.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the internal approval workflow for the PO.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the purchase order record was first inserted into the data lake.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter code of the currency used for the monetary amounts.']
  currency_rate decimal(12,6) [note: 'Rate used to convert foreign currency amounts to the company code currency.']
  data_governance_ref varchar [note: 'Reference to data governance catalog entry for lineage and ownership tracking']
  delivery_date date [note: 'Planned date by which the supplier should deliver the ordered items.']
  goods_receipt_date date [note: 'Date on which the ordered goods were physically received at the plant.']
  gr_ir_control_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether Goods Receipt/Invoice Receipt (GR/IR) posting is active for this PO.']
  gross_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total payable amount including net amount, taxes, and any surcharges.']
  incoterms varchar [note: 'Standardized trade terms defining delivery responsibilities and costs.']
  invoice_receipt_date date [note: 'Date on which the supplier\'s invoice was recorded in the system.']
  net_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total amount of the purchase order before taxes, fees, and discounts.']
  order_date date [note: 'Date on which the purchase order was created or released.']
  payment_terms varchar [note: 'Negotiated terms governing when and how the supplier will be paid.']
  po_number varchar [note: 'Externally visible purchase order number assigned by the procurement system.']
  po_type varchar [note: 'Category of the purchase order indicating its procurement contract model.']
  procurement_purchase_order_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the purchase order within the procure-to-pay process.']
  purchase_group varchar [note: 'Organizational group responsible for processing the purchase order.']
  purchasing_organization varchar [note: 'Entity within the enterprise that conducts procurement activities.']
  supplier_name varchar [note: 'Legal name of the supplier organization.']
  tax_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Aggregate tax amount applicable to the purchase order.']
  tax_code varchar [note: 'Code representing the tax jurisdiction and rate applied to the PO.']
  total_quantity varchar [note: 'Aggregate quantity of all line items on the purchase order.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the purchase order record.']

  Note: 'Legally binding procurement document issued to a supplier for delivery of direct materials, indirect materials, MRO, tooling, or services. Captures PO number, PO type (standard, blanket, consignment, subcontracting, service), supplier, plant, delivery date, incoterms, payment terms, total net value, currency, tax code, account assignment, approval workflow status, and GR/IR (Goods Receipt/Invoice Receipt) control flags. Core transactional entity of the procure-to-pay process. Sourced from SAP MM (EKKO/EKPO).'
}

Enables granular spend tracking and goods receipt matching at the part level. Sourced from SAP MM PO item table (EKPO).'

Supports blanket PO releases and scheduling agreement delivery lines. Sourced from SAP MM contract (EKKO with doc type MK/WK).'
}

  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Last update timestamp']
  vat_number varchar [note: 'VAT number']

  Note: 'Supplier-submitted invoice for goods or services delivered to Automotive, processed through SAP MM invoice verification (Logistics Invoice Verification - LIV). Captures invoice number, supplier invoice reference, invoice date, posting date, gross amount, tax amount, currency, payment terms, due date, three-way match status (PO/GR/Invoice), tolerance check result, blocking reason, and payment status. Enables accounts payable processing and spend actuals capture. Sourced from SAP MM invoice document (RBKP/RSEG).'
}

Table procurement.info_record {

  effective_until date [note: 'Date on which the info record expires (null if open‑ended).']
  info_record_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier assigned to the info record, e.g., IR00001234.']
  info_record_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the info record.']
  info_record_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the info record (standard, contract, or framework agreement).']
  last_price_update_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent price change.']
  lead_time_days varchar [note: 'Planned delivery lead time in days from order to receipt.']
  minimum_order_quantity varchar [note: 'Minimum quantity that must be ordered under this info record.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or special instructions related to the info record.']

  updated_by varchar [note: 'System user identifier who last updated the record.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the info record.']
  vendor_evaluation_score decimal(3,2) [note: 'Score (0‑5) reflecting supplier performance for this material.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'System user identifier who created the record.']

  Note: 'Purchasing info record storing the commercial relationship between a supplier and a specific material or service category, including the last negotiated price, price validity period, planned delivery time, over/under-delivery tolerance, reminder days, and vendor evaluation score. Serves as the default pricing and delivery condition source when creating purchase orders. Sourced from SAP MM purchasing info record (EINE/EINA/ME11).'
}

Table procurement.supplier_evaluation {
  supplier_evaluation_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for each supplier evaluation record.']

  supplier_evaluator_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employee or system that performed the evaluation.']
  comments varchar [note: 'Free‑text notes from the evaluator providing context or observations.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the supplier meets regulatory and internal compliance requirements.']
  cost_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Score (0‑100) for commercial performance (price competitiveness, invoice accuracy).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the evaluation record was first created in the system.']
  delivery_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Score (0‑100) for delivery performance (OTD, schedule adherence).']
  development_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Score (0‑100) for development dimension (responsiveness, engineering change support).']
  digital_collaboration_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Score for supplier digital collaboration capability (e-procurement portal usage)']
  evaluation_date date [note: 'Date on which the evaluation was performed.']
  evaluation_method varchar [note: 'How the evaluation data was collected (automated, manual, or mixed).']
  evaluation_number varchar [note: 'Human‑readable identifier for the evaluation (e.g., EV‑2024‑Q1).']
  evaluation_period varchar
  evaluation_status varchar [note: 'Current processing state of the evaluation record.']
  evaluation_type varchar [note: 'Frequency or trigger of the evaluation (annual, quarterly, or ad‑hoc).']
  evaluation_version varchar [note: 'Version number for the evaluation record to support revisions.']
  failed_criteria_count varchar [note: 'Number of criteria where the supplier fell below the required threshold.']
  invoice_accuracy_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of invoices that match purchase order terms without discrepancies.']
  on_time_delivery_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of deliveries arriving on or before the promised date.']
  overall_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Aggregated score (0‑100) summarising supplier performance.']
  passed_criteria_count varchar [note: 'Number of criteria where the supplier met or exceeded the threshold.']
  period_end_date date [note: 'Last day of the period covered by the evaluation.']
  period_start_date date [note: 'First day of the period covered by the evaluation.']
  ppm_defect_rate decimal(6,3) [note: 'Parts‑per‑million defect rate observed during the period.']
  price_variance_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage difference between contracted price and actual invoiced price.']
  quality_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Score (0‑100) for quality dimension (PPM defect rate, PPAP compliance).']
  recommended_action varchar [note: 'Suggested strategic response based on evaluation results.']
  risk_level varchar [note: 'Risk classification derived from evaluation outcomes.']
  supplier_category varchar [note: 'Business classification of the supplier (e.g., Tier 1, Tier 2).']
  supplier_region varchar [note: 'ISO‑3 country code of the supplier\'s primary operating location.']
  total_criteria_count varchar [note: 'Number of evaluation criteria applied to the supplier.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the most recent modification to the evaluation record.']

  Note: 'Periodic formal assessment of supplier performance across quality (PPM defect rate, PPAP compliance), delivery (OTD - On-Time Delivery, schedule adherence), commercial (price competitiveness, invoice accuracy), and development (responsiveness, engineering change support) dimensions. Captures evaluation period, overall score, sub-scores by criterion, evaluator, evaluation method (automated from SAP QM/MM or manual), and recommended action (maintain, develop, reduce, disqualify). Supports supplier development programs and strategic sourcing decisions.'
}

  tax_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Tax component associated with the approved budget.']
  tax_code varchar [note: 'Tax code applicable to the requisition.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the requisition record.']
  wbs_element varchar [note: 'WBS element code linking the requisition to project accounting hierarchy.']

  Note: 'Capital expenditure procurement request for tooling, dies, fixtures, production equipment, or plant infrastructure required for new model programs or capacity expansion. Captures CapEx project code, asset category, justification type (new model, replacement, capacity), estimated investment, approved budget, plant, cost center, WBS element, AFE (Authorization for Expenditure) number, approval authority level, and CapEx status. Distinct from standard MRO or direct material purchase requisitions due to asset capitalization workflow.'
}

Business justification: Service entry sheets in SAP MM are always created against a service purchase order. The existing purchase_order_number is a denormalized STRING; a proper FK to procurement_purchase_order enables struc']
  procurement_supplier_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the supplier providing the service.']
  supplier_contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.supplier_contract. Business justification: Service entry sheets for framework service agreements should reference the governing supplier_contract. This enables contract utilization tracking, service spend against contract value monitoring, and']
  work_center_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the work center responsible for the service execution.']
  acceptance_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the service acceptance process.']
  acceptance_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the service entry sheet was accepted.']
  acceptor_name varchar [note: 'Name of the person who accepted the service entry sheet.']
  account_assignment varchar [note: 'Account assignment object (e.g., cost object, internal order) used for financial posting.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the service entry sheet record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for the monetary amounts.']
  data_governance_ref varchar [note: 'Reference to data governance catalog entry for lineage and ownership tracking']
  external_reference_code varchar [note: 'Optional reference to an external system identifier.']
  invoice_triggered boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the service entry sheet has triggered invoice verification (true) or not (false).']
  net_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Net monetary value after tax and any discounts.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes or comments entered by users.']
  quantity_performed decimal(12,2) [note: 'Actual quantity of service units delivered (e.g., hours, days).']
  service_category varchar [note: 'Classification of the service type performed.']
  service_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the service was performed (principal business event date).']
  service_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the service execution completed.']
  service_entry_sheet_description varchar [note: 'Free‑text description of the service performed.']
  service_entry_sheet_status varchar [note: 'Overall lifecycle status of the service entry sheet.']
  service_line_item varchar [note: 'Identifier of the line item on the service purchase order.']
  service_location varchar [note: 'Physical location or facility where the service was performed.']
  service_order_number varchar [note: 'External service order number associated with the entry sheet.']
  service_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the service execution began.']

  tax_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Tax component associated with the service total.']
  tax_code varchar [note: 'Tax code applicable to the service for tax calculation purposes.']
  total_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Gross monetary value of the services before taxes and discounts.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Unit in which the performed quantity is measured.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the service entry sheet record.']

  Note: 'Confirmation document for services rendered by a supplier against a service purchase order, used for indirect procurement (maintenance services, contract labor, engineering services, logistics services). Captures service entry sheet number, service PO reference, service line items, actual quantities performed, acceptance status, acceptor name, acceptance date, and value accepted. Triggers invoice verification for service-based procurement. Sourced from SAP MM service entry sheet (ML81N/ESLH/ESLL).'
}

Table procurement.procurement_price_condition {

  calculation_type varchar [note: 'How condition is calculated (fixed, percentage, per unit)']
  condition_amount decimal(18,2)
  condition_code varchar
  condition_rate decimal(18,2)

  data_governance_ref varchar [note: 'Reference to data governance catalog entry for lineage and ownership tracking']

  early_payment_discount_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Early payment discount percentage']
  escalation_formula varchar [note: 'Price escalation/de-escalation formula']
  freight_condition_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Freight cost condition amount']
  incoterm_code varchar [note: 'Incoterm applicable to this price condition (e.g., DDP, FCA, EXW)']
  is_active boolean [note: 'Whether this condition is currently active']
  material_number varchar
  maximum_order_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'Maximum order quantity cap for this price condition tier']
  minimum_order_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'Minimum order quantity threshold for this price condition to apply']
  negotiation_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to negotiation event or round']
  packaging_cost_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Packaging cost amount']
  price_amount decimal(18,2)
  price_basis_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'Quantity basis for per-unit price calculation (e.g., price per 100 units)']
  price_change_reason varchar [note: 'Reason for the most recent price condition change or update']
  price_list_type varchar [note: 'Type of price list (standard, promotional, contract)']
  price_unit decimal(18,3)
  raw_material_index_code varchar [note: 'Raw material price index code for escalation']
  scale_basis varchar [note: 'Basis for scale pricing (quantity, value, weight)']
  scale_quantity_from decimal(18,3) [note: 'Lower bound of the quantity scale for tiered pricing']
  scale_quantity_to decimal(18,3) [note: 'Upper bound of the quantity scale for tiered pricing']
  surcharge_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Additional surcharge amount (e.g., raw material surcharge, logistics surcharge)']
  tooling_amortization_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Tooling amortization amount per unit']
  unit_of_measure varchar
  updated_timestamp timestamp
  valid_from_date date [note: 'Start date of condition validity']
  valid_to_date date [note: 'End date of condition validity']
  volume_rebate_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Volume rebate percentage']
  valid_from date
  valid_to date

  Note: 'Negotiated pricing conditions and surcharges applicable to a supplier-material combination, including base price, freight surcharge, packaging surcharge, raw material index adjustment, currency exchange surcharge, and volume discount tiers. Captures condition type, validity period, scale basis, scale quantity, condition value, and currency. Enables accurate PO pricing and supports price variance analysis. Sourced from SAP MM condition records (KONP/KONH/MEK1). This product is the single source of truth (SSOT) for its concept in the ECM Lakehouse.'
}

  Note: 'Inbound delivery schedule communicated to suppliers specifying exact delivery windows, quantities, and dock assignments for JIT and JIS supply to assembly plants. Enables synchronized inbound logistics and production line feeding. Distinct from scheduling agreement lines which are the contractual commitment.'
}

Table procurement.supplier_nonconformance {

  closure_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the nonconformance was formally closed.']

  corrective_action_due_date date [note: 'Date by which the supplier must implement a corrective action.']
  corrective_action_status varchar [note: 'Current state of the supplier corrective action request (SCAR).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the nonconformance record was first created in the lakehouse.']
  defect_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code that classifies the type of defect (e.g., surface, dimensional, functional).']
  defect_description varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the observed defect.']
  detected_date date [note: 'Detected date']
  detection_point varchar [note: 'Location in the value chain where the nonconformance was detected.']
  detection_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact date‑time when the defect was first recorded.']
  eight_d_report_reference varchar [note: 'Identifier of the associated 8‑D problem‑solving report.']
  ncr_number varchar [note: 'NCR number']
  nonconformance_number varchar [note: 'Business‑visible identifier assigned to the nonconformance event, used in reporting and communication.']
  nonconformance_status varchar
  part_description varchar [note: 'Brief textual description of the part.']
  part_number varchar [note: 'Manufacturer part number (or SKU) of the component that failed inspection.']
  portal_submission_flag boolean [note: 'Whether nonconformance was submitted via supplier collaboration portal']

  supplier_nonconformance_status varchar [note: 'Status']
