Supports homologation compliance and import duty cost tracking.'
}

Table logistics.vehicle_handover {
  vehicle_handover_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for each vehicle handover record.']
  aftersales_repair_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to aftersales.repair_order. Business justification: Process: Vehicle handover records are tied to the corresponding repair order to monitor service flow and liability.']
  compliance_document_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.compliance_document. Business justification: Needed for Vehicle Handover Compliance Certification linking handover record to the vehicle\'s compliance certificate.']
  connected_vehicle_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to mobility.connected_vehicle. Business justification: Vehicle handover workflow associates the handed‑over VIN with the connected vehicle to trigger post‑delivery service enrollment.']
  finished_vehicle_stock_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.finished_vehicle_stock. Business justification: Needed for Vehicle Handover Warranty Traceability: links handover record to inventory vehicle record for warranty and compliance tracking.']
  production_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to manufacturing.production_order. Business justification: Dealer handover requires linking to the production order to support warranty registration and regulatory compliance.']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Revenue from vehicle handover is recognized in the responsible profit center for margin reporting.']
  receiving_party_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the receiving party (dealer or customer).']
  shipment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to logistics.shipment. Business justification: Linking handover to shipment provides traceability from transport to dealer receipt.']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.sku. Business justification: Handover record needs SKU to capture exact vehicle configuration handed to dealer.']
  vehicle_created_by_user_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the system user who created the handover record.']
  vehicle_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the system user who created the handover record.']
  vehicle_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.vehicle_order. Business justification: Final handover of a vehicle to the dealer is tied to the sales order for warranty activation and final invoicing.']
  vin_registry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to vehicle.vin_registry. Business justification: Enables Vehicle Handover audit linking handover record to VIN master for warranty start and liability tracking.']
  acceptance_signature_status varchar [note: 'Status of the acceptance signature: signed, unsigned, or pending.']
  carrier_name varchar [note: 'Name of the logistics carrier responsible for the transport.']
  container_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the shipping container (if applicable) used for the vehicle.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the handover record was initially created in the system.']
  customs_document_number varchar [note: 'Reference number of the customs clearance document.']
  delay_reason varchar [note: 'Reason for any delay affecting the handover schedule.']
  emissions_kg_co2 decimal(8,2) [note: 'Carbon dioxide emissions generated during transport, measured in kilograms.']
  export_import_flag boolean [note: 'True if the handover involves cross‑border export or import.']
  fuel_consumption_liters decimal(8,2) [note: 'Fuel consumed by the transport vehicle during the handover journey.']
  handover_condition varchar [note: 'Condition of the vehicle at handover: new, used, reconditioned, or damaged.']
  handover_fee_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Gross fee charged for the handover service.']
  handover_fee_currency varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 currency code for the handover fee.']
  handover_fee_net_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Net amount after tax for the handover fee.']
  handover_fee_tax_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Tax component of the handover fee.']
  handover_location varchar [note: 'Physical location (plant, compound, yard, or dealer site) where the handover took place.']
  handover_number varchar [note: 'Business-assigned handover reference number used for tracking and communication.']
  handover_status varchar [note: 'Current processing status of the handover record.']
  handover_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact date and time when the handover event occurred.']
  handover_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the handover scenario: dealer stock, retail customer, export, or import.']
  hazardous_material_flag boolean [note: 'True if the vehicle or its cargo includes hazardous materials.']
  hazardous_material_type varchar [note: 'Classification of hazardous material, if applicable.']
  home_delivery_flag boolean [note: 'Whether this handover was a home delivery.']
  incoterms_code varchar [note: 'International commercial term code governing the shipment (e.g., FOB, CIF).']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes captured during the handover process.']
  odometer_reading_km decimal(10,2) [note: 'Vehicle odometer reading at the moment of handover, expressed in kilometers.']
  otd_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the handover met the agreed on‑time delivery target.']
  pdi_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier linking to the Pre‑Delivery Inspection record.']
  receiving_party_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the receiving party is a dealer or an end‑customer.']
  temperature_control_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether temperature control was required for the shipment.']
  temperature_max_c decimal(5,2) [note: 'Maximum temperature maintained during transport, expressed in degrees Celsius.']
  temperature_min_c decimal(5,2) [note: 'Minimum temperature maintained during transport, expressed in degrees Celsius.']
  transport_mode varchar [note: 'Mode of transport used for moving the vehicle to the handover point.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the handover record.']
  warranty_end_date date [note: 'Date when the vehicle warranty expires.']
  warranty_start_date date [note: 'Date when the vehicle warranty becomes effective after handover.']

  Note: 'Record of formal vehicle handover from logistics/compound to dealer or end customer. Captures VIN, handover date, handover location, receiving party (dealer/customer), handover type (dealer stock/retail customer), odometer reading at handover, handover condition, PDI reference, and acceptance signature status. Marks the transfer of custody and triggers downstream billing and warranty start events.'
}

Table logistics.load_plan {
  load_plan_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key']

  capacity_utilization_pct decimal(5,2)
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Record creation timestamp']

  load_plan_number varchar [note: 'Load plan number']
  load_plan_status varchar
  load_status varchar [note: 'Load plan status']
  max_capacity varchar [note: 'Maximum capacity']
  planned_arrival_date date
  planned_delivery_date date [note: 'Planned delivery date']
  planned_departure_date date
  planned_load_date date [note: 'Planned load date']
  planned_volume_cbm decimal(12,3)
  planned_weight_kg decimal(12,2)
  returnable_container_count varchar [note: 'Number of returnable containers in this load plan.']
  transport_mode varchar [note: 'Transport mode']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Last update timestamp']
  utilization_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Utilization percentage']
  vehicle_count varchar [note: 'Number of vehicles in load']

  Note: 'Load planning record defining how finished vehicles are grouped and assigned to transport units (truck trailers, rail autoracks, RoRo vessel decks) for a given shipment or transport order. Captures load plan number, transport unit type, vehicle count, VIN assignments, weight/dimension constraints, loading sequence, special handling requirements (e.g., EV battery transport restrictions), and plan status. Supports transport utilization optimization and loading dock scheduling.'
}

Table logistics.vehicle_shipment_assignment {
  vehicle_shipment_assignment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key']
  connected_vehicle_id varchar
  last_mile_carrier_id varchar [note: 'FK to the carrier handling last-mile delivery segment.']
  load_plan_id varchar [note: 'FK to load plan']
  shipment_id varchar [note: 'FK to shipment']
  vin_registry_id varchar [note: 'FK to VIN registry']
  assignment_date date [note: 'Assignment date']
  assignment_status varchar [note: 'Assignment status']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Record creation timestamp']
  damage_flag boolean
  loaded_timestamp timestamp
  loading_sequence varchar
  notes varchar
  position_in_container varchar
  position_number varchar [note: 'Position number on transport']
  unloaded_timestamp timestamp
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Last update timestamp']
  vehicle_shipment_status varchar
  yard_slot_code varchar [note: 'Assigned yard slot code at destination compound.']

  Note: 'Represents the assignment of a connected vehicle to a specific outbound shipment. Each record captures the relationship between one shipment and one connected vehicle and includes attributes that are specific to that assignment, such as the vehicle\'s position in the container and its status within the shipment.. Existence Justification: A shipment can contain many connected vehicles and a connected vehicle can be part of multiple shipments over its lifecycle (e.g., initial plant shipment, returns, service relocations). Logistics planners actively create and maintain records linking each vehicle to a shipment, and the link carries attributes such as the vehicle\'s position in the container.'
}

Table logistics.route {
  route_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for route']
  carrier_id varchar [note: 'FK to logistics.carrier']
  return_route_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on route (return_route_id)']
  actual_arrival_time timestamp [note: 'Real‑time arrival timestamp recorded when a shipment reaches the destination.']
  actual_departure_time timestamp [note: 'Real‑time departure timestamp recorded when a shipment leaves the origin.']
  average_travel_time_minutes varchar [note: 'Historical average travel time for the route in minutes.']
  carrier_mode varchar [note: 'Primary transportation mode used on the route.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Current regulatory compliance state of the route.']
  cost_usd decimal(12,2) [note: 'Average cost incurred to operate the route per shipment, expressed in US dollars.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the route record was first created.']
  dangerous_goods_approved_flag boolean [note: 'Whether this route is approved for dangerous goods transport.']
  destination_location varchar [note: 'Standard code of the ending location or depot for the route.']
  distance_km decimal(10,2) [note: 'Typical distance covered by the route in kilometers.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the route definition becomes active.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the route definition is retired or superseded; null if open‑ended.']
  emissions_kg_co2 decimal(10,2) [note: 'Estimated carbon dioxide emissions generated per shipment on the route.']
  fuel_consumption_l_per_100km decimal(5,2) [note: 'Typical fuel consumption for the route expressed in liters per 100 kilometers.']
  is_express boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the route supports express (time‑critical) shipments.']
  last_maintenance_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent maintenance activity affecting the route (e.g., road work, carrier audit).']
  last_mile_segment_flag boolean [note: 'Whether this route includes a last-mile delivery segment.']
  maintenance_interval_days varchar [note: 'Standard number of days between required maintenance checks for the route.']
  max_load_capacity_tons decimal(8,2) [note: 'Maximum payload the route can handle, expressed in metric tons.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional free‑form remarks or special instructions for the route.']
  on_time_performance_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of shipments that arrived on or before the scheduled arrival time.']
  origin_location varchar [note: 'Standard code of the starting location or depot for the route.']
  priority_level varchar [note: 'Business priority assigned to the route for planning and allocation.']
  regulatory_region varchar [note: 'Geopolitical region governing compliance requirements for the route.']
  returnable_packaging_route_flag boolean [note: 'Whether this route is used for returnable packaging circulation.']
  route_code varchar [note: 'Standardized alphanumeric code used to reference the route in logistics systems.']
  route_description varchar [note: 'Free‑form text describing the route, its purpose, and any special handling notes.']
  route_group varchar [note: 'Logical grouping identifier for related routes (e.g., region, corridor).']
  route_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the transportation route.']
  route_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the route.']
  route_type varchar [note: 'Category describing the geographic scope of the route.']
  scheduled_arrival_time timestamp [note: 'Planned arrival date and time for shipments on this route.']
  scheduled_departure_time timestamp [note: 'Planned departure date and time for shipments on this route.']
  transport_mode varchar
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time of the most recent modification to the route record.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for route. Referenced by route_id.'
}

Table logistics.container {
  container_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for container']
  carrier_id varchar [note: 'FK to logistics.carrier']
  parent_container_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on container (parent_container_id)']
  actual_arrival_date date [note: 'Date the container actually arrived at destination.']
  condition varchar [note: 'Overall physical condition of the container.']
  container_number varchar [note: 'Standard alphanumeric container identification number printed on the container exterior.']
  container_status varchar [note: 'Current operational state of the container within the logistics network.']
  container_type varchar [note: 'Category of container based on design and intended cargo (e.g., dry, refrigerated).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the container record was first created in the system.']
  current_cycle_count varchar [note: 'Current number of completed cycles.']
  current_location varchar [note: 'Identifier of the facility, yard, or terminal where the container is presently located.']
  customs_clearance_date date [note: 'Date when the container cleared customs.']
  customs_status varchar [note: 'Current customs processing status for the container.']
  damage_description varchar [note: 'Narrative description of any reported damage.']
  damage_report_flag boolean [note: 'True if a damage incident has been recorded for the container.']
  dangerous_goods_approved_flag boolean [note: 'Whether this container is approved for dangerous goods transport.']
  dangerous_goods_certified_flag boolean [note: 'Whether container is certified for dangerous goods transport']
  departure_date date [note: 'Date the container left the origin facility.']
  estimated_arrival_date date [note: 'Planned date for the container\'s arrival at destination.']
  field_service_info varchar [note: 'Generic field service info placeholder']
  gps_latitude double [note: 'Latitude coordinate from the container\'s GPS device.']
  gps_longitude double [note: 'Longitude coordinate from the container\'s GPS device.']
  humidity_control boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the container can regulate humidity levels.']
  is_reefer boolean
  is_returnable boolean [note: 'Whether this container is a returnable/reusable packaging unit']
  last_event_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent logistics event associated with the container.']
  last_inspection_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent safety and condition inspection.']
  lease_end_date date [note: 'Date when the container lease or ownership period ends; null if open‑ended.']
  lease_start_date date [note: 'Date when the container lease or ownership period began.']
  location_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the current location was recorded.']
  maintenance_status varchar [note: 'Current maintenance compliance of the container.']
  max_cycle_count varchar [note: 'Maximum number of cycles before container retirement.']
  max_payload_kg decimal(10,2) [note: 'Maximum cargo weight the container can carry, calculated as weight_capacity minus tare_weight.']
  next_maintenance_due date [note: 'Planned date for the next scheduled maintenance activity.']
  owner varchar
  owner_company varchar [note: 'Legal entity that owns or leases the container.']
  pool_code varchar [note: 'Returnable container pool circuit code.']
  pool_owner varchar [note: 'Owner of the container pool (OEM, supplier, third-party)']
  refrigerated decimal(18,2) [note: 'True if the container is a refrigerated (reefer) unit.']
  seal_number varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the security seal applied to the container.']
  size varchar
  size_feet varchar [note: 'Standard length of the container in feet (e.g., 20, 40).']
  tare_weight_kg decimal(10,2) [note: 'Weight of the empty container without any cargo.']
  temperature_control boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the container is equipped with temperature regulation.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the container record.']
  volume_cbm decimal(12,3)
  voyage_number varchar [note: 'Identifier for the specific shipment voyage the container is part of.']
  weight_capacity_kg decimal(10,2) [note: 'Maximum allowable gross weight for the container, including cargo and tare weight.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for container. Referenced by container_id.'
}

Table logistics.shipping_line {
  shipping_line_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for shipping_line']
  parent_shipping_line_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on shipping_line (parent_shipping_line_id)']
  address_line1 varchar [note: 'First line of the shipping line\'s primary office address.']
  address_line2 varchar [note: 'Second line of the shipping line\'s primary office address (optional).']
  carrier_type varchar [note: 'Category of transport services provided by the shipping line.']
  city varchar [note: 'City of the shipping line\'s primary office.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Current compliance status of the shipping line with regulatory and internal standards.']
  country varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 3166‑1 alpha‑3 country code of the shipping line\'s primary office. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: USA|CAN|MEX|GBR|DEU|FRA|JPN|CHN|KOR|BRA|IND|AUS — promote to reference product]']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the shipping line record was first created in the system.']
  dangerous_goods_certified_flag boolean [note: 'Whether this shipping line is certified for dangerous goods transport.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the shipping line became an active carrier for the enterprise.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the shipping line\'s contract or relationship ends (null if open‑ended).']
  fleet_size varchar [note: 'Total number of vessels or transport units owned or operated by the carrier.']
  headquarters_country varchar
  hub_location varchar [note: 'Main operational hub or terminal used by the carrier for consolidation.']
  insurance_cert_number varchar [note: 'Reference number of the carrier\'s liability insurance certificate.']
  max_container_capacity varchar [note: 'Maximum number of twenty‑foot equivalent units the carrier can handle per shipment.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form field for any supplemental information about the shipping line.']
  postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal/ZIP code of the shipping line\'s primary office.']
  preferred_currency varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 currency code used for billing and settlements with the carrier.']
  primary_contact_email varchar [note: 'Email address for the primary contact.']
  primary_contact_name varchar [note: 'Name of the primary business contact for the shipping line.']
  primary_contact_phone varchar [note: 'Telephone number for the primary contact.']
  rating_score decimal(3,2) [note: 'Average performance rating (0.00‑5.00) assigned by the enterprise based on service quality, timeliness, and safety.']
  scac_code varchar [note: 'Four‑character code that uniquely identifies the carrier in transportation systems.']
  service_region varchar [note: 'Geographic region(s) where the carrier provides services (e.g., North America, Europe).']
  shipping_line_code varchar
  shipping_line_name varchar [note: 'Full legal name of the shipping line (carrier).']
  shipping_line_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the shipping line within the enterprise.']
  short_name varchar [note: 'Abbreviated or commonly used short name for the shipping line.']
  state varchar [note: 'State or province of the shipping line\'s primary office.']
  tax_identifier varchar [note: 'Government‑issued tax identifier for the carrier entity.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time of the most recent update to the shipping line record.']
  website_url varchar [note: 'Public website URL of the shipping line.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for shipping_line. Referenced by shipping_line_id.'
}

Table inventory.sku_master {
  sku_master_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key']
  abc_classification varchar [note: 'ABC classification']
  base_unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Base unit of measure']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Record creation timestamp']
  data_governance_ref varchar [note: 'Reference to data governance catalog entry for lineage and ownership tracking']
  gross_weight decimal(10,3) [note: 'Gross weight']
  is_batch_managed boolean [note: 'Whether batch managed']
  is_serialized boolean [note: 'Whether item is serialized']
  material_group varchar [note: 'Material group']
  material_type varchar [note: 'Material type']
  net_weight decimal(10,3) [note: 'Net weight']
  shelf_life_days varchar [note: 'Shelf life in days']
  sku_description varchar [note: 'SKU description']
  sku_number varchar [note: 'SKU number']
  sku_status varchar [note: 'SKU status']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Last update timestamp']
  weight_unit varchar [note: 'Weight unit']

  Note: 'SSOT for all Stock Keeping Unit (SKU) definitions across the enterprise. Owns the material master record for raw materials, production components, WIP sub-assemblies, finished vehicles, and service parts. Aligned with SAP MM material master (MARA/MARC). Captures SKU identity, classification, unit of measure, weight/dimensions, hazardous material flags, shelf-life, and MRP planning parameters. Referenced by all inventory movement and stock transactions.'
}

Table inventory.storage_location {
  storage_location_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for the storage location.']
  environmental_permit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.environmental_permit. Business justification: Hazardous material handling: storage locations need to be linked to the environmental permit governing emissions.']
  functional_location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to asset.functional_location. Business justification: MAINTENANCE PLANNING: Mapping storage locations to functional locations enables maintenance schedules and OEE reports that require inventory location context.']
  plant_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the plant to which the location belongs.']
  responsible_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Safety audit reports require a location manager; linking storage_location to employee enables tracking responsible employee for each location.']
  warehouse_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the warehouse or distribution center containing the location.']
  address_line1 varchar [note: 'Primary street address of the location.']
  address_line2 varchar [note: 'Secondary address information (suite, building).']
  agv_routing_priority varchar [note: 'Priority used by automated guided vehicles when selecting this location.']
  aisle varchar [note: 'Aisle designation within the zone.']
  bin varchar [note: 'Specific bin or shelf code for inventory placement.']
  capacity_quantity decimal(12,2) [note: 'Maximum amount of material the location can hold.']
  capacity_uom varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for capacity (e.g., kg, m3, units).']
  city varchar [note: 'City where the location is situated.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO country code of the location.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the record was first created in the system.']
  data_governance_ref varchar [note: 'Reference to data governance catalog entry for lineage and ownership tracking']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the location became active for inventory.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the location is scheduled to be retired (null if indefinite).']
  external_system_source varchar [note: 'Source system that provides the master data for this location.']
  fire_safety_rating varchar [note: 'Fire‑safety classification of the location.']
  hazardous_material_allowed boolean [note: 'True if hazardous parts may be stored in this location.']
  inventory_accuracy_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Measured accuracy of inventory records for this location.']
  is_default_location boolean [note: 'Indicates if this is the default location for new stock of its type.']
  last_inventory_count_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent physical inventory count.']
  latitude decimal(9,6) [note: 'Geographic latitude of the location.']
  location_code varchar [note: 'External code used in ERP/WMS to identify the location.']
  location_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the storage location.']
  location_type varchar [note: 'Category describing the physical storage configuration.']
  longitude decimal(9,6) [note: 'Geographic longitude of the location.']
  postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal or ZIP code for the location.']
  rack varchar [note: 'Rack identifier where the bin is located.']
  state varchar [note: 'State or province of the location.']
  storage_location_description varchar [note: 'Free‑form description or notes about the location.']
  storage_location_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the location.']
  temperature_controlled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the location is climate‑controlled.']
  temperature_range_celsius varchar [note: 'Allowed temperature range for the location (e.g., "0-5").']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the record.']
  used_capacity_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Current utilization of the location expressed as a percentage of total capacity.']
  zone varchar [note: 'Logical zone within the warehouse (e.g., receiving, bulk, cold).']

  Note: 'Master record for all physical and logical storage locations within plants, warehouses, distribution centers, and dealer parts depots. Captures location hierarchy (plant → warehouse → storage type → storage bin), location type (bulk, rack, floor, cold-chain), capacity constraints, and WM (Warehouse Management) configuration. Aligned with SAP WM storage location and bin master. Enables precise bin-level inventory tracking and AGV routing.'
}

Table inventory.stock_balance {
  stock_balance_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for each stock balance record.']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Stock balance valuation amounts must be posted to a GL account for financial statements.']
  sku_master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.sku_master. Business justification: stock_balance records inventory per SKU; linking to sku_master provides authoritative SKU attributes and removes redundant material_number.']
  storage_location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.storage_location. Business justification: stock_balance also references a storage location; adding storage_location_id FK normalizes location data.']
  batch_number varchar [note: 'Identifier of the production batch when batch management is active.']
  blocked_stock_qty varchar [note: 'Quantity that is blocked due to quality or administrative reasons.']
  consignment_stock_qty varchar [note: 'Quantity owned by a supplier but stored at the plant.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for the valuation price.']
  data_governance_ref varchar [note: 'Reference to data governance catalog entry for lineage and ownership tracking']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date after which the material is considered expired or unusable.']
  goods_movement_type varchar [note: 'SAP movement type code indicating the nature of the transaction (e.g., receipt, issue, transfer).']
  in_transit_stock_qty varchar [note: 'Quantity that is currently being transferred between locations.']
  is_serialized boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the material is managed at the serial number level.']
  last_movement_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time of the most recent goods movement affecting this stock record.']
  lifecycle_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the stock quantity (e.g., unrestricted, blocked).']
  lot_number varchar [note: 'Lot identifier for traceability of the material.']
  manufacturing_date date [note: 'Date the material was produced or assembled.']
  physical_location_hierarchy varchar [note: 'Concatenated path representing plant, warehouse, aisle, and bin (e.g., PL01/WH02/AIS03/BIN04).']
  plant_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the manufacturing plant or site where the stock is held.']
  purchase_order_number varchar [note: 'Most recent purchase order that affected the stock balance.']
  quality_inspection_stock_qty varchar [note: 'Quantity currently under quality inspection.']
  quality_status varchar [note: 'Result of the latest quality inspection for the stock batch.']
  quantity_on_hand varchar [note: 'Total physical quantity of the material available at the location.']
  record_audit_created timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the stock balance record was first created in the data lake.']
  record_audit_updated timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the stock balance record.']
  safety_stock_qty varchar [note: 'Reserved quantity to protect against demand variability.']
  serial_number varchar [note: 'Serial number of the individual unit when serialization is enabled.']
  stock_category varchar [note: 'Broad classification of the stock for reporting (e.g., raw material, finished good).']
  supplier_code varchar [note: 'Code of the primary supplier for the material.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Unit in which the stock quantity is measured (e.g., EA, KG, L).']
  unrestricted_stock_qty varchar [note: 'Quantity that is free for use or sale.']
  valuation_area_code varchar [note: 'Accounting valuation area for inventory valuation purposes.']
  valuation_price decimal(15,2) [note: 'Monetary value per unit for the material based on the valuation type.']
  valuation_type varchar [note: 'Method used for inventory valuation.']

  Note: 'Current on-hand stock balance snapshot for each SKU at each storage location, plant, and valuation area. Captures unrestricted stock, quality inspection stock, blocked stock, consignment stock, in-transit stock, and safety stock levels. Aligned with SAP MM stock overview (MMBE / MARD). Supports MRP execution, inventory turnover analysis, and obsolescence monitoring. Updated by every goods movement transaction.'
}

Table inventory.goods_movement {
  goods_movement_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for each goods movement transaction.']
  agv_movement_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the Automated Guided Vehicle that executed the movement, when applicable.']
  batch_record_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.batch_record. Business justification: goods_movement tracks batch movements; linking to batch_record centralizes batch attributes.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Goods movement postings require a cost‑center for internal cost allocation; finance cost_center is the authoritative source for reporting.']
  goods_customer_party_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the customer for goods issue movements.']
  goods_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the system user who posted the movement.']
  goods_party_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the customer for goods issue movements.']
  goods_posted_by_user_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the system user who posted the movement.']
  movement_type_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.inventory_movement_type. Business justification: Add FK to standardize movement type lookup, remove redundant free‑text column, and give inventory_movement_type an inbound reference.']
  part_master_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the material or component being moved.']
  procurement_purchase_order_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the purchase order associated with a goods receipt.']
  procurement_supplier_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the supplier for goods receipt movements.']
  production_order_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the production order linked to the movement.']
  reversal_of_movement_goods_movement_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the original goods movement that this record reverses.']
  serialized_unit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.serialized_unit. Business justification: goods_movement may involve serialized components; linking to serialized_unit provides full component details.']
  source_storage_location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.storage_location. Business justification: Normalize source storage location reference; replace string with FK to storage_location']
  amount_local decimal(15,2) [note: 'Value of the movement in the plant\'s local currency.']
  amount_usd decimal(15,2) [note: 'Value of the movement converted to US dollars for consolidated reporting.']
  base_uom varchar [note: 'Unit of measure associated with the quantity field.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the goods movement record was initially created.']
  currency varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for monetary values.']
  data_governance_ref varchar [note: 'Reference to data governance catalog entry for lineage and ownership tracking']
  delivery_note_number varchar [note: 'Delivery document number for goods issue movements.']
  destination_plant varchar [note: 'Plant receiving the material.']
  destination_storage_location varchar [note: 'Storage location in the destination plant where the material is placed.']
  goods_movement_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the goods movement record.']
  inspection_document_number varchar [note: 'Identifier of the quality inspection document associated with the movement.']
  is_automated boolean [note: 'True if the movement was performed automatically by a system (e.g., AGV).']
  is_lot_tracked boolean [note: 'True if the material is tracked by lot.']
  is_serial_tracked boolean [note: 'True if the material is tracked by serial number.']
  line_sequence varchar [note: 'Ordering number of this line within the goods movement document.']
  location_zone varchar [note: 'Specific zone or area within the warehouse for the storage location.']
  lot_number varchar [note: 'Lot number used for traceability of batch‑controlled items.']
  movement_reason varchar [note: 'Business reason prompting the inventory movement.']
  posting_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the goods movement was posted.']
  posting_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact date and time when the movement was posted.']
  quality_inspection_status varchar [note: 'Outcome of any quality inspection linked to the movement.']
  quantity decimal(15,3) [note: 'Quantity of material moved, expressed in the base unit of measure.']
  reference_document_number varchar [note: 'Number of the source document that triggered the movement, such as purchase order or production order.']
  reference_document_type varchar [note: 'Type of the reference document (Purchase Order, Production Order, Work Order, Delivery, Service Order).']
  reversal_indicator boolean [note: 'True if this record represents a reversal of a previous movement.']
  source_plant varchar [note: 'Plant where the material originated.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the record.']
  valuation_type varchar [note: 'Inventory valuation method used for this movement.']
  warehouse_number varchar [note: 'Identifier of the warehouse where the movement occurs.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of every inventory movement event including goods receipts (GR), goods issues (GI), stock transfers, returns, and scrapping. Aligned with SAP MM material document (MSEG/MKPF). Captures movement type, quantity, source and destination storage locations, reference document (purchase order, production order, delivery), posting date, and batch/serial number. Provides full audit trail for lot traceability and IATF 16949 compliance.'
}

Table inventory.stock_transfer_order {
  stock_transfer_order_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the stock transfer order.']
  destination_bin_id varchar [note: 'Warehouse bin where the material will be placed.']
  destination_plant_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the plant receiving the stock.']
  primary_stock_employee_id varchar [note: 'Employee responsible for picking the material.']
  source_bin_id varchar [note: 'Warehouse bin from which the material is taken.']
  source_plant_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the plant where stock is sourced.']
  stock_created_by_user_employee_id varchar [note: 'User identifier of the person who created the transfer order.']
  stock_employee_id varchar [note: 'Employee responsible for picking the material.']
  stock_updated_by_user_employee_id varchar [note: 'User identifier of the last person who modified the transfer order.']
  tertiary_stock_updated_by_user_employee_id varchar [note: 'User identifier of the last person who modified the transfer order.']
  vehicle_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.vehicle_order. Business justification: Transfer order creation is driven by a vehicle order; linking them supports the Transfer Order Planning report used in production logistics.']
  agv_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the AGV assigned to execute the transfer.']
  batch_number varchar [note: 'Batch identifier for the material batch being moved.']
  confirmation_status varchar [note: 'Result of the confirmation step before execution.']
  cost_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Internal cost associated with moving the stock.']
  cost_center_code varchar [note: 'Internal cost‑center responsible for the transfer expense.']
  cost_currency varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for the cost amount.']
  data_governance_ref varchar [note: 'Reference to data governance catalog entry for lineage and ownership tracking']
  execution_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the stock transfer was completed.']
  execution_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when physical movement of stock began.']
  handling_instructions varchar [note: 'Free‑text instructions for special handling during transfer.']
  hazardous_material_flag boolean [note: 'True if the material is classified as hazardous.']
  is_jis boolean [note: 'Indicates if the transfer follows Just‑In‑Sequence sequencing.']
  is_jit boolean [note: 'Indicates if the transfer supports Just‑In‑Time replenishment.']
  lot_number varchar [note: 'Lot or batch identifier for traceability.']
  material_description varchar [note: 'Human‑readable description of the material.']
  material_number varchar [note: 'SAP material master number of the item being transferred.']
  movement_reason_code varchar [note: 'Reason code explaining why the stock is being transferred.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Business priority assigned to the transfer order.']
  project_number varchar [note: 'Project identifier if the transfer is linked to a specific project.']
  quantity decimal(15,3) [note: 'Amount of material to be moved.']
  record_audit_created timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the record was first captured.']
  record_audit_updated timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the record.']
  serial_number_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the material is tracked by serial number.']
  special_handling_flag boolean [note: 'True if any non‑standard handling procedures apply.']
  stock_transfer_order_status varchar [note: 'Current processing status of the transfer order.']
  temperature_control_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether temperature‑controlled handling is required.']
  transfer_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the transfer order was created in the system.']
  transfer_order_number varchar [note: 'External reference number assigned to the transfer order.']
  transfer_type varchar [note: 'Category of stock movement represented by the order.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Measurement unit for the transfer quantity.']

  Note: 'Warehouse Management transfer order governing the physical movement of stock between storage bins, storage types, or plants. Aligned with SAP WM transfer order (LT0A). Captures source and destination bin, transfer quantity, movement reason, AGV assignment, picker assignment, confirmation status, and execution timestamps. Supports JIT/JIS sequencing for line-side replenishment and inter-plant stock balancing.'
}

Table inventory.mrp_requirement {
  mrp_requirement_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the MRP requirement record.']
  mrp_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the internal planner or user who created/maintained the requirement.']
  mrp_planner_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the internal planner or user who created/maintained the requirement.']
  procurement_supplier_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the preferred supplier for the material, if known.']
  regulatory_requirement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_requirement. Business justification: Planning under regulation: MRP calculations consider mandatory regulatory constraints for material planning.']
  storage_location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.storage_location. Business justification: Link MRP requirement to storage_location for proper location tracking']
  batch_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the material is managed in batches.']
  data_governance_ref varchar [note: 'Reference to data governance catalog entry for lineage and ownership tracking']
  demand_source varchar [note: 'Origin of the demand that generated the requirement.']
  exception_message varchar [note: 'System-generated message describing any planning exception for the requirement.']
  lead_time_days varchar [note: 'Planned procurement or production lead time expressed in days.']
  lot_size decimal(15,3) [note: 'Minimum production or procurement lot size applicable to the material.']
  material_number varchar [note: 'SAP master data identifier for the material (SKU).']
  mrp_requirement_status varchar [note: 'Current processing state of the requirement.']
  planning_horizon_days varchar [note: 'Number of days covered by the planning run that produced this requirement.']
  planning_scenario varchar [note: 'Planning run scenario used to generate the requirement (e.g., MPS, MRP, DRP).']
  plant_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the manufacturing plant where the requirement applies.']
  priority_code varchar [note: 'Business priority assigned to the requirement for planning urgency.']
  quantity_required decimal(15,3) [note: 'Total quantity of the material required for the planning horizon.']
  record_audit_created timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the requirement record was first created in the system.']
  record_audit_updated timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the requirement record.']
  reorder_point decimal(15,3) [note: 'Inventory level that triggers a new procurement proposal.']
  requirement_date date [note: 'Date by which the material is needed to meet production schedules.']
  requirement_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier assigned to the requirement by the MRP run.']
  requirement_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the requirement is independent (external demand) or dependent (internal component need).']
  safety_stock decimal(15,3) [note: 'Buffer stock maintained to protect against demand variability and supply delays.']
  source_of_supply varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the material will be sourced internally or from external suppliers.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Measurement unit for the required quantity.']

  Note: 'MRP (Material Requirements Planning) planned requirement record generated by SAP MRP run (MD04/MD05). Captures dependent and independent demand requirements for each SKU, planned order proposals, reorder points, lot sizes, lead times, and exception messages. Drives procurement requisitions and production orders. Supports safety stock calculation, demand smoothing, and supply gap analysis across the manufacturing network.'
}

Table inventory.cycle_count {
  cycle_count_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the physical inventory cycle count event.']
  cycle_counter_employee_id varchar [note: 'Employee who performed the physical count.']
  cycle_employee_id varchar [note: 'Employee who performed the physical count.']
  plant_id varchar [note: 'Manufacturing plant where the inventory is stored.']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the master SKU (product) being counted.']
  storage_location_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the physical storage location (warehouse, plant, or depot) where the count took place.']
  abc_classification varchar [note: 'ABC inventory classification driving count frequency.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the count was approved.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Employee who approved the count after review.']
  book_quantity decimal(15,3) [note: 'Quantity recorded in the system (book) prior to the count.']
  compliance_iatf16949_flag boolean [note: 'True if the count complies with IATF 16949 inventory control requirements.']
  compliance_nhtsa_flag boolean [note: 'True if the count satisfies NHTSA traceability obligations.']
  cost_center_code varchar [note: 'Internal cost center associated with the inventory location.']
  count_date date [note: 'Date on which the physical count was performed.']
  count_document_number varchar [note: 'External document number assigned by SAP MM for the inventory count (e.g., MI01/MI07).']
  count_frequency_days varchar [note: 'Number of days between scheduled counts for this SKU/location.']
  count_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the count event.']
  count_type varchar [note: 'Specifies whether the count is a full inventory, partial, or scheduled cycle count.']
  counted_quantity decimal(15,3) [note: 'Quantity physically counted during the inventory event.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when the count record was first created.']
  data_governance_ref varchar [note: 'Reference to data governance catalog entry for lineage and ownership tracking']
  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'Fiscal year in which the count took place.']
  inventory_category varchar [note: 'High‑level classification of the inventory item.']
  is_locked boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the count record is locked from further edits.']
  is_obsolete boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the SKU is marked as obsolete.']
  last_count_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent prior count for this SKU/location.']
  lot_number varchar [note: 'Batch or lot identifier for traceability of the counted material.']
  method varchar [note: 'Technique used to perform the count.']
  model_year varchar [note: 'Model year of the vehicle or component associated with the SKU.']
  next_scheduled_count_date date [note: 'Planned date for the next cycle count based on ABC classification.']
  physical_inventory_doc_type varchar [note: 'SAP document type for the inventory count.']
  posted_by varchar [note: 'Employee who posted the count results.']
  posted_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the count was posted to inventory.']
  recount_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a recount was required after the initial count.']
  recount_number varchar [note: 'Sequential number of the recount attempt, if applicable.']
  remarks varchar [note: 'Free‑text field for additional comments or observations.']
  reorder_point_quantity decimal(15,3) [note: 'Quantity at which a replenishment order should be triggered.']
  safety_stock_quantity decimal(15,3) [note: 'Predefined safety stock level for the SKU at the location.']
  serial_number_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the counted items are serialized (true) or not (false).']
  storage_bin varchar [note: 'Alphanumeric code of the specific bin or shelf within the location.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Measurement unit for quantity fields (e.g., EA for each, KG for kilograms).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp of the most recent modification to the count record.']
  variance_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage variance calculated as (variance_quantity / book_quantity) * 100.']
  variance_quantity decimal(15,3) [note: 'Difference between counted and book quantities (counted - book).']
  variance_reason varchar [note: 'Narrative explanation for any observed variance.']

  Note: 'Physical inventory cycle count event record for a specific SKU at a specific storage location. Aligned with SAP MM physical inventory document (MI01/MI07). Captures count date, counted quantity, book quantity, variance quantity, variance percentage, count status (planned, counted, posted, approved), counter identity, and recount flag. Supports inventory accuracy KPIs, ABC classification-driven count frequency, and IATF 16949 inventory control requirements.'
}

Table inventory.batch_record {
  batch_record_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the batch record.']
  compliance_document_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.compliance_document. Business justification: Batch certification: each production batch must reference the compliance document proving it meets standards.']
  sku_master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.sku_master. Business justification: batch_record describes a material batch; linking to sku_master consolidates material master data.']
  storage_location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.storage_location. Business justification: Normalize batch record location; replace string with FK to storage_location']
  batch_classification varchar [note: 'Category describing the type of batch.']
  batch_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name or description for the batch.']
  batch_number varchar [note: 'External business identifier assigned to the batch by the manufacturer.']
  batch_owner_department varchar [note: 'Internal department responsible for the batch.']
  batch_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the batch.']
  compliance_carb_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates compliance with California Air Resources Board regulations where applicable.']
  compliance_iatf16949_flag boolean [note: 'True if the batch meets IATF 16949 traceability requirements.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the batch record was first created in the system.']
  data_governance_ref varchar [note: 'Reference to data governance catalog entry for lineage and ownership tracking']
  expiration_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the batch relative to its expiry date.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Date after which the batch is no longer usable for production or sale.']
  hazardous_material_flag boolean [note: 'True if the batch contains hazardous substances requiring special handling.']
  last_inspection_user varchar [note: 'User identifier of the employee who performed the latest inspection.']
  lot_traceability_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the batch is required to be traceable for IATF 16949 compliance.']
  manufacturing_date date [note: 'Date on which the batch was produced.']
  material_grade varchar [note: 'Quality grade of the material as defined by internal standards.']
  production_site_code varchar [note: 'Code of the plant or facility where the batch was manufactured.']
  quality_inspection_date date [note: 'Date when the latest quality inspection was performed.']
  quality_inspection_status varchar [note: 'Result of the most recent quality inspection for the batch.']
  quality_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numeric score (0‑100) representing overall quality assessment of the batch.']
  quantity_in_base_units decimal(18,3) [note: 'Total quantity of material in the batch expressed in its base unit of measure.']
  restricted_use_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the batch is subject to restricted‑use regulations (e.g., safety‑critical components).']
  serial_number varchar [note: 'Serial number for high‑value serialized items within the batch (e.g., ECUs, batteries).']
  supplier_lot_reference varchar [note: 'Reference number provided by the supplier for the original lot.']
  tensile_strength_mpa decimal(10,2) [note: 'Measured tensile strength of the material in megapascals.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Unit in which the batch quantity is measured.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the batch record.']

  Note: 'Lot/batch master record for materials managed with batch traceability, including production components, chemicals, raw materials, and serialized high-value parts. Aligned with SAP MM batch master (MCH1/MCHA). Captures batch number, manufacturing date, expiry date, supplier lot reference, quality inspection status, batch classification characteristics (e.g., material grade, tensile strength), and restricted-use flags. Mandatory for IATF 16949 lot traceability and recall readiness.'
}

Table inventory.serialized_unit {
  serialized_unit_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for the serialized unit record.']
  connected_vehicle_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to mobility.connected_vehicle. Business justification: Warranty & service diagnostics need to map each serialized component to the vehicle it is installed on for recall and repair actions.']
  defect_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.defect_code. Business justification: REQUIRED: Warranty analysis of serialized units requires a reference to the official defect definition; defect_code_id provides this link for the Warranty Defect Impact Report.']
  installed_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Installation records need the employee who installed the serialized component for warranty and service history.']
  sku_master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.sku_master. Business justification: serialized_unit belongs to a parent SKU; linking to sku_master removes redundant parent_sku field.']
  storage_location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.storage_location. Business justification: Serialized units are stored in locations; replace location_code with FK to storage_location']
  associated_vin varchar [note: 'VIN of the vehicle where the unit is installed, if applicable.']
  batch_number varchar [note: 'Manufacturing batch identifier.']
  calibration_date date [note: 'Date when the unit was last calibrated.']
  calibration_due_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for next calibration.']
  capacity_ah decimal(8,2) [note: 'Battery capacity in ampere-hours, if applicable.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Status of compliance with applicable regulations (e.g., safety, emissions).']
  component_type varchar [note: 'Category of the component represented by the serialized unit.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the record was created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO currency code for purchase price.']
  data_governance_ref varchar [note: 'Reference to data governance catalog entry for lineage and ownership tracking']
  dimensions_mm varchar [note: 'Physical dimensions (LxWxH) in millimeters.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date after which the component should not be used (e.g., for batteries).']
  firmware_version varchar [note: 'Version of firmware installed on the unit.']
  health_status varchar [note: 'Overall health assessment of the unit based on diagnostics.']
  installation_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the unit is installed in a vehicle or other equipment.']
  is_defective boolean [note: 'Indicates if the unit has been marked as defective.']
  last_service_date date [note: 'Date when the unit last underwent service or maintenance.']
  lot_number varchar [note: 'Batch or lot identifier for traceability.']
  manufacture_date date [note: 'Date the component was manufactured.']
  purchase_price decimal(12,2) [note: 'Cost paid to acquire the component.']
  receipt_date date [note: 'Date the component was received into inventory.']
  safety_certification_code varchar [note: 'Code of safety certification (e.g., NCAP, FMVSS).']
  serial_number varchar [note: 'Manufacturer-assigned unique serial number for the component.']
  serialized_unit_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the serialized unit.']
  supplier_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the supplier that provided the component.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the record.']
  voltage_nominal decimal(6,2) [note: 'Design nominal voltage of the component.']
  warranty_end_date date [note: 'Date when the warranty coverage for the unit expires.']
  warranty_period_months varchar [note: 'Duration of warranty in months.']
  warranty_start_date date [note: 'Date when the warranty coverage for the unit begins.']
  weight_kg decimal(8,2) [note: 'Physical weight of the component.']

  Note: 'Serialized inventory record for high-value, individually tracked components such as ECUs (Electronic Control Units), EV battery packs, ADAS sensor modules, and transmission assemblies. Captures serial number, parent SKU, current stock status, current storage location, installation status (in-stock, installed-in-vehicle, returned, scrapped), VIN association when installed, and warranty start date. Supports end-to-end component traceability from goods receipt through vehicle assembly to field service.'
}

Table inventory.wip_order_stock {
  wip_order_stock_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each WIP order stock line record.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: WIP order stock consumption is charged to a cost center for work‑in‑process cost accounting.']
  production_order_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the production order to which this WIP line belongs.']
  sku_master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.sku_master. Business justification: wip_order_stock references component SKUs; linking to sku_master normalizes component data.']
  storage_location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.storage_location. Business justification: WIP order stock staging location should reference storage_location entity']
  backflush_flag boolean [note: 'True if the component is backflushed automatically at operation completion.']
  batch_number varchar [note: 'Identifier of the manufacturing batch to which the component belongs.']
  component_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name or description of the component.']
  cost_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Standard cost of the component at the time of issue.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the WIP line record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 code of the currency for cost_amount.']
  data_governance_ref varchar [note: 'Reference to data governance catalog entry for lineage and ownership tracking']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date from which the WIP line is considered active.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date after which the WIP line is no longer valid (e.g., component scrapped or re‑allocated).']
  inventory_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the component stock in the WIP area.']
  issued_quantity decimal(15,3) [note: 'Actual quantity of the component that has been issued to the shop floor for this line.']
  line_sequence varchar [note: 'Sequential number of the line within the production order.']
  lot_number varchar [note: 'Batch/lot identifier for traceability of the component.']
  operation_sequence varchar [note: 'Sequence number of the shop‑floor operation where the component is staged or consumed.']
  remarks varchar [note: 'Optional free‑text notes or comments about the WIP line.']
  required_quantity decimal(15,3) [note: 'Quantity of the component that the production order plans to issue for the operation.']
  scrap_quantity decimal(15,3) [note: 'Quantity of the component recorded as scrap for this line.']
  serial_number_flag boolean [note: 'True if the component is tracked at serial‑number level.']
  uom varchar [note: 'Measurement unit for quantities (e.g., each, kilogram).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the most recent modification to the WIP line record.']
  wip_order_stock_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the WIP line.']

  Note: 'Work-in-Progress (WIP) inventory record tracking components and sub-assemblies staged or consumed against a specific production order on the shop floor. Aligned with SAP PP production order component stock (CO11N/COGI). Captures production order reference, operation sequence, component SKU, required quantity, issued quantity, backflush status, scrap quantity, and staging location. Provides real-time WIP visibility for MES integration and production cost accounting.'
}

Table inventory.finished_vehicle_stock {
  finished_vehicle_stock_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for each finished vehicle stock record.']
  assigned_sales_rep_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Dealer sales assignment report tracks which sales rep is responsible for each finished vehicle allocation.']
  connected_vehicle_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to mobility.connected_vehicle. Business justification: Fleet management and OTA update processes require joining inventory vehicle records with connected‑vehicle telemetry for status and compliance reporting.']
  finished_allocation_dealer_dealership_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the dealer to which the vehicle is allocated.']
  finished_dealership_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the dealer to which the vehicle is allocated.']
  homologation_record_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.homologation_record. Business justification: Vehicle approval: link each finished VIN to its homologation record required for market entry.']
  invoice_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to billing.invoice. Business justification: Vehicle sales invoicing requires linking each finished vehicle (VIN) to its sales invoice for reconciliation of inventory and revenue.']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Vehicle profitability analysis attributes each finished vehicle to a profit center for margin reporting.']
  vehicle_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.vehicle_order. Business justification: Allocation report requires linking each finished vehicle to the specific sales order it fulfills, enabling real‑time order fulfillment tracking.']
  vehicle_program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to engineering.vehicle_program. Business justification: Supports dealership allocation and program performance reports linking finished vehicles back to their engineering program.']
  vin_registry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to vehicle.vin_registry. Business justification: Needed for Finished Vehicle Stock report linking each inventory vehicle to its VIN registry for warranty, recall, and regulatory compliance.']
  aging_days varchar [note: 'Number of days the vehicle has been in its current status.']
  allocation_date date [note: 'Date the vehicle was assigned to a dealer.']
  batch_number varchar [note: 'Internal production batch number associated with the vehicle.']
  body_style varchar [note: 'Physical body configuration (e.g., sedan, SUV, truck).']
  color varchar [note: 'Paint color of the vehicle as defined by the manufacturer.']
  current_location_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the compound, yard, warehouse, or dealer where the vehicle is presently stored.']
  data_governance_ref varchar [note: 'Reference to data governance catalog entry for lineage and ownership tracking']
  delivery_date date [note: 'Actual date the vehicle left the plant or compound for the dealer/customer.']
  emission_standard varchar [note: 'Regulatory emission classification (e.g., Euro 6, EPA Tier 3).']
  expected_delivery_date date [note: 'Planned delivery date based on logistics schedule.']
  hold_code varchar [note: 'Code indicating why a vehicle is on hold (e.g., quality, finance).']
  hold_reason varchar [note: 'Free‑text explanation for the hold code.']
  location_type varchar [note: 'Category of the current location.']
  lot_number varchar [note: 'Batch identifier used for traceability of the vehicle batch.']
  model_code varchar [note: 'Manufacturer\'s code representing the vehicle model (e.g., CX5, F150).']
  msrp decimal(12,2) [note: 'Standard retail price set by the manufacturer for the vehicle configuration.']
  plant_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the plant where the vehicle was assembled.']
  powertrain_type varchar [note: 'Primary propulsion technology of the vehicle.']
  production_date date [note: 'Calendar date when the vehicle completed assembly (EOL).']
  production_line varchar [note: 'Specific line or bay within the plant that produced the vehicle.']
  recall_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the vehicle is subject to a safety recall (true) or not (false).']
  record_audit_created timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the stock record was first created in the system.']
  record_audit_updated timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the stock record.']
  stock_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the vehicle within inventory.']
  trim_level varchar [note: 'Specific trim or equipment level of the vehicle (e.g., Sport, Limited).']
  vin varchar [note: 'Globally unique 17‑character identifier assigned to each vehicle.']
  warranty_end_date date [note: 'Date when the vehicle warranty period expires.']
  warranty_start_date date [note: 'Date when the vehicle warranty period begins.']

  Note: 'Finished vehicle inventory record tracking completed vehicles from end-of-line (EOL) through PDI (Pre-Delivery Inspection), compound storage, and dealer allocation. Captures VIN, model/trim/color configuration, plant of manufacture, current compound or yard location, stock status (in-production, PDI-pending, PDI-complete, allocated, in-transit, delivered), hold codes, and aging days. Bridges manufacturing and logistics domains for vehicle order fulfillment.'
}

Table inventory.service_parts_stock {
  service_parts_stock_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the service parts stock record.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Service parts inventory cost is allocated to a cost center for service department expense tracking.']
  issued_to_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Service parts issuance logs the technician receiving parts; required for service cost accounting and warranty traceability.']
  spare_parts_catalog_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to asset.spare_parts_catalog. Business justification: SERVICE PARTS INVENTORY: Tying service parts to the spare‑parts catalog eliminates redundancy and supports warranty and compliance reporting.']
  storage_location_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the warehouse or dealer location where the stock is held.']
  warehouse_id varchar [note: 'FK to inventory.warehouse']
  aisle varchar [note: 'Aisle identifier within the warehouse layout.']
  batch_number varchar [note: 'Identifier for the manufacturing batch of the part.']
  bin_number varchar [note: 'Alphanumeric identifier of the storage bin or pallet location.']
  cost_per_unit decimal(12,2) [note: 'Standard cost of a single unit of the part in the local currency.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 code of the currency for cost values.']
  cycle_count_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the scheduled cycle count for the location.']
  data_governance_ref varchar [note: 'Reference to data governance catalog entry for lineage and ownership tracking']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date after which the part should not be used (e.g., perishable components).']
  inventory_status varchar [note: 'Overall lifecycle status of the stock record.']
  last_cost_update_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent cost update for the part.']
  last_count_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent physical inventory count.']
  last_issue_date date [note: 'Date when the most recent stock issue (dispatch) was recorded.']
  last_receipt_date date [note: 'Date when the most recent stock receipt was recorded.']
  lead_time_days varchar [note: 'Average number of days from order placement to receipt for this part.']
  lot_number varchar [note: 'Batch or lot identifier for traceability of the part.']
  max_stock_level varchar [note: 'Upper bound for stock to avoid over‑stocking.']
  min_stock_level varchar [note: 'Safety stock threshold below which replenishment is triggered.']
  obsolescence_date date [note: 'Effective date when the part becomes obsolete.']
  obsolescence_reason varchar [note: 'Reason for obsolescence (e.g., discontinued, replaced by new model).']
  obsolescence_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the part is active, pending obsolescence, or obsolete.']
  part_revision varchar [note: 'Revision identifier indicating engineering change level of the part.']
  quantity_available varchar [note: 'Units available for allocation to orders (excluding reserved stock).']
  quantity_committed varchar [note: 'Units already committed to pending service orders.']
  quantity_on_hand varchar [note: 'Total number of units physically present in the location.']
  record_audit_created timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the stock record was initially created in the system.']
  record_audit_updated timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the stock record.']
  reorder_point varchar [note: 'Inventory level that triggers a replenishment order.']
  safety_stock varchar [note: 'Buffer quantity kept to protect against demand variability.']
  serial_number_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the part is tracked by individual serial numbers.']
  shelf varchar [note: 'Shelf identifier within the aisle for the part.']
  supersession_part_number varchar [note: 'Part number that supersedes this part in the product lifecycle.']
  valuation_method varchar [note: 'Inventory valuation method applied to the part stock.']
  warranty_expiration_date date [note: 'Date when the part\'s warranty coverage ends.']
  warranty_status varchar [note: 'Current warranty coverage status of the part.']

  Note: 'After-sales service parts inventory record tracking spare parts and accessories across the central parts distribution center (PDC), regional warehouses, and dealer parts rooms. Captures part number, supersession chain, current stock level by location, min/max replenishment levels, fill rate, backorder quantity, and obsolescence classification. Supports dealer parts ordering, warranty repair fulfillment, and TSB (Technical Service Bulletin) parts pre-positioning.'
}

Table inventory.hold {
  hold_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the inventory hold record.']
  initiated_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Hold audit requires knowing which employee initiated the inventory hold; supports compliance and root‑cause analysis.']
  recall_campaign_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.compliance_recall_campaign. Business justification: Recall management: holds placed due to a recall must reference the specific recall campaign.']
  sku_master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.sku_master. Business justification: inventory_hold blocks stock for a SKU; linking to sku_master eliminates duplicate SKU identifier.']
  spare_parts_catalog_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to asset.spare_parts_catalog. Business justification: SPARE‑PART HOLD MANAGEMENT: Linking hold records to catalog items provides cost tracking and regulatory compliance for held spare parts.']
  storage_location_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the warehouse or plant location where the hold applies.']
  supplier_nonconformance_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the regulatory non-conformance case linked to the hold.']
  actual_release_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the hold was actually released.']
  batch_number varchar [note: 'Batch or lot identifier for the inventory affected.']
  comments varchar [note: 'Free-text comments describing the hold context.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the hold record was created in the system.']
  data_governance_ref varchar [note: 'Reference to data governance catalog entry for lineage and ownership tracking']
  disposition_decision varchar [note: 'Action to be taken after hold is released.']
  expected_release_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Planned date and time for hold release.']
  hold_number varchar [note: 'System-generated hold reference number.']
  hold_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the hold.']
  hold_type varchar [note: 'Category of hold indicating reason source.']
  initiating_department varchar [note: 'Department that initiated the hold.']
  is_critical_hold boolean [note: 'Indicates if the hold is critical to production safety.']
  lot_number varchar [note: 'Lot identifier for traceability.']
  quantity_held decimal(15,3) [note: 'Total quantity of items placed on hold.']
  reason_code varchar [note: 'Code representing specific reason for the hold as defined by quality or engineering.']
  related_ecn_number varchar [note: 'Reference to the ECN that triggered the hold, if applicable.']
  related_ppap_status varchar [note: 'Status of the supplier PPAP related to the hold.']
  serial_number_end varchar [note: 'Ending serial number in the range held.']
  serial_number_start varchar [note: 'Starting serial number in the range held (if serialized).']
  source varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the hold was generated automatically by the system or entered manually.']
  start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the hold became effective.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Measurement unit for the held quantity.']
  updated_by varchar [note: 'User identifier who last modified the hold record.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the hold record.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User identifier who created the hold record.']

  Note: 'Inventory hold and block record restricting the use or movement of specific stock due to quality holds, engineering change notices (ECN), regulatory non-conformance, supplier PPAP failures, or recall investigations. Captures hold type, affected SKU/batch/serial range, quantity blocked, hold reason code, initiating department, hold start date, expected release date, and disposition decision (use-as-is, rework, scrap, return-to-supplier). Supports APQP/PPAP compliance and quality containment actions.'
}

Table inventory.replenishment_order {
  replenishment_order_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the replenishment order.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Replenishment orders are budgeted and expensed against a cost center for internal cost control.']
  destination_location_storage_location_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the consuming location (line‑side, assembly station, dealer parts room).']
  primary_replenishment_storage_location_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the supplying storage location (warehouse, supermarket, PDC).']
  procurement_supplier_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the supplier or internal source providing the material.']
  requested_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Replenishment request workflow records the planner who initiates the order; needed for approval routing and audit trails.']
  sku_master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.sku_master. Business justification: replenishment_order requests stock for a SKU; linking to sku_master centralizes SKU attributes.']
  trade_compliance_record_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.trade_compliance_record. Business justification: Import/export compliance: each replenishment order for imported parts must reference its trade compliance record.']
  actual_delivery_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the replenishment was physically received at the destination.']
  batch_number varchar [note: 'Batch identifier for the replenished material.']
  cost_per_unit decimal(12,2) [note: 'Standard cost of a single unit of the SKU at the time of order.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the replenishment order record was first created in the system.']
  data_governance_ref varchar [note: 'Reference to data governance catalog entry for lineage and ownership tracking']
  event_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The moment the replenishment request was generated (e.g., pull signal from the shop floor).']
  fulfillment_status varchar [note: 'Current fulfillment state of the order.']
  is_critical boolean [note: 'Indicates if the SKU is a critical component requiring priority handling.']
  last_modified_by varchar [note: 'User identifier who last modified the replenishment order record.']
  lead_time_days varchar [note: 'Planned number of days from order release to delivery.']
  lot_number varchar [note: 'Lot identifier for batch‑controlled items, if applicable.']
  max_stock_level varchar [note: 'Maximum allowable inventory level for the SKU at the source location.']
  min_stock_level varchar [note: 'Minimum desired inventory level for the SKU at the source location.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or special instructions for the replenishment.']
  order_number varchar [note: 'External reference number assigned to the replenishment order.']
  order_status varchar [note: 'Current processing state of the replenishment order.']
