Supports lead nurturing and conversion tracking.'
}

Table sales.trade_in {
  trade_in_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the trade-in transaction record.']
  cpo_listing_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.cpo_listing. Business justification: A trade-in vehicle that passes CPO inspection becomes a CPO listing. The trade_in table has a cpo_eligible_flag indicating this workflow. Adding cpo_listing_id to trade_in creates the direct traceabil']
  dealership_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the dealership that appraised and accepted the trade-in vehicle.']
  trade_appraiser_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the sales representative or appraiser who evaluated the trade-in vehicle.']
  trade_customer_party_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer who traded in the vehicle.']
  trade_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the sales representative or appraiser who evaluated the trade-in vehicle.']
  trade_party_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer who traded in the vehicle.']
  vehicle_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the associated new vehicle purchase order for which this trade-in was accepted.']
  accepted_date date [note: 'Date when the customer and dealership agreed to the trade-in terms and the trade-in was officially accepted.']
  accident_history_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the trade-in vehicle has a reported accident history (True/False).']
  allowance decimal(15,2) [note: 'Agreed-upon credit amount applied toward the new vehicle purchase, which may differ from appraised value due to negotiation.']
  appraisal_date date [note: 'Date when the trade-in vehicle was appraised and evaluated by the dealership.']
  appraised_value decimal(15,2) [note: 'Fair market value of the trade-in vehicle as determined by the dealership appraiser, before negotiation.']
  body_style varchar [note: 'Body style classification of the trade-in vehicle. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: sedan|coupe|suv|truck|van|wagon|convertible|hatchback — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  condition_grade varchar [note: 'Overall condition assessment of the trade-in vehicle based on physical inspection (excellent, good, fair, poor).']
  cpo_eligible_flag boolean [note: 'Whether trade-in vehicle is eligible for CPO certification']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this trade-in record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary amounts in this trade-in transaction.']
  digital_channel_flag boolean
  disposition_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Actual sale or disposal amount realized when the trade-in vehicle was disposed of, used to calculate trade-in profitability.']
  disposition_date date [note: 'Date when the trade-in vehicle was sold, transferred, or otherwise disposed of by the dealership.']
  disposition_type varchar [note: 'Planned or actual disposition channel for the trade-in vehicle after acceptance (wholesale auction, certified pre-owned program, retail resale, internal fleet, scrap).']
  exterior_color varchar [note: 'Exterior paint color of the trade-in vehicle.']
  exterior_condition varchar [note: 'Assessment of the exterior body condition of the trade-in vehicle (paint, body panels, glass).']
  inspection_completed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a full mechanical and safety inspection was completed on the trade-in vehicle (True/False).']
  inspection_date date [note: 'Date when the detailed inspection of the trade-in vehicle was completed.']
  interior_color varchar [note: 'Interior upholstery color of the trade-in vehicle.']
  interior_condition varchar [note: 'Assessment of the interior condition of the trade-in vehicle (seats, dashboard, carpets, controls).']
  lien_holder varchar [note: 'Name of the financial institution or entity holding a lien on the trade-in vehicle, if applicable.']
  make varchar [note: 'Manufacturer or brand of the trade-in vehicle (e.g., Ford, Toyota, Honda).']
  mechanical_condition varchar [note: 'Assessment of the mechanical and operational condition of the trade-in vehicle (engine, transmission, drivetrain).']
  model varchar [note: 'Model name of the trade-in vehicle (e.g., F-150, Camry, Accord).']
  model_year varchar [note: 'Model year of the trade-in vehicle as designated by the manufacturer.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this trade-in record was last modified or updated.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-form notes and comments regarding the trade-in vehicle, appraisal, condition, or negotiation details.']
  odometer_reading varchar [note: 'Mileage reading on the trade-in vehicle at the time of appraisal, in miles or kilometers as per regional standard.']
  odometer_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the odometer reading (miles or kilometers).']
  outstanding_loan_balance decimal(15,2) [note: 'Remaining loan balance owed on the trade-in vehicle at the time of trade, if applicable.']
  powertrain_type varchar [note: 'Type of powertrain in the trade-in vehicle: ICE (Internal Combustion Engine), HEV (Hybrid Electric Vehicle), PHEV (Plug-in Hybrid Electric Vehicle), BEV (Battery Electric Vehicle), FCEV (Fuel Cell Electric Vehicle).']
  reconditioning_cost decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total cost incurred to recondition the trade-in vehicle for resale (repairs, detailing, inspection).']
  service_records_available boolean [note: 'Indicates whether complete service and maintenance records are available for the trade-in vehicle (True/False).']
  tire_condition varchar [note: 'Assessment of the tire condition and tread depth on the trade-in vehicle.']
  title_status varchar [note: 'Legal title status of the trade-in vehicle (clean, salvage, rebuilt, lemon, flood).']
  trade_in_number varchar [note: 'Business-facing unique transaction number assigned to the trade-in for tracking and reference purposes.']
  transmission_type varchar [note: 'Type of transmission in the trade-in vehicle (manual, automatic, CVT, dual-clutch).']
  trim_level varchar [note: 'Trim or package level of the trade-in vehicle (e.g., LX, EX, Limited, Sport).']
  vin varchar [note: '17-character Vehicle Identification Number of the trade-in vehicle, uniquely identifying the vehicle being traded.']

  Note: 'Record of a customer vehicle trade-in evaluated and accepted as part of a new vehicle purchase transaction. Captures trade-in vehicle details (VIN, make, model, year, mileage, condition grade), appraised value, agreed trade-in allowance, appraisal date, appraising dealer, and disposition (wholesale auction, certified pre-owned, retail resale). Links to the associated vehicle order.'
}

Table sales.delivery_appointment {
  delivery_appointment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the delivery appointment record. Primary key for the delivery appointment entity.']
  dealership_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the dealership or delivery center where the vehicle handover will occur.']
  delivery_customer_party_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer who will take delivery of the vehicle.']
  delivery_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the sales representative or delivery specialist assigned to conduct the vehicle handover and customer orientation.']
  delivery_party_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer who will take delivery of the vehicle.']
  delivery_vehicle_vin_registry_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific vehicle unit being delivered, linked by VIN (Vehicle Identification Number).']
  delivery_vin_registry_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific vehicle unit being delivered, linked by VIN (Vehicle Identification Number).']
  fi_contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.fi_contract. Business justification: Vehicle delivery appointments are the point at which F&I contracts are executed and signed. Adding fi_contract_id to delivery_appointment links the delivery event to the specific F&I contract being fi']
  pdi_technician_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the technician who performed the Pre-Delivery Inspection.']
  primary_delivery_specialist_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the sales representative or delivery specialist assigned to conduct the vehicle handover and customer orientation.']
  rescheduled_from_appointment_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the previous delivery appointment if this appointment is a rescheduled version.']
  service_subscription_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to mobility.service_subscription. Business justification: Delivery appointment must verify and activate the customer\'s subscribed connectivity services; link provides the exact subscription record.']
  shipment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to logistics.shipment. Business justification: Appointment execution requires the shipment tracking number to confirm the correct vehicle is being delivered to the customer.']
  vehicle_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the confirmed vehicle order for which this delivery appointment is scheduled.']
  actual_delivery_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the vehicle was actually handed over to the customer and delivery was completed.']
  appointment_duration_minutes varchar [note: 'Expected duration of the delivery appointment in minutes, including vehicle walkthrough, paperwork, and handover.']
  appointment_number varchar [note: 'Business-facing unique appointment reference number used for customer communication and tracking. Format: DA-YYYYMMDD-sequence.']
  appointment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the delivery appointment. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: scheduled|confirmed|in_progress|completed|cancelled|no_show|rescheduled — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  cancellation_reason varchar [note: 'Reason provided for cancellation if the delivery appointment was cancelled.']
  connected_services_activated boolean [note: 'Indicates whether connected vehicle services and mobile app access were activated during delivery.']
  created_by_user_code varchar [note: 'Reference to the user who created the delivery appointment record.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the delivery appointment record was first created in the system.']
  customer_arrival_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the customer arrived at the delivery location for their appointment.']
  customer_confirmation_status varchar [note: 'Status indicating whether the customer has confirmed their attendance at the scheduled delivery appointment.']
  customer_confirmed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the customer confirmed the delivery appointment.']
  delivery_address_line1 varchar [note: 'Primary street address line for the delivery location if delivery is not at a standard dealership facility.']
  delivery_address_line2 varchar [note: 'Secondary address line (suite, apartment, building) for the delivery location.']
  delivery_city varchar [note: 'City name for the delivery location.']
  delivery_country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO country code for the delivery location.']
  delivery_location_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the delivery location where the vehicle will be handed over to the customer.']
  delivery_postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal or ZIP code for the delivery location.']
  delivery_satisfaction_score varchar [note: 'Customer satisfaction rating for the delivery experience, typically on a scale of 1-10, collected immediately after delivery.']
  delivery_state_province varchar [note: 'State or province code for the delivery location.']
  delivery_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the delivery service level provided to the customer.']
  digital_channel_flag boolean
  digital_owner_manual_sent boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the digital owner manual and vehicle information was sent to the customer.']
  documentation_status varchar [note: 'Status of delivery documentation preparation including title, registration, warranty cards, and owner manuals.']
  field_service_info varchar [note: 'Field service related information, consolidating field service concepts.']
  financing_status varchar [note: 'Status of financing approval and funding for the vehicle purchase if applicable.']
  handover_duration_minutes varchar [note: 'Actual duration of the vehicle handover process from customer arrival to departure.']
  home_delivery_flag boolean [note: 'Whether delivery is to customer home (direct-to-consumer)']
  last_reminder_sent_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the most recent appointment reminder was sent to the customer.']
  modified_by_user_code varchar [note: 'Reference to the user who last modified the delivery appointment record.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the delivery appointment record was last modified.']
  pdi_completed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the Pre-Delivery Inspection was completed and the vehicle was cleared for delivery.']
  pdi_status varchar [note: 'Status of the Pre-Delivery Inspection process ensuring the vehicle meets quality standards before customer handover.']
  reminder_sent_count varchar [note: 'Number of appointment reminder notifications sent to the customer via email, SMS, or phone.']
  scheduled_delivery_date date [note: 'The date on which the vehicle delivery appointment is scheduled to occur.']
  scheduled_delivery_time timestamp [note: 'The precise date and time when the customer is expected to arrive for vehicle handover.']
  special_instructions varchar [note: 'Any special instructions or requirements for the delivery appointment such as accessibility needs, language preferences, or specific requests.']
  trade_in_status varchar [note: 'Status of trade-in vehicle processing if the customer is trading in a vehicle as part of the transaction.']
  vehicle_orientation_completed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the delivery specialist completed the vehicle features and controls orientation with the customer.']
  vehicle_preparation_status varchar [note: 'Status of vehicle preparation activities including cleaning, fueling, accessory installation, and final detailing before delivery.']
  vin varchar [note: '17-character Vehicle Identification Number uniquely identifying the vehicle being delivered. ISO 3779 standard format.']

  Note: 'Scheduled vehicle delivery appointment for a confirmed vehicle order, coordinating the handover of a new vehicle to the customer at a dealer or direct delivery point. Captures scheduled delivery date and time, delivery location, assigned delivery specialist, PDI (Pre-Delivery Inspection) completion status, customer confirmation status, and actual delivery completion timestamp. Triggers post-delivery customer satisfaction follow-up.'
}

Table sales.channel {
  channel_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the sales channel. Primary key.']
  applicable_powertrain_types varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of powertrain types eligible for this channel (e.g., ICE, EV, HEV, PHEV, FCEV). Used for product eligibility and incentive program alignment.']
  applicable_vehicle_types varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of vehicle types that can be sold through this channel (e.g., sedan, SUV, truck, EV, HEV, PHEV, commercial). Used for channel eligibility rules in lead and opportunity management.']
  channel_category varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the channel is direct (OEM-owned, no intermediary), indirect (dealer-mediated or third-party), or hybrid (combination of direct and indirect elements).']
  channel_code varchar [note: 'Short alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the sales channel (e.g., RETAIL_DEALER, DIRECT_CONSUMER, FLEET_COMM, GOVT, EXPORT_CKD, ONLINE_CONFIG, CPO). Used for operational reporting and system integration.']
  channel_description varchar [note: 'Detailed textual description of the sales channel, including its purpose, target customer segments, operational model, and any special characteristics or restrictions.']
  channel_name varchar [note: 'Full descriptive name of the sales channel (e.g., Retail Dealer Network, Direct-to-Consumer, Fleet and Commercial Sales, Government Sales, Export/CKD, Online Configurator, Certified Pre-Owned).']
  channel_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the sales channel: active (currently operational), inactive (temporarily not in use), suspended (on hold pending review), planned (future channel not yet launched), discontinued (permanently retired).']
  channel_type varchar [note: 'High-level classification of the sales channel: retail (traditional dealer), direct (OEM direct-to-consumer), fleet (commercial/fleet sales), government (public sector contracts), export (international/CKD/SKD), online (digital configurator/e-commerce), cpo (certified pre-owned). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: retail|direct|fleet|government|export|online|cpo — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  commission_model varchar [note: 'Type of commission structure applied to this channel: fixed (flat fee per unit), percentage (percent of sale price), tiered (volume-based tiers), hybrid (combination), none (no commission, direct OEM).']
  country_codes varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country codes where this channel operates (e.g., USA, CAN, MEX, DEU, GBR, CHN, JPN). Used for geographic sales attribution and regulatory compliance.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this sales channel record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage.']
  crm_integration_enabled boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this channel is integrated with the CRM system (Salesforce Automotive Cloud) for lead and opportunity tracking (True) or operates independently (False).']
  default_commission_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Default commission rate percentage applied to sales through this channel (e.g., 3.50 for 3.5%). Null if commission model is fixed or none.']
  distribution_channel varchar [note: 'SAP SD distribution channel code associated with this sales channel. Used for order processing, pricing, and logistics routing.']
  division varchar [note: 'SAP SD division code representing the product line or business unit served by this channel (e.g., passenger vehicles, commercial vehicles, parts and accessories).']
  dms_integration_enabled boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this channel is integrated with the Dealer Management System (CDK Global DMS) for inventory and order management (True) or not (False).']
  ecommerce_enabled_flag boolean [note: 'Whether channel supports full e-commerce transactions']
  ecommerce_platform varchar
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when this sales channel was or will be discontinued. Null for currently active channels with no planned end date.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when this sales channel became or will become operational. Used for temporal validity and historical reporting.']
  erp_sales_channel_code varchar [note: 'Corresponding sales channel code in the ERP system (SAP S/4HANA SD module). Used for order-to-cash integration and financial reporting.']
  field_service_info varchar [note: 'Field service related information, consolidating field service concepts.']
  incentive_eligibility varchar [note: 'Indicates whether sales through this channel are eligible for OEM incentive programs: eligible (fully eligible), ineligible (excluded from incentives), conditional (eligible under specific conditions).']
  is_dealer_mediated boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this channel involves a dealer or distributor intermediary (True) or is a direct OEM channel (False). Used for dealer network analytics and commission calculations.']
  is_digital boolean [note: 'Whether this is a digital/online sales channel']
  is_direct_oem boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this channel is a direct OEM-owned channel (True) or an indirect dealer-mediated channel (False). Used for revenue attribution and margin analysis.']
  lead_source_priority varchar [note: 'Numeric priority ranking for lead routing to this channel (1 = highest priority). Used in lead assignment and opportunity allocation logic.']
  market_region varchar [note: 'Geographic market region(s) where this sales channel is applicable (e.g., North America, Europe, Asia-Pacific, Latin America, Middle East Africa). May be a comma-separated list for multi-region channels.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this sales channel record was last modified. Used for audit trail and change tracking.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-form notes or comments about the sales channel, including operational considerations, historical context, or special instructions.']
  owner varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the business unit or individual responsible for managing this sales channel. Used for accountability and escalation.']
  pricing_strategy varchar [note: 'Pricing approach used in this channel: msrp (Manufacturer Suggested Retail Price with dealer negotiation), negotiated (fully negotiated pricing), fixed (no-haggle fixed price), dynamic (algorithm-driven pricing), auction (competitive bidding).']
  sales_organization varchar [note: 'SAP SD sales organization code responsible for this channel. Links to the organizational hierarchy for revenue reporting and territory management.']
  supports_cpo boolean
  supports_financing boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this channel offers financing options (True) or requires cash/external financing (False).']
  supports_fleet_sales boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this channel supports fleet or commercial bulk sales (True) or is limited to individual retail sales (False).']
  supports_leasing boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this channel offers leasing options (True) or does not support leasing (False).']
  supports_online_configuration boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this channel supports online vehicle configuration and ordering (True) or requires in-person interaction (False).']
  supports_online_orders boolean
  supports_subscriptions boolean
  supports_trade_in boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this channel accepts trade-in vehicles as part of the transaction (True) or does not support trade-ins (False).']

  Note: 'Reference master defining the sales channels through which vehicles are sold, including retail dealer, direct-to-consumer, fleet/commercial, government, export/CKD, online configurator, and certified pre-owned channels. Captures channel code, channel name, channel type, applicable market regions, and whether the channel is direct (OEM) or indirect (dealer-mediated). Used for channel attribution across leads, opportunities, and orders.'
}

Table sales.price_adjustment {
  price_adjustment_id decimal(18,2) [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the price adjustment record. Primary key.']
  dealership_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the dealership applying or requesting the price adjustment.']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Price Adjustment Posting: adjustments must be posted to a specific GL account for audit and financial impact tracking.']
  price_customer_party_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer receiving the price adjustment.']
  price_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the manager or executive who authorized this price adjustment.']
  price_party_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer receiving the price adjustment.']
  price_vehicle_vin_registry_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific vehicle configuration or unit to which this price adjustment applies.']
  price_vin_registry_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific vehicle configuration or unit to which this price adjustment applies.']
  primary_price_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the manager or executive who authorized this price adjustment.']
  quote_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the sales quote to which this price adjustment applies.']
  sales_incentive_program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.sales_incentive_program. Business justification: Price adjustments are frequently tied to OEM incentive programs. The current model stores program_code and program_name as denormalized strings on price_adjustment. Adding sales_incentive_program_id n']
  vehicle_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the vehicle order to which this price adjustment applies.']
  adjusted_price decimal(15,2) [note: 'The resulting price after this adjustment is applied. May be further adjusted by subsequent adjustments in the pricing waterfall.']
  adjustment_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'The monetary value of the price adjustment in the transaction currency. Negative values represent discounts or reductions; positive values represent surcharges or premiums (rare but possible for special equipment or expedited delivery).']
  adjustment_category varchar [note: 'High-level grouping of the adjustment type for reporting and analysis purposes. Distinguishes between manufacturer-funded incentives, dealer-funded discounts, and other adjustment sources. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: manufacturer_incentive|dealer_discount|regional_program|customer_loyalty|fleet_commercial|employee_affiliate|promotional_campaign|competitive_response|inventory_management|executive_override — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  adjustment_method varchar [note: 'Method by which the adjustment is calculated: fixed dollar amount, percentage of MSRP or invoice, tiered based on volume or other criteria, or formula-based calculation.']
  adjustment_number varchar [note: 'Business-facing unique reference number for the price adjustment, used for tracking and audit purposes.']
  adjustment_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'The percentage rate of the price adjustment when the adjustment method is percentage-based. Stored as a decimal (e.g., 5.00 for 5%).']
  adjustment_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the price adjustment indicating the program or reason for the non-standard pricing. Includes dealer discretionary discounts, manufacturer-authorized regional allowances, conquest and loyalty programs, fleet volume discounts, employee purchase programs, and other special pricing categories. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: dealer_discount|regional_allowance|conquest_bonus|loyalty_discount|fleet_volume_discount|employee_purchase_program|military_discount|first_responder_discount|college_graduate_discount|lease_subvention|finance_subvention|trade_assistance|competitive_conquest|model_year_clearance|inventory_reduction|executive_authorization — 16 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  applied_date date [note: 'Date on which the price adjustment was actually applied to the quote or order.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date on which the price adjustment was approved by the authorizing manager.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current approval state of the price adjustment request. Tracks the adjustment through the authorization workflow.']
  audit_date date [note: 'Date on which this price adjustment was reviewed during an audit process.']
  audit_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this price adjustment has been flagged for audit review due to unusual amount, authorization level, or other risk factors.']
  audit_outcome varchar [note: 'Result of the audit review for this price adjustment, if audited.']
  authorization_level varchar [note: 'The organizational level required to approve this price adjustment. Higher-value or non-standard adjustments require escalated authorization. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: dealer_manager|regional_manager|zone_director|national_sales_director|vice_president|executive_authorization|automated_program — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  base_price decimal(15,2) [note: 'The original price (MSRP or invoice price) before this adjustment is applied, providing context for the adjustment magnitude.']
  cost_center decimal(18,2) [note: 'Cost center to which the financial impact of this price adjustment is allocated for internal accounting purposes.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this price adjustment record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the adjustment amount (e.g., USD, CAD, EUR, MXN).']
  digital_channel_flag boolean
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date after which this price adjustment is no longer valid. Null for adjustments without expiration.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date from which this price adjustment becomes valid and can be applied to transactions.']
  fiscal_period varchar [note: 'Fiscal period (month or quarter) within the fiscal year when the adjustment was applied.']
  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'Fiscal year in which the price adjustment was applied, used for financial reporting and incentive accrual tracking.']
  funding_source varchar [note: 'Entity responsible for funding the price adjustment. Distinguishes between manufacturer-funded incentives and dealer-funded discounts.']
  gl_account varchar [note: 'General ledger account code to which the price adjustment amount is posted for financial reporting.']
  justification varchar [note: 'Business rationale and supporting explanation for the price adjustment. Provides audit trail and context for non-standard pricing decisions.']
  model_year varchar [note: 'Model year of the vehicle to which this price adjustment applies. Adjustments often vary by model year for inventory clearance purposes.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this price adjustment record was last modified.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional free-text notes or comments related to the price adjustment, providing supplementary context beyond the structured justification field.']
  online_exclusive_flag boolean [note: 'Whether price adjustment is exclusive to online orders']
  region_code varchar [note: 'Geographic sales region code where the adjustment is applicable. Regional allowances and programs vary by market.']
  rejection_date date [note: 'Date on which the price adjustment request was rejected, if applicable.']
  rejection_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation provided by the approving authority for rejecting the price adjustment request.']
  sales_channel varchar [note: 'Distribution channel through which the sale is being made. Different channels may have different adjustment authorization rules and limits. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: retail|fleet|commercial|government|employee|direct|online|auction — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  stacking_allowed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this price adjustment can be combined (stacked) with other adjustments and incentives. Some programs are mutually exclusive.']
  submitting_user_code varchar [note: 'Reference to the sales representative or dealer user who submitted the price adjustment request.']
  territory_code varchar [note: 'Sales territory code within the region where the adjustment is applicable.']
  vin varchar [note: '17-character unique identifier for the vehicle to which this price adjustment applies, if the vehicle has been assigned a VIN at the time of adjustment.']

  Note: 'Record of a price adjustment applied to a vehicle order or quote beyond standard MSRP and published incentive programs. Captures adjustment type (dealer discount, regional allowance, conquest bonus, loyalty discount, fleet volume discount, employee purchase program), adjustment amount or percentage, authorization level, approving manager, and justification. Provides audit trail for non-standard pricing decisions.'
}

Table sales.order_status_event {
  order_status_event_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the order status event record. Primary key.']
  dealership_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the dealership or dealer location that placed or is fulfilling the order.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'The user or system account that triggered the status change. May be a human user ID or a system service account identifier.']
  order_customer_party_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer who placed the order. Enables customer-specific order tracking and communication.']
  order_party_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer who placed the order. Enables customer-specific order tracking and communication.']
  order_vehicle_vin_registry_id varchar [note: 'Internal unique identifier for the vehicle master record associated with this order.']
  order_vin_registry_id varchar [note: 'Internal unique identifier for the vehicle master record associated with this order.']
  vehicle_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the vehicle order that experienced the status change. Links to the parent order entity.']
  actual_delivery_date date [note: 'The actual date when the vehicle was delivered to the dealer or customer. Populated when the order transitions to delivered status.']
  actual_production_completion_date date [note: 'The actual date when the vehicle completed assembly and exited the production line. Populated when the order transitions to built status.']
  actual_production_start_date date [note: 'The actual date when the vehicle entered the production line. Populated when the order transitions to in_production status.']
  carrier_name varchar [note: 'The name of the logistics carrier or transportation provider responsible for shipping the vehicle. Relevant for shipped and in_transit status events.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this order status event record was first created in the data system. Audit field for data lineage and compliance.']
  delay_days varchar [note: 'The number of days the order is delayed relative to the original schedule. Calculated when a status transition occurs later than planned.']
  destination_location varchar [note: 'The destination location where the vehicle is being delivered. Typically the dealership code or customer delivery address reference.']
  digital_channel_flag boolean
  digital_notification_sent_flag boolean [note: 'Whether digital notification was sent to customer for this status change']
  estimated_delivery_date date [note: 'The estimated date when the vehicle is expected to arrive at the dealer or customer location. Updated as the order progresses through logistics.']
  event_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or comments associated with the status change event. Provides additional context or details not captured in structured fields.']
  event_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The precise date and time when the order status transition occurred in the source system. This is the business event time, distinct from audit timestamps.']
  event_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the status event. Distinguishes routine status progressions from exceptions, cancellations, or amendments.']
  exception_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this status event represents an exception or deviation from the normal order fulfillment process. True if exception, False otherwise.']
  exception_reason varchar [note: 'Detailed explanation of the exception or deviation, if exception_flag is True. Examples include production delay, quality hold, parts shortage, logistics delay, customer request.']
  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'The fiscal year in which the status event occurred. Used for financial and operational reporting alignment.']
  model_year varchar [note: 'The model year of the vehicle associated with the order. Used for product lifecycle and sales analytics.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this order status event record was last modified in the data system. Audit field for data lineage and compliance.']
  new_status varchar [note: 'The order status after this transition event. Captures the to-state in the order lifecycle. Enables end-to-end order tracking through the fulfillment pipeline. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: confirmed|scheduled_for_production|in_production|built|quality_released|shipped|in_transit|at_dealer|delivered|cancelled — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  notification_channel varchar [note: 'The communication channel used to notify the customer of the status change. Examples include email, SMS, mobile app push notification, customer portal, phone call.']
  notification_sent_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a customer notification was sent for this status change event. True if notification sent, False otherwise.']
  origin_location varchar [note: 'The location from which the vehicle was shipped. Typically the manufacturing plant or distribution center code.']
  plant_code varchar [note: 'The code of the manufacturing plant or assembly facility where the vehicle is being produced. Relevant for production and post-production status events.']
  previous_status varchar [note: 'The order status immediately before this transition event. Captures the from-state in the order lifecycle. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: placed|confirmed|scheduled_for_production|in_production|built|quality_released|shipped — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  production_line varchar [note: 'The specific production line or assembly line within the plant where the vehicle is being built. Captured during in-production status events.']
  quality_release_date date [note: 'The date when the vehicle passed final quality inspection and was released for shipment. Populated when the order transitions to quality_released status.']
  region varchar [note: 'The sales region or geographic market where the order is being fulfilled. Used for regional performance tracking and logistics planning.']
  responsible_party varchar [note: 'The business unit, department, or role responsible for the status transition. Examples include Production Planning, Manufacturing, Quality Assurance, Logistics, Dealer Network.']
  sales_channel varchar [note: 'The sales channel through which the order was placed. Examples include retail (dealer), fleet sales, commercial vehicle sales, direct-to-consumer, online sales.']
  scheduled_production_date date [note: 'The planned date when the vehicle is scheduled to enter production. Populated when the order transitions to scheduled_for_production status.']
  shipment_date date [note: 'The date when the vehicle was shipped from the manufacturing plant or distribution center. Populated when the order transitions to shipped status.']
  tracking_number varchar [note: 'The tracking number or shipment reference provided by the carrier for end-to-end logistics visibility.']
  triggering_system varchar [note: 'The source system or application that initiated the status change event. Examples include SAP SD (Sales and Distribution), MES (Manufacturing Execution System), TMS (Transportation Management System), WMS (Warehouse Management System), DMS (Dealer Management System), Salesforce Automotive Cloud, or manual entry. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: SAP_SD|MES|WMS|TMS|DMS|SALESFORCE|MANUAL — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  triggering_user_code varchar [note: 'The user or system account that triggered the status change. May be a human user ID or a system service account identifier.']
  vin varchar [note: 'The 17-character Vehicle Identification Number assigned to the vehicle associated with this order. Populated once the vehicle enters production and a VIN is assigned.']

  Note: 'Lifecycle status change event record for a vehicle order, capturing each transition through the order fulfillment pipeline (placed → confirmed → scheduled for production → in production → built → quality released → shipped → in transit → at dealer → delivered). Records event timestamp, previous status, new status, triggering system (SAP SD, MES, logistics), and responsible party. Enables end-to-end order tracking and customer communication.'
}

Table sales.campaign {
  campaign_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for the sales campaign record.']
  campaign_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the sales representative or manager responsible for the campaign.']
  channel_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.channel. Business justification: Sales campaigns are executed through specific sales channels (retail, fleet, digital, etc.). The campaign table has a digital_campaign_flag and marketing_channels string but no structured FK to the ch']
  owner_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the sales representative or manager responsible for the campaign.']
  sales_territory_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.sales_territory. Business justification: Sales campaigns are targeted at specific sales territories. The current model stores territory_code as a denormalized string. Adding sales_territory_id normalizes this reference, enabling territory-ba']
  actual_spent_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total amount actually spent on the campaign to date.']
  actual_spent_currency varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 currency code for the actual spent amount.']
  budget_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Planned monetary budget allocated to the campaign.']
  budget_currency varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 currency code for the budget amount.']
  campaign_code varchar
  campaign_description varchar [note: 'Free‑form text describing the campaign goals, scope, and key messages.']
  campaign_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the campaign used in reporting and UI.']
  campaign_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the campaign.']
  campaign_type varchar [note: 'Category of the campaign indicating its strategic purpose.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the campaign record was first created.']
  dealer_participation_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether dealers are required to participate in the campaign.']
  digital_campaign_flag boolean [note: 'Whether campaign targets digital/online channels']
  digital_channel_flag boolean
  end_date date [note: 'Date when the campaign is scheduled to end.']
  field_service_info varchar [note: 'Field service related information, consolidating field service concepts.']
  kpi_market_share_actual decimal(5,2) [note: 'Observed market‑share percentage change achieved by the campaign.']
  kpi_market_share_target decimal(5,2) [note: 'Target market‑share percentage increase for the nameplate during the campaign period.']
  kpi_sales_volume_actual varchar [note: 'Actual number of vehicles sold attributable to the campaign.']
  kpi_sales_volume_target varchar [note: 'Planned number of vehicles to be sold as a result of the campaign.']
  marketing_channels varchar [note: 'Primary channels used to deliver the campaign messaging.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form field for any supplemental information or remarks.']
  objective varchar [note: 'High‑level business objective the campaign aims to achieve (e.g., increase sales volume, improve market share).']
  priority varchar [note: 'Business priority assigned to the campaign.']
  region_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO code representing the primary geographic region of the campaign.']
  start_date date [note: 'Date when the campaign becomes active.']
  target_customer_segment varchar [note: 'Customer segment the campaign is designed for.']
  target_model_year varchar [note: 'Model year of the vehicle nameplate targeted by the campaign.']
  target_nameplate varchar [note: 'Vehicle nameplate (model family) that the campaign is aimed at.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the campaign record.']

  Note: 'Master record for a structured sales and marketing campaign targeting specific customer segments, nameplates, or market regions to drive vehicle sales. Captures campaign name, campaign type (model launch, clearance, seasonal, conquest, loyalty), target nameplate and model year, target customer segment, campaign period, budget allocation, participating dealers or regions, and campaign objectives. Distinct from incentive programs — campaigns coordinate multi-channel outreach while incentive programs define financial offers.'
}

Table sales.campaign_response {
  campaign_response_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the campaign response record. Primary key.']
  campaign_customer_party_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the existing customer who responded to the campaign, if the respondent is an existing customer.']
  campaign_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the sales or marketing campaign that generated this response.']
  campaign_party_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the existing customer who responded to the campaign, if the respondent is an existing customer.']
  campaign_rep_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the sales representative assigned to follow up on this campaign response.']
  campaign_sales_representative_rep_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the sales representative assigned to follow up on this campaign response.']
  configurator_session_id varchar [note: 'FK to configurator session if response led to configuration']
  dealership_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the dealership assigned to follow up on this campaign response, or the dealership where the response was captured.']
  individual_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the prospect record if the respondent is a new prospect not yet in the customer master.']
  opportunity_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the opportunity record if this response was converted to a sales opportunity.']
  primary_campaign_lead_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the lead record if this response was converted to a lead.']
  city varchar [note: 'City where the respondent is located.']
  conversion_date date [note: 'The date when this campaign response was converted to a lead or opportunity.']
  converted_to_lead boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this campaign response was converted into a lead record (True/False).']
  converted_to_opportunity boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this campaign response was converted into a sales opportunity record (True/False).']
  country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO country code where the respondent is located.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this campaign response record was first created in the system.']
  digital_channel_flag boolean
  field_service_info varchar [note: 'Field service related information, consolidating field service concepts.']
  financing_interest boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the respondent expressed interest in financing options (True/False).']
  model_year_interest varchar [note: 'The model year of the vehicle the respondent expressed interest in.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this campaign response record was last modified or updated.']
  opt_in_email boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the respondent opted in to receive email communications (True/False).']
  opt_in_phone boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the respondent opted in to receive phone call communications (True/False).']
  opt_in_sms boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the respondent opted in to receive SMS communications (True/False).']
  postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal or ZIP code of the respondent\'s location.']
  powertrain_interest varchar [note: 'The powertrain type the respondent expressed interest in: ICE (Internal Combustion Engine), HEV (Hybrid Electric Vehicle), PHEV (Plug-in Hybrid Electric Vehicle), BEV (Battery Electric Vehicle), FCEV (Fuel Cell Electric Vehicle).']
  purchase_timeframe varchar [note: 'The timeframe within which the respondent indicated they plan to purchase a vehicle (e.g., immediate, 1-3 months, 3-6 months, 6-12 months, over 12 months, undecided).']
  region_code varchar [note: 'The sales region code where the campaign response originated or is assigned for follow-up.']
  respondent_email varchar [note: 'Email address of the individual who responded to the campaign.']
  respondent_first_name varchar [note: 'First name of the individual who responded to the campaign.']
  respondent_last_name varchar [note: 'Last name of the individual who responded to the campaign.']
  respondent_phone varchar [note: 'Phone number of the individual who responded to the campaign.']
  response_channel varchar [note: 'The channel through which the prospect or customer responded to the campaign (e.g., email click, web form submission, phone inquiry, dealer visit, mobile app, social media).']
  response_date date [note: 'The calendar date when the prospect or customer responded to the campaign, used for day-level reporting and analytics.']
  response_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or comments captured during the response or follow-up activities.']
  response_number varchar [note: 'Business-facing unique identifier or tracking number for the campaign response, used for reference and reporting purposes.']
  response_score varchar [note: 'Numeric score representing the quality or likelihood of conversion for this campaign response, typically calculated by lead scoring algorithms.']
  response_source varchar [note: 'The specific source or touchpoint that generated the response (e.g., email campaign name, landing page URL, event name, advertisement ID).']
  response_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the campaign response (e.g., new, contacted, qualified, converted to lead or opportunity, disqualified, closed).']
  response_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the prospect or customer responded to the campaign. This is the principal business event timestamp for this transaction.']
  response_type varchar [note: 'The type or nature of the response action taken by the prospect or customer (e.g., inquiry, test drive request, quote request, brochure download, event registration, callback request).']
  state_province varchar [note: 'State or province where the respondent is located.']
  test_drive_requested boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the respondent requested a test drive as part of their response (True/False).']
  trade_in_interest boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the respondent expressed interest in trading in an existing vehicle (True/False).']
  utm_campaign varchar [note: 'UTM campaign tracking parameter captured from the response URL for digital attribution.']
  utm_medium varchar [note: 'UTM medium tracking parameter captured from the response URL for digital attribution.']
  utm_source varchar [note: 'UTM source tracking parameter captured from the response URL for digital attribution.']
  vehicle_interest_category varchar [note: 'The category or body style of vehicle the respondent expressed interest in (e.g., sedan, SUV, truck, van, coupe, convertible, hatchback, wagon, commercial vehicle). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: sedan|suv|truck|van|coupe|convertible|hatchback|wagon|commercial — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  vehicle_model_interest varchar [note: 'The specific vehicle model or nameplate the respondent expressed interest in.']

  Note: 'Record of a prospect or customer response to a sales campaign, capturing the response channel (email click, web form, dealer visit, phone inquiry), response date, responding individual reference, campaign attributed, and whether the response converted to a lead or opportunity. Enables campaign ROI measurement and attribution modeling.'
}

Table sales.competitor_vehicle {
  competitor_vehicle_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the competitor vehicle record in the competitive intelligence catalog.']
  adas_level varchar [note: 'The SAE automation level of the competitor vehicle\'s Advanced Driver Assistance Systems (ADAS) capabilities (Level 0 through Level 5).']
  base_msrp decimal(12,2) [note: 'The manufacturer suggested retail price (MSRP) for the base trim level of the competitor vehicle, used for pricing comparison and competitive positioning.']
  battery_capacity_kwh decimal(6,2) [note: 'The battery pack capacity in kilowatt-hours for electric and plug-in hybrid vehicles. Null for pure ICE vehicles.']
  body_style varchar [note: 'The body style configuration of the competitor vehicle (e.g., sedan, SUV, pickup truck). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: sedan|coupe|hatchback|wagon|suv|crossover|pickup|van|convertible|roadster — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  cargo_volume_cubic_ft decimal(6,2) [note: 'The cargo volume capacity in cubic feet, used for utility and practicality comparison.']
  competitive_positioning varchar [note: 'Classification of how this competitor vehicle positions against our portfolio: direct (head-to-head competitor), indirect (adjacent segment), or emerging (new market entrant).']
  competitor_brand varchar [note: 'The brand name of the competing Original Equipment Manufacturer (OEM) such as Toyota, Ford, BMW, Tesla, etc.']
  competitor_vehicle_status varchar [note: 'The current status of the competitor vehicle in the market: active (currently available), discontinued (no longer produced), upcoming (announced but not yet available), or rumored (unconfirmed future model).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this competitor vehicle record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'The three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the base MSRP (e.g., USD, EUR, CAD).']
  data_source varchar [note: 'The source of the competitive intelligence data (e.g., manufacturer website, automotive press, dealer intelligence, third-party research firm).']
  digital_channel_flag boolean
  discontinuation_date date [note: 'The date when this competitor vehicle model was discontinued or end of production (EOP) was announced. Null if still in production.']
  drivetrain_type varchar [note: 'The drivetrain configuration: FWD (Front-Wheel Drive), RWD (Rear-Wheel Drive), AWD (All-Wheel Drive), or 4WD (Four-Wheel Drive).']
  electric_range_miles varchar [note: 'The all-electric driving range in miles for BEV and PHEV vehicles, based on EPA or WLTP testing standards.']
  engine_displacement_liters decimal(4,2) [note: 'The engine displacement in liters for ICE or hybrid powertrains. Null for pure electric vehicles.']
  field_service_info varchar [note: 'Field service related information, consolidating field service concepts.']
  fuel_economy_city_mpg decimal(5,2) [note: 'The EPA-rated city fuel economy in miles per gallon for ICE and hybrid vehicles.']
  fuel_economy_combined_mpg decimal(5,2) [note: 'The EPA-rated combined fuel economy in miles per gallon for ICE and hybrid vehicles.']
  fuel_economy_highway_mpg decimal(5,2) [note: 'The EPA-rated highway fuel economy in miles per gallon for ICE and hybrid vehicles.']
  horsepower varchar [note: 'The maximum horsepower output of the competitor vehicle powertrain, used for performance comparison.']
  key_features varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of key feature highlights and differentiators of the competitor vehicle used in competitive battle cards and sales coaching (e.g., adaptive cruise control, panoramic sunroof, premium audio system).']
  last_updated_date date [note: 'The date when the competitor vehicle information was last updated by the sales strategy team.']
  launch_date date [note: 'The date when this competitor vehicle model was launched or introduced to the market.']
  market_availability_region varchar [note: 'The geographic regions or markets where this competitor vehicle is available for sale (e.g., North America, Europe, Asia-Pacific).']
  model_name varchar [note: 'The specific model name or nameplate of the competitor vehicle (e.g., Camry, F-150, Model 3, Accord).']
  model_year varchar [note: 'The model year (MY) designation of the competitor vehicle, representing the production year classification used for marketing and regulatory purposes.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this competitor vehicle record was last modified in the system.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes capturing additional competitive intelligence, market insights, or strategic observations about this competitor vehicle.']
  powertrain_type varchar [note: 'The powertrain technology of the competitor vehicle: ICE (Internal Combustion Engine), HEV (Hybrid Electric Vehicle), PHEV (Plug-in Hybrid Electric Vehicle), BEV (Battery Electric Vehicle), or FCEV (Fuel Cell Electric Vehicle).']
  safety_rating_ncap varchar [note: 'The New Car Assessment Programme (NCAP) overall safety rating (e.g., 5-star, 4-star) from Euro NCAP, NHTSA, or other regional testing authority.']
  seating_capacity varchar [note: 'The maximum number of passengers the competitor vehicle can accommodate.']
  subscription_offering_flag boolean [note: 'Whether competitor offers subscription/VaaS model']
  torque_lb_ft varchar [note: 'The maximum torque output in pound-feet, used for performance and capability comparison.']
  towing_capacity_lbs varchar [note: 'The maximum towing capacity in pounds when properly equipped, relevant for trucks and SUVs.']
  transmission_type varchar [note: 'The transmission type and configuration (e.g., 8-speed automatic, CVT, 6-speed manual, single-speed electric).']
  vehicle_segment varchar [note: 'The market segment classification of the competitor vehicle used for competitive positioning analysis. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: compact|midsize|fullsize|luxury|suv|crossover|truck|van|sports|electric — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  win_loss_relevance varchar [note: 'The relevance level of this competitor vehicle in win/loss analysis and opportunity tracking: high (frequently encountered), medium (occasionally encountered), or low (rarely encountered).']

  Note: 'Reference catalog of competitor vehicles tracked for competitive intelligence in the sales process. Captures competitor brand, model name, model year, segment classification, powertrain type, base price, key feature highlights, and win/loss relevance. Used in opportunity management to record competitive situations and support sales rep coaching. Maintained by the sales strategy team.'
}

Table sales.win_loss {
  win_loss_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the win/loss analysis record. Primary key.']
  campaign_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the marketing campaign that generated the lead, if applicable.']
  competitor_vehicle_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.competitor_vehicle. Business justification: Win/loss analysis tracks which competitor vehicle was chosen or competed against. The current model stores competitor_brand, competitor_model, and competitor_model_year as denormalized strings. Adding']
  dealer_incentive_program_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the incentive program that was offered during the opportunity, if applicable.']
  dealership_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the dealership where the opportunity was managed.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the user who reviewed and approved the win/loss analysis.']
  opportunity_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the closed sales opportunity that this win/loss analysis documents.']
  sales_territory_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the sales territory where the opportunity was managed.']
  win_customer_party_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer associated with the opportunity.']
  win_party_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer associated with the opportunity.']
  win_rep_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the sales representative who managed the opportunity and provided the win/loss analysis.']
  win_sales_representative_rep_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the sales representative who managed the opportunity and provided the win/loss analysis.']
  win_vehicle_vin_registry_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the vehicle configuration that was the subject of the opportunity.']
  win_vin_registry_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the vehicle configuration that was the subject of the opportunity.']
  analysis_number boolean [note: 'Business-facing unique identifier for the win/loss analysis record, used for tracking and reporting.']
  analysis_status boolean [note: 'Current status of the win/loss analysis record in the review workflow.']
  competitor_price decimal(18,2) [note: 'Reported selling price of the competitor vehicle, if known. Used for competitive pricing analysis.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the win/loss analysis record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary amounts in this record.']
  customer_decision_factor_availability boolean [note: 'Indicates whether vehicle availability and delivery timing were a significant factor in the customer\'s decision.']
  customer_decision_factor_brand boolean [note: 'Indicates whether brand reputation and loyalty were a significant factor in the customer\'s decision.']
  customer_decision_factor_features boolean [note: 'Indicates whether vehicle features and specifications were a significant factor in the customer\'s decision.']
  customer_decision_factor_financing boolean [note: 'Indicates whether financing terms and options were a significant factor in the customer\'s decision.']
  customer_decision_factor_price decimal(18,2) [note: 'Indicates whether price was a significant factor in the customer\'s decision.']
  customer_decision_factor_service boolean [note: 'Indicates whether after-sales service and dealer support were a significant factor in the customer\'s decision.']
  deal_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total monetary value of the opportunity at the time of closure. For won deals, this is the actual sale amount; for lost deals, this is the estimated value.']
  digital_channel_flag boolean
  fi_factor_flag boolean [note: 'Whether F&I terms were a factor in win/loss decision']
  fiscal_quarter varchar [note: 'Fiscal quarter in which the opportunity outcome was recorded.']
  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'Fiscal year in which the opportunity outcome was recorded, used for financial reporting and performance tracking.']
  lead_source varchar [note: 'Original source of the lead that generated this opportunity, used to evaluate marketing channel effectiveness.']
  model_year varchar [note: 'Model year of the vehicle that was the subject of the opportunity.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the win/loss analysis record was last modified.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional free-form notes and observations about the win/loss outcome, providing supplementary context for strategic analysis.']
  opportunity_type varchar [note: 'Type of sales opportunity: new vehicle purchase, used vehicle purchase, lease, or fleet contract.']
  outcome varchar [note: 'Final outcome of the sales opportunity: won (deal closed successfully), lost (customer chose competitor or declined), no_decision (customer postponed decision indefinitely), cancelled (opportunity withdrawn).']
  outcome_date date [note: 'Date when the final outcome was determined and the opportunity was closed.']
  outcome_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise timestamp when the opportunity outcome was recorded in the system.']
  primary_reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code representing the primary reason for the win or loss outcome. Used for structured analysis and reporting.']
  primary_reason_description varchar [note: 'Detailed explanation of the primary reason for the outcome, providing context beyond the standardized code.']
  region_code varchar [note: 'Geographic region code where the opportunity was managed, used for regional performance analysis.']
  reviewed_by_user_code varchar [note: 'Reference to the user who reviewed and approved the win/loss analysis.']
  reviewed_date date [note: 'Date when the win/loss analysis was reviewed and approved.']
  sales_channel varchar [note: 'Channel through which the opportunity was pursued: retail (individual consumer), fleet (corporate fleet), commercial (business customer), direct (OEM direct sales), online (digital channel).']
  sales_rep_assessment varchar [note: 'Qualitative assessment and commentary from the sales representative regarding the outcome, including insights on customer behavior, competitive dynamics, and lessons learned.']
  secondary_reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code for a contributing secondary reason for the outcome, if applicable.']
  secondary_reason_description varchar [note: 'Detailed explanation of the secondary contributing reason for the outcome.']

  Note: 'Record capturing the outcome analysis of a closed sales opportunity, documenting whether the deal was won or lost, primary reason for outcome, competitor vehicle selected (if lost), customer decision factors, deal value, and sales rep assessment. Provides structured win/loss intelligence to improve sales strategy, product positioning, and incentive program design.'
}

Table sales.contract {
  contract_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the executed sales contract. Primary key for the sales contract entity.']
  compliance_document_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.compliance_document. Business justification: Contracts must attach the specific compliance certification documents required for the vehicle sale.']
  contract_customer_party_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer who is the purchasing party in this sales contract. Links to the customer master data.']
  contract_party_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer who is the purchasing party in this sales contract. Links to the customer master data.']
  contract_rep_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the sales representative who facilitated the contract execution. Links to employee or dealer personnel master data.']
  contract_sales_representative_rep_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the sales representative who facilitated the contract execution. Links to employee or dealer personnel master data.']
  contract_vehicle_vin_registry_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific vehicle being sold under this contract. Links to vehicle master data.']
  contract_vin_registry_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific vehicle being sold under this contract. Links to vehicle master data.']
  dealership_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the dealership that facilitated or executed the sales contract. Null for direct OEM (Original Equipment Manufacturer) sales.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the user or manager who provided final approval for the contract. Null for contracts not requiring approval.']
  fi_contract_id varchar [note: 'FK to associated F&I contract']
  fleet_contract_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the master fleet contract framework if this sales contract is executed under a pre-negotiated fleet agreement. Null for non-fleet transactions.']
  quote_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.quote. Business justification: A sales contract formalizes the terms established in a quote. Adding quote_id to contract creates a direct traceability link from the executed contract back to the originating quote, supporting audit ']
  vehicle_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the vehicle order that preceded this contract. Links the contract to the original order placement.']
  agreed_price decimal(18,2) [note: 'The final negotiated selling price for the vehicle as agreed in the contract, before taxes and fees.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date when the contract received final internal approval from authorized personnel. Null for contracts not requiring approval.']
  approved_by_user_code varchar [note: 'Reference to the user or manager who provided final approval for the contract. Null for contracts not requiring approval.']
  cancellation_date date [note: 'Date when the contract was cancelled or voided. Null for active, completed, or terminated contracts.']
  cancellation_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation for why the contract was cancelled. Includes customer request, financing denial, vehicle unavailability, or other business reasons. Null for non-cancelled contracts.']
  contract_date date [note: 'The date when the sales contract was executed and became legally binding. This is the principal business event timestamp for the contract.']
  contract_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique business identifier for the sales contract. Used for customer communication, legal reference, and cross-system tracking.']
  contract_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the sales contract. Draft indicates initial creation, pending approval awaits authorization, executed means signed by all parties, active indicates in-force, completed means fulfilled, cancelled means voided before execution, terminated means ended after execution. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|pending_approval|executed|active|completed|cancelled|terminated — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  contract_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the sales contract indicating the nature of the transaction. Retail purchase is standard consumer sale, lease is rental agreement, fleet purchase is bulk commercial order, government contract is public sector procurement, commercial sale is business-to-business transaction, demo sale is demonstrator vehicle sale.']
  contracting_party_name varchar [note: 'Legal name of the selling party (OEM or dealership) as it appears on the contract. Used for legal documentation and compliance.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when the contract record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary amounts in this contract.']
  customer_legal_name varchar [note: 'Full legal name of the customer as it appears on the executed contract. May differ from customer master name for legal entities.']
  delivery_date date [note: 'Scheduled or actual date when the vehicle is to be delivered to the customer as specified in the contract.']
  delivery_location varchar [note: 'Physical location where the vehicle will be delivered to the customer. May be dealership address, customer address, or alternate location.']
  digital_channel_flag boolean
  discount_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total discount applied to the MSRP (Manufacturer Suggested Retail Price) to arrive at the agreed price. Includes dealer discounts and negotiated reductions.']
  document_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier or URI to the physical or digital contract document stored in the document management system.']
  down_payment_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Initial payment made by the customer at contract execution. Null for full financing or lease agreements with no down payment.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'The date when the contract terms expire or the agreement concludes. Nullable for indefinite or single-transaction contracts.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'The date when the contract terms become effective and obligations begin. May differ from contract date for future-dated agreements.']
  fees_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total additional fees included in the contract such as documentation fees, registration fees, delivery charges, and dealer administrative fees.']
  field_service_info varchar [note: 'Field service related information, consolidating field service concepts.']
  financing_term_months varchar [note: 'Duration of the financing or lease agreement in months. Null for cash purchases.']
  financing_type varchar [note: 'Method of payment or financing arrangement specified in the contract. Cash is full payment, loan is third-party financing, lease is rental agreement, captive finance is OEM financial services, third party finance is external lender, fleet finance is commercial fleet financing.']
  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'Fiscal year in which the contract was executed. Used for financial reporting and sales performance tracking.']
  incentive_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total manufacturer or dealer incentives applied to this contract. Includes rebates, loyalty bonuses, conquest incentives, and promotional offers.']
  interest_rate decimal(5,4) [note: 'Annual percentage rate (APR) for financed purchases. Expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.0499 for 4.99%). Null for cash purchases and leases.']
  model_year varchar [note: 'Model year of the vehicle being sold. Represents the manufacturer\'s designated year for the vehicle model, which may differ from calendar year.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when the contract record was last modified. Used for audit trail and change tracking.']
  monthly_payment_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Recurring monthly payment amount for financed or leased vehicles. Null for cash purchases.']
  msrp decimal(18,2) [note: 'The manufacturer\'s suggested retail price for the vehicle at the time of contract execution. Used for discount calculation and reporting.']
  region_code varchar [note: 'Geographic region code where the contract was executed. Used for regional sales reporting and compliance tracking.']
  sales_channel varchar [note: 'Distribution channel through which the sale was executed. Dealer retail is traditional dealership, direct OEM is manufacturer direct-to-consumer, fleet sales is commercial bulk, online is digital sales platform, auction is wholesale auction, broker is third-party intermediary.']
  signed_date date [note: 'Date when all parties signed the contract and it became legally binding. May differ from contract date for backdated or future-dated agreements.']
  special_terms varchar [note: 'Free-text field capturing any special conditions, clauses, or negotiated terms specific to this contract that are not captured in standard fields.']
  tax_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total sales tax, value-added tax, or other government-mandated taxes applied to the transaction as specified in the contract.']
  total_contract_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'The total amount payable by the customer including agreed price, taxes, fees, less trade-in value. This is the final contract obligation amount.']
  trade_in_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Agreed value of the customer\'s trade-in vehicle applied as credit toward the purchase. Null if no trade-in is involved.']
  value decimal(18,2)
  vin varchar [note: 'The 17-character unique identifier assigned to the vehicle being sold. Globally unique identifier per ISO 3779 standard.']
  warranty_mileage_limit varchar [note: 'Maximum mileage covered under warranty terms. Expressed in miles or kilometers depending on region. Null if no mileage limit applies.']
  warranty_term_months varchar [note: 'Duration of warranty coverage in months from delivery date. Null if no warranty is provided.']
  warranty_type varchar [note: 'Type of warranty coverage included in the contract. Standard is manufacturer base warranty, extended is purchased additional coverage, certified pre-owned is CPO (Certified Pre-Owned) program warranty, none for as-is sales.']

  Note: 'Executed legal sales contract between the OEM or dealer and the customer formalizing the vehicle purchase or lease agreement. Captures contract type (retail purchase, lease, fleet purchase agreement, government contract), contract date, contracting parties, vehicle reference, agreed terms (price, financing, warranty, delivery), contract status (draft, executed, cancelled, completed), and document reference. Distinct from fleet_contract which is a pre-execution commercial framework; this is the executed legal instrument per transaction.'
}

Table sales.regional_sales_plan {
  regional_sales_plan_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the regional sales plan record.']
  campaign_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.campaign. Business justification: Regional sales plans are executed through structured marketing and sales campaigns. Adding campaign_id to regional_sales_plan links the plan to its primary supporting campaign, enabling plan-to-campai']
  primary_regional_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier for the sales leader or manager responsible for this regional sales plan.']
  regional_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier for the sales leader or manager responsible for this regional sales plan.']
  sales_territory_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.sales_territory. Business justification: Regional sales plans are created for a specific sales territory; linking them via sales_territory_id provides the necessary hierarchy and removes the need to store territory code redundantly.']
  tertiary_regional_created_by_user_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier for the user who created this regional sales plan record.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date when the regional sales plan was formally approved.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current approval status of the regional sales plan in the governance workflow.']
  approved_by_user_code varchar [note: 'Identifier for the executive or authority who approved this regional sales plan.']
  average_msrp decimal(12,2) [note: 'Average MSRP assumed for revenue planning calculations.']
  body_style varchar [note: 'Vehicle body style classification for detailed product mix planning.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code for the primary country within the region.']
  created_by_user_code varchar [note: 'Identifier for the user who created this regional sales plan record.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this regional sales plan record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for all monetary values in this plan.']
  dealer_network_code varchar [note: 'Identifier for the dealer network or distribution channel associated with this regional plan.']
