  finance_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'The total amount financed by the customer under the retail installment contract, including vehicle price, F&I products, taxes, and fees, less down payment and trade-in allowance. Null for cash deals.']
  financing_type varchar [note: 'The method by which the customer is financing the vehicle purchase. \'cash\' = outright purchase; \'retail_finance\' = retail installment contract through a lender; \'lease\' = closed-end consumer lease; \'balloon\' = balloon payment retail contract.']
  fleet_sale boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the vehicle was sold as part of a fleet or commercial account transaction rather than a retail consumer sale. Fleet sales are tracked separately for CAFE compliance, OEM fleet incentive programs, and sales mix reporting.']
  front_end_gross decimal(12,2) [note: 'Gross profit earned on the vehicle sale itself (front-end), calculated as sale price minus dealer invoice cost and pack. Excludes F&I product revenue. Key dealer profitability metric reported in DMS and OEM dealer scorecards.']
  lender_name varchar [note: 'Name of the financial institution (captive finance company, bank, credit union) that approved and funded the retail installment contract or lease. Null for cash deals. Used for lender mix reporting and dealer reserve analysis.']
  loan_term_months varchar [note: 'The contractual term of the retail installment contract expressed in months (e.g., 36, 48, 60, 72, 84). Used for lender mix analysis, payment affordability reporting, and portfolio risk assessment.']
  model_year varchar [note: 'The Model Year (MY) of the vehicle sold, as defined by the OEM and NHTSA. Used for CAFE compliance reporting, incentive program eligibility, and sales mix analytics.']
  monthly_payment decimal(10,2) [note: 'The customer\'s contracted monthly payment amount under the retail installment contract or lease agreement. Used for affordability analysis and customer financial profiling.']
  msrp_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'The Manufacturer Suggested Retail Price (MSRP) of the vehicle as stickered, inclusive of all factory-installed options and destination charges. Expressed in the local currency (USD). Used as the baseline for discount calculation, incentive reporting, and dealer margin analysis.']
  oem_incentive_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total value of OEM-funded customer incentives applied to the deal, including cash-back rebates, conquest bonuses, loyalty bonuses, and special program pricing. Sourced from OEM incentive program data and reconciled against dealer statements.']
  oem_program_code varchar [note: 'OEM-assigned program code identifying the specific incentive or sales program under which the vehicle was sold (e.g., employee pricing, conquest, loyalty, fleet, rental). Used for OEM incentive claim submission and program performance reporting.']
  pdi_completed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the Pre-Delivery Inspection (PDI) was completed and signed off by the dealer technician prior to vehicle delivery to the customer. PDI completion is required for warranty activation and OEM delivery standards compliance.']
  sale_date date [note: 'The calendar date on which the retail vehicle sale was consummated — i.e., the date the buyer signed the retail installment contract or cash purchase agreement. This is the principal business event date used for sales reporting, OEM incentive eligibility, and regulatory compliance.']
  sale_price decimal(12,2) [note: 'The agreed-upon selling price of the vehicle as negotiated between the dealer and the customer, before trade-in allowance, taxes, and fees. This is the gross capitalized cost for lease deals or the selling price for retail finance and cash deals.']
  sales_tax_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'State and local sales tax assessed on the vehicle sale transaction. Calculated based on the taxable selling price and the applicable jurisdiction tax rate. Used for tax remittance reporting and deal cost reconciliation.']
  stock_number varchar [note: 'Dealer-assigned inventory stock number for the vehicle unit at the time of sale. Used for dealer inventory management and reconciliation with CDK Global DMS inventory records.']
  trade_in_allowance decimal(12,2) [note: 'The agreed trade-in value credited to the customer for a vehicle traded in as part of the deal. Reduces the amount financed or cash due. Null if no trade-in vehicle was involved in the transaction.']
  trade_in_payoff_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'The outstanding loan or lease payoff balance on the customer\'s trade-in vehicle at the time of the deal. If payoff exceeds trade-in allowance, the difference (negative equity) is typically rolled into the new deal.']
  trade_in_vin varchar [note: 'The 17-character VIN of the customer\'s trade-in vehicle, if applicable. Used for used vehicle inventory intake, auction disposition tracking, and trade-in equity analysis.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when the retail sale record was last modified in the data platform, reflecting deal amendments, status changes, or corrections sourced from CDK Global DMS. Conforms to ISO 8601 format yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  vehicle_condition varchar [note: 'Condition classification of the vehicle at point of sale. \'new\' indicates a new, untitled vehicle; \'used\' indicates a previously titled vehicle; \'certified_pre_owned\' indicates a manufacturer-certified pre-owned vehicle meeting OEM CPO program standards.']
  warranty_start_date date [note: 'The date on which the OEM new vehicle limited warranty period commences, typically aligned with the delivery_date or sale_date per OEM warranty policy. Used for warranty claim eligibility determination.']

  Note: 'Records the retail sale of a new or used vehicle by a dealership to an end customer. Captures VIN, sale date, sale price, MSRP, discount amount, trade-in details, financing type (cash, retail finance, lease), F&I products sold, salesperson, and deal status. Sourced from CDK Global DMS F&I module. SSOT for dealer-level vehicle sales transactions.'
}

Table dealer.dealer_service_appointment {
  dealer_service_appointment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the dealer_service_appointment data product (auto-inserted pre-linking).']
  aftersales_service_appointment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to aftersales.service_appointment. Business justification: Business process: Dealer DMS schedules service appointments; aftersales processes them. Linking ensures appointment sync for parts, labor, and warranty tracking.']
  connected_vehicle_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to mobility.connected_vehicle. Business justification: Service Appointment Optimization uses real‑time vehicle location from the connected vehicle; required for scheduling and parts readiness.']
  dealership_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to dealer.dealership. Business justification: Dealer service appointments belong to a dealership; add dealership_id FK to link to dealership and remove redundant dealer_id column.']
  maintenance_work_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to asset.maintenance_work_center. Business justification: Service appointments are scheduled against specific service bays (work centers); linking supports the Service Capacity Planning report.']
  party_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.party. Business justification: Service appointment scheduling needs to associate the customer (party) with the appointment for service history and follow‑up.']
  service_advisor_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Service appointments are assigned to a service advisor; required for labor costing and service KPI dashboards.']
  vin_registry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to vehicle.vin_registry. Business justification: Service appointment scheduling must reference the specific vehicle VIN to allocate resources and track service history.']
  appointment_date date [note: 'Date of the service appointment']
  appointment_number varchar
  appointment_status varchar [note: 'Status (scheduled, confirmed, in-progress, completed, cancelled, no-show)']
  appointment_time timestamp [note: 'Scheduled time of appointment']
  booking_channel varchar
  check_in_timestamp timestamp
  checked_in_at timestamp
  checked_in_timestamp timestamp
  completed_at timestamp
  created_timestamp timestamp
  customer_concern varchar [note: 'Customer-reported concern or issue']
  customer_concern_description varchar
  customer_name varchar [note: 'Name of the customer']
  customer_phone varchar [note: 'Customer phone number']
  drop_off_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when vehicle was dropped off']
  estimated_cost decimal(18,2)
  estimated_cost_amount decimal(18,2)
  estimated_duration_hours decimal(4,2) [note: 'Estimated service duration in hours']
  estimated_duration_minutes varchar
  estimated_labor_hours decimal(6,2)
  home_service_flag boolean [note: 'Whether this appointment is for mobile/home service delivery.']
  loaner_requested_flag boolean
  loaner_required_flag boolean [note: 'Whether a loaner vehicle is needed']
  mileage_at_service varchar [note: 'Vehicle mileage at time of service']
  mileage_in varchar
  odometer_reading varchar
  pickup_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when vehicle was picked up']
  scheduled_at timestamp
  scheduled_end_timestamp timestamp
  scheduled_start_timestamp timestamp
  scheduled_timestamp timestamp
  service_type varchar [note: 'Type of service (maintenance, repair, recall, warranty)']
  updated_timestamp timestamp

  Note: 'Scheduled service appointments at a dealership for vehicle maintenance, warranty repair, recall service, or customer-pay work. Tracks appointment date/time, customer, VIN, service type, advisor assigned, estimated duration, appointment status (scheduled, checked-in, in-progress, completed, no-show), and transportation option (loaner, shuttle, wait). Sourced from CDK Global DMS service scheduling module. This product is the single source of truth (SSOT) for its concept in the ECM Lakehouse.'
}

Table dealer.dealer_repair_order {
  dealer_repair_order_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the dealer_repair_order data product (auto-inserted pre-linking).']
  aftersales_repair_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to aftersales.repair_order. Business justification: Process: Dealer creates a repair order in DMS; it must reference the central aftersales repair order for warranty validation and labor costing.']
  connected_vehicle_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to mobility.connected_vehicle. Business justification: Repair Order Diagnostic Integration pulls live diagnostic data from the connected vehicle to streamline repair workflow.']
  maintenance_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to asset.maintenance_order. Business justification: Dealer repair orders are executed using the plant\'s maintenance order system, enabling unified cost, warranty, and downtime analysis.']
  party_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.party. Business justification: Repair orders must be linked to the owning customer for warranty claim processing and regulatory reporting.']
  technician_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Repair orders must record the technician performing work for warranty compliance and labor allocation.']
  vin_registry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to vehicle.vin_registry. Business justification: Repair orders must be tied to the VIN registry to manage warranty repairs and parts usage per vehicle.']
  cause_description varchar [note: 'Diagnosed cause']
  close_date date [note: 'Date repair order was closed']
  closed_at timestamp
  closed_timestamp timestamp
  complaint_description varchar [note: 'Customer complaint description']
  correction_description varchar [note: 'Correction performed']
  created_timestamp timestamp
  labor_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total labor charges']
  labor_hours decimal(9,2)
  mileage varchar [note: 'Vehicle mileage at time of repair']
  open_date date [note: 'Date repair order was opened']
  opened_at timestamp
  opened_timestamp timestamp
  parts_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total parts charges']
  payment_type varchar [note: 'Payment type (customer pay, warranty, internal, insurance)']
  pickup_delivery_flag boolean [note: 'Whether vehicle pickup and delivery service is included.']
  repair_description varchar
  repair_order_number varchar
  repair_order_status varchar
  repair_status varchar
  ro_number varchar [note: 'Repair order number']
  ro_status varchar [note: 'Status (open, in-progress, parts-on-order, completed, invoiced)']
  total_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total repair order amount']
  total_labor_amount decimal(18,2)
  total_parts_amount decimal(18,2)
  updated_timestamp timestamp
  warranty_flag boolean

  Note: 'Detailed repair order (RO) record for each vehicle service event at a dealership. Captures RO number, open/close dates, VIN, mileage-in, complaint/cause/correction (3C), labor operations, technician assignments, parts consumed, warranty vs customer-pay vs internal split, total labor hours, total parts cost, and RO status. Core operational record for dealer service operations sourced from CDK Global DMS. This product is the single source of truth (SSOT) for its concept in the ECM Lakehouse.'
}

Table dealer.dealer_incentive_program {
  dealer_incentive_program_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the OEM-sponsored dealer incentive program record in the Databricks Silver Layer.']
  announcement_date date [note: 'Date on which the incentive program was formally announced to the dealer network via the dealer portal or official OEM communications. Precedes effective_start_date to allow dealer preparation.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or employee ID of the OEM finance or sales leadership authority who formally approved this incentive program. Required for SOX compliance and internal audit purposes.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the incentive program was formally approved by OEM finance and sales leadership, transitioning from draft to active status. Required for SOX compliance audit trail.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the incentive program record was first created in the OEM system of record (Salesforce Automotive Cloud or SAP S/4HANA). Formatted as yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX per enterprise data standards.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for all monetary amounts in this incentive program (e.g., USD, CAD, EUR, MXN). Supports multi-market OEM operations.']
  dealer_tier_eligibility varchar [note: 'Specifies which dealer performance tier classification is eligible to participate in this program. Platinum/Gold/Silver/Bronze tiers are OEM-assigned based on prior-year sales volume and CSI scores. \'All\' indicates no tier restriction.']
  dealer_type_eligibility varchar [note: 'Restricts program participation to specific dealer ownership models: franchise dealers, OEM-owned stores, or independent dealers. \'All\' applies to both OEM-owned and independent franchise dealer models.']
  dms_program_reference varchar [note: 'The corresponding program identifier as recorded in CDK Global DMS used by dealer locations. Enables reconciliation between OEM master incentive records and dealer-side DMS reporting.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the incentive program measurement period closes and no further dealer performance accrual is accepted. Nullable for open-ended programs.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the incentive program becomes active and dealers begin accruing credit toward performance tiers. Aligns with the measurement period start.']
  eligible_dealer_count varchar [note: 'Number of dealer locations determined to be eligible for participation in this incentive program at the time of program launch. Used for budget planning and program reach analysis by OEM sales operations.']
  eligible_nameplates varchar [note: 'Pipe-delimited list of vehicle nameplates (brand/model lines) eligible for this incentive program (e.g., \'F-150|Mustang Mach-E|Explorer\'). Null if program applies to all nameplates. Managed in Salesforce Automotive Cloud vehicle catalog.']
  eligible_powertrain_types varchar [note: 'Powertrain technology types eligible for this program. ICE (Internal Combustion Engine), EV (Electric Vehicle), HEV (Hybrid Electric Vehicle), PHEV (Plug-in Hybrid Electric Vehicle), or all. Critical for EV adoption incentive programs aligned with CAFE and EPA compliance targets.']
  floor_plan_rate_pct decimal(7,4) [note: 'For floor plan assistance programs, the percentage of dealer inventory financing cost subsidized by the OEM. Expressed as an annual percentage rate (APR) subsidy. Null for non-floor-plan program types.']
  home_delivery_bonus_flag boolean [note: 'Whether this incentive program includes home delivery bonuses.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the incentive program record in the source system. Used for incremental data pipeline processing and audit trail in the Databricks Silver Layer.']
  max_payout_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The maximum total monetary payout a single dealer can earn under this incentive program during the measurement period. Acts as a cap to control OEM financial exposure. Null if no cap is defined.']
  measurement_period_type varchar [note: 'Defines the cadence over which dealer performance is measured for incentive qualification. Monthly and quarterly are most common for volume bonuses; annual for loyalty programs; custom for special campaigns.']
  min_dealer_tenure_months varchar [note: 'Minimum number of months a dealer must have been an active franchisee to be eligible for this program. Ensures new dealers meet a baseline operational maturity requirement.']
  model_year varchar [note: 'The vehicle Model Year (MY) to which this incentive program applies. Restricts eligible vehicles to a specific MY production cycle (e.g., MY2024, MY2025). Null if program applies across all model years.']
  oem_program_sponsor varchar [note: 'Name or organizational unit of the OEM department sponsoring and funding this incentive program (e.g., \'North America Sales Operations\', \'EV Business Unit\', \'Truck Division\'). Used for internal cost allocation.']
  payout_date date [note: 'Scheduled calendar date on which OEM processes and disburses earned incentive payments to qualifying dealers. May differ from effective_end_date due to reconciliation periods.']
  payout_structure_type varchar [note: 'Defines how the incentive payout is calculated. Flat bonus: fixed dollar amount per tier. Per unit: dollar amount multiplied by units sold. Percentage of MSRP (Manufacturer Suggested Retail Price): payout as a percentage of vehicle MSRP. Percentage of invoice: payout as a percentage of dealer invoice price.']
  performance_metric varchar [note: 'The primary KPI used to measure dealer performance for this incentive program. Units sold for volume bonuses; NPS (Net Promoter Score) or CSAT score for satisfaction bonuses; EV units sold for EV adoption programs; PDI (Pre-Delivery Inspection) compliance rate for quality programs. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: units_sold|nps_score|csat_score|ev_units_sold|market_share_pct|pdi_compliance_rate|revenue|floor_plan_days — promote to reference product]']
  program_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric code assigned by OEM regional sales operations to uniquely identify the incentive program across systems including Salesforce Automotive Cloud and CDK Global DMS.']
  program_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative description of the program objectives, eligibility rules, and payout mechanics as communicated to the dealer network. Sourced from OEM regional sales operations documentation.']
  program_manager_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the OEM regional sales operations manager responsible for administering this incentive program. Used for dealer escalation and program governance.']
  program_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the dealer incentive program (e.g., \'Q3 2024 EV Adoption Bonus\', \'Stair-Step Volume Bonus – Truck MY2024\'). Used in dealer communications and DMS reporting.']
  program_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the incentive program. Draft: under design by OEM regional sales ops. Active: open for dealer participation and accrual. Suspended: temporarily paused. Closed: measurement period ended, payouts being processed. Cancelled: terminated before completion.']
  program_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the incentive program structure. Volume bonus rewards unit sales thresholds; stair-step provides escalating payouts per tier; CSAT bonus rewards customer satisfaction scores; EV adoption incentivizes electric vehicle sales; floor plan assistance subsidizes dealer inventory financing costs; market representation rewards geographic coverage. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: volume_bonus|stair_step|csat_bonus|ev_adoption|floor_plan_assistance|market_representation|loyalty_retention|conquest — promote to reference product]']
  region_code varchar [note: 'OEM-defined regional sales territory code identifying the geographic scope of the incentive program (e.g., \'NE\', \'SE\', \'MW\', \'SW\', \'W\', \'NATIONAL\'). Managed by OEM regional sales operations.']
  requires_pdi_compliance boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the dealer must maintain a minimum PDI (Pre-Delivery Inspection) compliance rate as a prerequisite for incentive eligibility. True enforces PDI compliance gating; False means PDI compliance is not a condition.']
  requires_training_certification boolean [note: 'Indicates whether dealer sales or service staff must hold current OEM-mandated training certifications (e.g., EV product training, ADAS certification) as an eligibility prerequisite for this program.']
  retroactive_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the stair-step payout structure applies retroactively to all units sold once a higher tier is achieved (True), or only incrementally to units sold above each threshold (False). Critical for stair-step program financial modeling.']
  salesforce_program_reference varchar [note: 'The Salesforce Automotive Cloud record ID for this incentive program. Enables bi-directional synchronization between the Databricks Silver Layer and Salesforce CRM for dealer portal visibility.']
  stackable_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this incentive program can be combined (stacked) with other concurrent OEM incentive programs for the same dealer and vehicle. True allows stacking; False means the program is mutually exclusive with other programs.']
  terms_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the program terms and conditions document (e.g., \'v1.0\', \'v2.1\'). Tracks amendments to program rules and ensures dealers are evaluated against the correct version of terms.']
  tier1_payout_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary payout amount (in program currency) awarded to dealers achieving Tier 1 performance threshold. May represent a flat bonus or per-unit amount depending on payout_structure_type.']
  tier1_threshold decimal(18,4) [note: 'Minimum performance metric value a dealer must achieve to qualify for Tier 1 (entry-level) payout. Units depend on performance_metric (e.g., number of vehicles sold, NPS score points).']
  tier2_payout_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary payout amount (in program currency) awarded to dealers achieving Tier 2 performance threshold. Null if program has fewer than 2 tiers.']
  tier2_threshold decimal(18,4) [note: 'Minimum performance metric value a dealer must achieve to qualify for Tier 2 payout. Must be greater than tier1_threshold. Null if program has fewer than 2 tiers.']
  tier3_payout_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary payout amount (in program currency) awarded to dealers achieving Tier 3 performance threshold. Null if program has fewer than 3 tiers.']
  tier3_threshold decimal(18,4) [note: 'Minimum performance metric value a dealer must achieve to qualify for Tier 3 payout. Must be greater than tier2_threshold. Null if program has fewer than 3 tiers.']
  tier_count varchar [note: 'Number of performance tiers defined within this incentive program (e.g., 3 for Bronze/Silver/Gold, 5 for stair-step programs). Determines the granularity of the payout structure.']
  total_program_budget decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total OEM-allocated budget for this incentive program across all participating dealers. Used by OEM regional sales operations for financial planning and CapEx/OpEx tracking in SAP S/4HANA CO.']

  Note: 'OEM-sponsored dealer incentive and bonus programs including volume bonuses, stair-step programs, customer satisfaction bonuses, EV adoption incentives, and floor plan assistance. Defines program name, type, eligible nameplates, MY, performance tiers, payout structure, measurement period, and eligibility criteria. Managed by OEM regional sales operations.'
}

Table dealer.dealer_incentive_claim {
  dealer_incentive_claim_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for each dealer incentive claim record. Primary key for the incentive_claim data product.']
  dealer_incentive_program_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the OEM incentive program against which this claim is submitted. Links to the incentive program master record.']
  primary_dealer_dealership_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the dealer submitting this incentive claim. Links to the dealer master record in the dealer domain.']
  reviewer_employee_id varchar [note: 'Employee identifier of the OEM staff member who reviewed and approved or rejected the claim. Supports audit trail, segregation of duties compliance, and workload reporting for the dealer incentive operations team.']
  accrual_posted boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a financial accrual has been posted in SAP FI for this incentive claim liability. True = accrual posted; False = accrual not yet posted. Used for period-end financial close management and EBITDA reporting accuracy.']
  adjustment_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Net monetary adjustment applied by the OEM during claim review, representing the difference between claimed_amount and approved_amount. A negative value indicates a reduction; positive indicates an upward correction. Supports audit trail and dispute resolution.']
  adjustment_reason varchar [note: 'Reason code explaining why the approved_amount differs from the claimed_amount. Populated by the OEM reviewer when an adjustment is applied. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: ineligible_units|duplicate_claim|missing_evidence|calculation_error|program_cap_exceeded|other — promote to reference product]']
  approved_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary amount approved by the OEM after review and validation of the dealer\'s claim. May differ from claimed_amount due to adjustments, disallowed units, or partial approvals. This is the amount committed for payment processing in SAP FI.']
  approved_units varchar [note: 'Number of vehicle units or qualifying transactions validated and approved by the OEM reviewer. May be less than claimed_units if some units are found ineligible during audit. Used to calculate the approved_amount for volume-based programs.']
  claim_number varchar [note: 'Externally visible, human-readable unique claim reference number assigned at submission. Used by dealers and OEM finance teams to track and reference the claim across systems including CDK Global DMS and SAP FI.']
  claim_period_end_date date [note: 'End date of the performance or sales period covered by this incentive claim. Defines the close of the measurement window. Together with claim_period_start_date, establishes the claim period boundary for audit and reconciliation.']
  claim_period_start_date date [note: 'Start date of the performance or sales period covered by this incentive claim. Defines the beginning of the measurement window for which the dealer is claiming the incentive.']
  claim_status varchar [note: 'Current workflow state of the incentive claim through the OEM review and payment lifecycle. Valid states: submitted (initial dealer submission), under_review (OEM audit in progress), approved (claim validated and approved for payment), rejected (claim denied), paid (payment disbursed via SAP FI), cancelled (withdrawn by dealer or voided by OEM).']
  claim_type varchar [note: 'Category of incentive claim being submitted, reflecting the nature of the OEM incentive program. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: volume_bonus|retail_incentive|floor_plan|marketing_support|customer_satisfaction|parts_sales|service_absorption — promote to reference product]']
  claimed_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Gross monetary amount claimed by the dealer against the incentive program, expressed in the transaction currency. This is the dealer-submitted figure prior to OEM review and approval. Used for financial accrual and liability estimation.']
  claimed_metric_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the claimed_metric_value field, indicating how the performance metric is expressed (e.g., units for vehicle count, percentage for service absorption rate, score for NPS/CSI, amount for revenue-based claims).']
  claimed_metric_value decimal(18,4) [note: 'The quantitative performance metric value being claimed by the dealer, applicable for non-unit-based programs such as customer satisfaction scores (NPS), service absorption rates, or parts sales amounts. Complements claimed_units for metric-driven incentive types.']
  claimed_units varchar [note: 'Number of vehicle units or qualifying transactions the dealer is claiming against the incentive program for the claim period. Applicable for volume-based incentive programs such as volume bonus and retail incentive programs.']
  cost_center_code varchar [note: 'SAP CO cost center code to which the incentive payout is charged. Used for internal cost allocation and management accounting of dealer incentive expenditure by brand, region, or program type.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the market in which the dealer operates and the claim is filed. Supports multi-market OEM operations and ensures correct regulatory and tax treatment of incentive payouts.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the incentive claim record was first created in the system. Represents the audit creation time (distinct from submission_date which is the business event date). Used for data lineage, audit trail, and Silver layer ingestion tracking.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for all monetary amounts on this claim (claimed_amount, approved_amount, paid_amount). Supports multi-currency dealer networks operating in different markets.']
  dealer_contact_email varchar [note: 'Email address of the dealer representative responsible for this claim. Used for claim status notifications, evidence requests, and dispute correspondence. Classified as confidential PII per GDPR and applicable privacy regulations.']
  dealer_contact_name varchar [note: 'Name of the dealer-side representative who submitted or is responsible for this incentive claim. Used for communication, follow-up, and audit correspondence between OEM and dealer.']
  delivery_type varchar [note: 'Type of delivery associated with this incentive claim.']
  dispute_date date [note: 'Date on which the dealer formally raised a dispute against the OEM\'s claim decision. Populated only when dispute_flag is True. Used to track dispute aging and compliance with contractual dispute resolution timelines.']
  dispute_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the dealer has formally disputed the OEM\'s decision on this claim (e.g., disputed rejection or adjusted amount). True = claim is under dispute; False = no dispute raised. Triggers escalation workflow in Salesforce Automotive Cloud.']
  dispute_resolution_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes documenting the outcome and resolution of a dealer dispute on this claim. Captures the agreed resolution, any revised approved amount, and the basis for the final decision. Supports audit trail and dealer relationship management.']
  dms_claim_reference varchar [note: 'Claim reference number as recorded in the dealer\'s CDK Global DMS system. Enables cross-system reconciliation between the OEM\'s incentive claim record and the dealer\'s own DMS records. Critical for audit and dispute resolution.']
  evidence_document_ref varchar [note: 'Reference identifier or URL pointing to the supporting evidence package submitted by the dealer with the claim (e.g., sales invoices, PDI records, customer satisfaction survey results, parts purchase orders). Stored in the document management system linked from CDK Global DMS or Salesforce Automotive Cloud.']
  evidence_verified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the OEM reviewer has verified the supporting evidence submitted with the claim. True = evidence reviewed and confirmed valid; False = evidence not yet verified or found insufficient. Used to gate claim approval workflow.']
  gl_account_code varchar [note: 'SAP FI general ledger account code used to classify the incentive payout for financial reporting. Ensures correct P&L treatment of dealer incentive costs in accordance with IFRS 15 and OEM chart of accounts.']
  model_year varchar [note: 'The vehicle model year (MY) associated with the units or transactions being claimed. Used to align incentive claims with specific model year programs and OEM production cycles (e.g., MY2024, MY2025).']
  paid_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Actual monetary amount disbursed to the dealer for this claim. May differ from approved_amount due to netting, offsets, or partial payment arrangements. Populated upon payment confirmation from SAP FI.']
  payment_date date [note: 'Date on which the approved incentive amount was disbursed to the dealer\'s bank account. Populated upon payment confirmation from SAP FI. Used for cash flow reporting and dealer payment aging analysis.']
  payment_reference varchar [note: 'SAP FI payment document number or bank transfer reference assigned when the approved claim is paid. Links the incentive claim to the corresponding financial transaction in SAP FI Accounts Payable for reconciliation and audit.']
  powertrain_type varchar [note: 'Powertrain technology category of the vehicles covered by this claim. Values: ICE (Internal Combustion Engine), HEV (Hybrid Electric Vehicle), PHEV (Plug-in Hybrid Electric Vehicle), EV (Electric Vehicle), FCEV (Fuel Cell Electric Vehicle). Used to track EV/electrification incentive program uptake and CAFE compliance reporting.']
  program_quarter varchar [note: 'Fiscal quarter of the OEM incentive program period covered by this claim (Q1–Q4). Used for quarterly accrual reporting, dealer performance benchmarking, and incentive budget variance analysis.']
  program_year varchar [note: 'Fiscal or calendar year of the OEM incentive program under which this claim is filed. Used to segregate claims by annual program cycle for budget tracking, accrual management, and year-end financial close in SAP FI.']
  region_code varchar [note: 'OEM sales region or zone code to which the submitting dealer belongs. Used for regional incentive program management, territory-based performance benchmarking, and regional finance reporting.']
  rejection_reason varchar [note: 'Free-text or coded explanation provided by the OEM reviewer when a claim is rejected (claim_status = rejected). Documents the specific grounds for denial to support dealer dispute resolution and compliance with IATF 16949 dealer management requirements.']
  review_date date [note: 'Date on which the OEM completed the review of the incentive claim and updated the claim_status to approved or rejected. Used for SLA tracking of claim processing turnaround time.']
  sap_fi_document_number varchar [note: 'SAP FI accounting document number generated when the incentive claim liability or payment is posted in the general ledger. Provides direct traceability between the incentive claim and the SAP FI financial posting for audit and reconciliation purposes.']
  submission_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the dealer formally submitted the incentive claim to the OEM. This is the principal business event date for the claim lifecycle and is used for period-end cut-off and aging analysis.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the incentive claim record. Tracks the last modification for change data capture (CDC), audit trail, and Silver layer incremental processing in the Databricks Lakehouse.']
  vehicle_line_code varchar [note: 'OEM product line or nameplate code identifying the vehicle model family associated with this claim (e.g., F-150, Silverado, Model 3). Used to segment incentive claims by vehicle line for program performance analysis and brand-level reporting.']

  Note: 'Individual dealer claims submitted against OEM incentive programs. Tracks claim submission date, program, claim period, claimed units or metric, supporting evidence, claim status (submitted, under review, approved, rejected, paid), approved amount, and payment reference. Integrates with SAP FI for payout processing.'
}

Table dealer.performance_scorecard {
  performance_scorecard_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the dealer performance scorecard record.']
  performance_dealer_dealership_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the dealer being evaluated in this scorecard.']
  performance_dealership_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the dealer being evaluated in this scorecard.']
  comments varchar [note: 'Additional notes or commentary from the OEM regarding the dealer\'s performance during this period.']
  composite_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'The overall weighted composite performance score combining all KPI categories (sales, service, parts, customer satisfaction, facility standards) used for franchise compliance and incentive eligibility.']
  composite_score_benchmark decimal(5,2) [note: 'The minimum composite score required for franchise compliance and incentive program eligibility.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this scorecard record was first created in the system.']
  csi_benchmark decimal(5,2) [note: 'The target or industry benchmark CSI score the dealer is expected to meet or exceed.']
  csi_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'The Customer Satisfaction Index score measuring overall customer satisfaction with sales and service experience, typically on a scale of 0-100 or 0-1000 depending on OEM methodology.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'The three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary amounts in this scorecard.']
  days_supply varchar [note: 'The average number of days of inventory on hand at the dealer based on current sales rate.']
  delivery_satisfaction_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Customer satisfaction score for delivery operations.']
  dms_integration_source varchar [note: 'The Dealer Management System from which raw performance data was extracted for this scorecard.']
  facility_standards_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'The score measuring dealer compliance with OEM facility image and standards requirements (showroom, service bays, signage, etc.).']
  fiscal_quarter varchar [note: 'The fiscal quarter (1-4) to which this scorecard period belongs, if applicable.']
  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'The fiscal year to which this scorecard period belongs.']
  franchise_compliance_status varchar [note: 'The dealer\'s franchise agreement compliance status based on this scorecard\'s performance results.']
  incentive_eligibility_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the dealer met the minimum performance thresholds to qualify for OEM incentive programs during this scorecard period.']
  inventory_turn_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'The number of times the dealer\'s new vehicle inventory was sold and replaced during the scorecard period, calculated as sales divided by average inventory.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this scorecard record was last updated.']
  market_share_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'The dealer\'s share of total new vehicle sales in their assigned territory or market area during the scorecard period.']
  new_vehicle_sales_actual varchar [note: 'The actual number of new vehicles sold by the dealer during the scorecard period.']
  new_vehicle_sales_attainment_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'The percentage of the new vehicle sales objective achieved by the dealer (actual/objective * 100).']
  new_vehicle_sales_objective varchar [note: 'The target number of new vehicles the dealer was expected to sell during the scorecard period.']
  nps_benchmark decimal(5,2) [note: 'The target or industry benchmark NPS score the dealer is expected to meet or exceed.']
  nps_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'The Net Promoter Score measuring customer loyalty and likelihood to recommend the dealer, calculated as percentage of promoters minus percentage of detractors.']
  parts_fill_rate_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'The percentage of parts orders fulfilled from dealer inventory without backorder during the scorecard period.']
  parts_revenue_actual decimal(15,2) [note: 'The actual parts department revenue generated during the scorecard period.']
  parts_revenue_objective decimal(15,2) [note: 'The target parts department revenue for the scorecard period.']
  performance_tier varchar [note: 'The performance tier classification assigned to the dealer based on composite score and key metrics.']
  period_type varchar [note: 'The frequency of the scorecard evaluation period.']
  published_date date [note: 'The date this scorecard was officially published and communicated to the dealer.']
  reviewer_name varchar [note: 'The name of the OEM regional manager or analyst who reviewed and approved this scorecard.']
  scorecard_period_end_date date [note: 'The last day of the evaluation period covered by this scorecard.']
  scorecard_period_start_date date [note: 'The first day of the evaluation period covered by this scorecard (monthly or quarterly).']
  scorecard_status varchar [note: 'The current status of this scorecard record in its lifecycle.']
  service_absorption_rate_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'The percentage of fixed operations expenses covered by service and parts gross profit, a key profitability metric for dealer operations.']
  service_revenue_actual decimal(15,2) [note: 'The actual service department revenue generated during the scorecard period.']
  service_revenue_objective decimal(15,2) [note: 'The target service department revenue for the scorecard period.']
  training_compliance_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'The percentage of required OEM training programs completed by dealer staff during the scorecard period.']
  used_vehicle_sales_actual varchar [note: 'The actual number of used vehicles sold by the dealer during the scorecard period.']
  used_vehicle_sales_attainment_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'The percentage of the used vehicle sales objective achieved by the dealer.']
  used_vehicle_sales_objective varchar [note: 'The target number of used vehicles the dealer was expected to sell during the scorecard period.']
  warranty_claim_approval_rate_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'The percentage of warranty claims submitted by the dealer that were approved by the OEM during the scorecard period.']

  Note: 'Periodic (monthly/quarterly) dealer performance scorecard capturing KPIs across sales, service, parts, customer satisfaction, and facility standards. Tracks new vehicle sales vs objective, used vehicle sales, service absorption rate, CSI (Customer Satisfaction Index) score, NPS (Net Promoter Score), parts fill rate, and overall composite score. Used for franchise compliance and incentive eligibility determination.'
}

Table dealer.csi_survey {
  csi_survey_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the CSI survey response record.']
  csi_dealer_dealership_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the dealer location where the customer interaction occurred that triggered this survey.']
  csi_dealership_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the dealer location where the customer interaction occurred that triggered this survey.']
  csi_order_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the dealer order associated with this survey, if the survey is related to a sales transaction.']
  csi_repair_order_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the repair order associated with this survey, if the survey is related to a service transaction.']
  individual_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the customer who completed the survey.']
  party_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the customer who completed the survey.']
  vin_registry_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the vehicle associated with the customer interaction that triggered this survey.']
  complaint_category varchar [note: 'Category code classifying the nature of the customer complaint if complaint_flag is True (e.g., service_quality, pricing, staff_behavior, facility_issue).']
  complaint_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator (True/False) whether the survey response contains a customer complaint requiring follow-up action.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this survey record was first created in the system.']
  days_to_response varchar [note: 'Number of days between the customer interaction date and the survey completion date.']
  dealer_performance_impact_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator (True/False) whether this survey response will be included in the dealer\'s official performance scorecard and franchise compliance review.']
  delivery_experience_rating varchar [note: 'Customer rating of the delivery experience (1-10).']
  delivery_process_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Customer satisfaction score for the vehicle delivery process, applicable to sales satisfaction surveys.']
  facility_cleanliness_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Customer satisfaction score for the cleanliness and appearance of the dealership facility.']
  follow_up_completed_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator (True/False) whether the required follow-up action has been completed.']
  follow_up_completion_date date [note: 'Date when the follow-up action was completed, if applicable.']
  follow_up_required_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator (True/False) whether the survey response requires dealer or OEM follow-up contact with the customer.']
  interaction_date date [note: 'Date of the original customer interaction (sales delivery, service completion, etc.) that triggered this survey.']
  market_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code identifying the geographic market where the survey was conducted.']
  nps_category varchar [note: 'Classification of the customer based on NPS score: promoter (9-10), passive (7-8), or detractor (0-6).']
  nps_score varchar [note: 'Net Promoter Score indicating customer likelihood to recommend the dealer or brand, typically on a 0-10 scale.']
  oem_program_compliant_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator (True/False) whether the survey response meets OEM CSI program compliance thresholds for dealer performance evaluation.']
  overall_satisfaction_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Overall customer satisfaction score, typically on a scale (e.g., 1-5, 1-10, or 0-100) as defined by the OEM CSI program.']
  pricing_transparency_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Customer satisfaction score for the transparency and fairness of pricing communicated during the transaction.']
  problem_resolution_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Customer satisfaction score for how effectively any issues or concerns were resolved.']
  response_channel varchar [note: 'Channel through which the customer submitted the survey response (email, phone, SMS, web portal, mobile app, postal mail, or in-person at dealership). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: email|phone|sms|web|mobile_app|mail|in_person — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  sales_consultant_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Customer satisfaction score for the sales consultant interaction, applicable to sales satisfaction surveys.']
  sales_region_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the OEM sales region or district to which the dealer belongs for regional performance aggregation.']
  sentiment_category varchar [note: 'Categorical classification of the verbatim comment sentiment: positive, neutral, negative, or mixed.']
  sentiment_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Automated sentiment analysis score derived from the verbatim comment, typically ranging from -1 (negative) to +1 (positive).']
  service_advisor_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Customer satisfaction score for the service advisor interaction, applicable to service satisfaction surveys.']
  service_quality_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Customer satisfaction score for the quality of service work performed, applicable to service satisfaction surveys.']
  service_timeliness_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Customer satisfaction score for the timeliness of service completion, applicable to service satisfaction surveys.']
  staff_courtesy_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Customer satisfaction score for the courtesy and professionalism of dealership staff.']
  survey_completion_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of survey questions answered by the customer, indicating survey completion rate (0-100).']
  survey_date date [note: 'Date when the customer completed the survey.']
  survey_language_code varchar [note: 'Two-letter ISO 639-1 language code indicating the language in which the survey was presented and completed.']
  survey_program_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the specific OEM CSI survey program or campaign under which this survey was administered.']
  survey_status varchar [note: 'Status of the survey response: completed (fully answered), partial (incomplete), abandoned (started but not submitted), or invalid (failed validation).']
  survey_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise timestamp when the customer submitted the survey response.']
  survey_type varchar [note: 'Type of CSI survey indicating the customer interaction being evaluated (sales, service, delivery, parts, general, or Pre-Delivery Inspection).']
  survey_vendor_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the third-party vendor or platform that administered the survey (e.g., Medallia, Qualtrics, internal OEM system).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this survey record was last updated in the system.']
  vehicle_condition_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Customer satisfaction score for the condition of the vehicle at delivery or service completion.']
  verbatim_comment varchar [note: 'Free-text comment provided by the customer describing their experience, concerns, or suggestions.']
  vin varchar [note: '17-character Vehicle Identification Number of the vehicle associated with this survey response.']

  Note: 'Customer Satisfaction Index (CSI) survey responses collected post-sales and post-service at the dealership level. Captures survey type (sales satisfaction, service satisfaction), survey date, response channel, individual question scores, verbatim comments, overall satisfaction score, and OEM program compliance flag. Feeds dealer performance scorecards and franchise compliance reviews.'
}

Table dealer.service_capacity {
  service_capacity_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the service capacity configuration record. Primary key.']
  service_dealer_dealership_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the dealer location that owns this service capacity configuration.']
  service_dealership_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the dealer location that owns this service capacity configuration.']
  a_tech_headcount varchar [note: 'Number of A-level (master) technicians certified for complex diagnostics, ECU (Electronic Control Unit) programming, and advanced repair work.']
  adas_certified_tech_count varchar [note: 'Number of technicians certified for ADAS calibration, sensor diagnostics, and autonomous driving system service.']
  adas_service_bays varchar [note: 'Number of service bays equipped with ADAS (Advanced Driver Assistance Systems) calibration equipment for sensor alignment and diagnostic work.']
  available_labor_hours_per_day decimal(8,2) [note: 'Total productive labor hours available per day across all technicians, calculated as (technician headcount × hours per shift × efficiency factor). Used for service appointment scheduling and capacity utilization analysis.']
  avg_ro_cycle_time_hours decimal(6,2) [note: 'Average time in hours from RO (Repair Order) open to close. Used for scheduling optimization and capacity planning.']
  b_tech_headcount varchar [note: 'Number of B-level (journeyman) technicians certified for general maintenance and standard repair operations.']
  body_shop_bays varchar [note: 'Number of service bays designated for collision repair, body work, and paint operations.']
  body_shop_tech_count varchar [note: 'Number of technicians certified for collision repair, body work, and paint operations.']
  c_tech_headcount varchar [note: 'Number of C-level (apprentice) technicians performing basic maintenance tasks and assisting senior technicians.']
  capacity_config_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the service capacity configuration, typically used in DMS (Dealer Management System) integration and capacity planning reports.']
  capacity_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes regarding capacity constraints, planned expansions, seasonal adjustments, or special considerations for service scheduling.']
  capacity_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the service capacity configuration. Active indicates normal operations, seasonal indicates temporary capacity adjustments, reduced/expanded indicate capacity changes, under_review indicates pending capacity assessment.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this service capacity configuration record was first created in the system.']
  current_capacity_utilization_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Current utilization rate of service capacity expressed as a percentage. Calculated as (actual labor hours used / available labor hours) × 100. Used for capacity planning and scheduling optimization.']
  customer_pay_capacity_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of total service capacity allocated for customer pay (non-warranty) repair orders. Typically 60-70% of total capacity.']
  diagnostic_equipment_level varchar [note: 'Classification of diagnostic equipment capability. Basic: OBD scanners. Standard: factory scan tools. Advanced: ECU programming and ADAS calibration. Premium: full factory diagnostic suite with remote technical assistance.']
  dms_capacity_record_reference varchar [note: 'External identifier for this capacity configuration in the source DMS system. Used for data lineage and integration reconciliation.']
  dms_system_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the DMS platform used by the dealer for service scheduling and capacity management. Primary integration point for capacity data synchronization.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when this service capacity configuration expires or is superseded. Nullable for open-ended configurations.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when this service capacity configuration becomes effective. Used for capacity planning and scheduling optimization.']
  ev_certified_tech_count varchar [note: 'Number of technicians holding OEM (Original Equipment Manufacturer) certification for EV, HEV, and PHEV high-voltage system service and repair.']
  ev_service_bays varchar [note: 'Number of service bays equipped and certified for EV (Electric Vehicle), HEV (Hybrid Electric Vehicle), and PHEV (Plug-in Hybrid Electric Vehicle) service work.']
  express_service_bays varchar [note: 'Number of service bays designated for quick service operations such as oil changes, tire rotations, and PDI (Pre-Delivery Inspection).']
  general_service_bays varchar [note: 'Number of service bays designated for general maintenance and repair work on ICE (Internal Combustion Engine) vehicles.']
  has_mobile_service_capability boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the dealer offers mobile service capability for on-site customer vehicle service and OTA updates.']
  hours_per_shift decimal(4,2) [note: 'Standard duration of each work shift in hours, typically 8.0 or 10.0 hours.']
  internal_capacity_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of total service capacity allocated for internal work including PDI (Pre-Delivery Inspection), loaner maintenance, and dealer-owned vehicle service. Typically 10-15% of total capacity.']
  last_capacity_review_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent capacity assessment and configuration review. Used for capacity planning governance and audit trails.']
  loaner_fleet_size varchar [note: 'Total number of loaner vehicles available for customer use during service appointments. Used for customer satisfaction and service capacity planning.']
  loaner_fleet_utilization_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Current utilization rate of the loaner fleet expressed as a percentage. Calculated as (loaners in use / total loaner fleet size) × 100.']
  max_daily_appointments varchar [note: 'Maximum number of service appointments that can be scheduled per day based on bay capacity, technician availability, and average repair order duration.']
  mobile_service_slots varchar [note: 'Number of available mobile/home service slots per day.']
  mobile_service_technician_count varchar [note: 'Number of technicians dedicated to mobile service operations. Null if mobile service is not offered.']
  next_capacity_review_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next capacity assessment and configuration review. Typically quarterly or semi-annually.']
  operating_days_per_week varchar [note: 'Number of days per week the service department operates. Typically 5 or 6 days for most dealers.']
  parts_inventory_capacity_sqft decimal(10,2) [note: 'Total square footage of parts storage capacity at the dealer service location. Used for parts inventory planning and JIT (Just-in-Time) delivery optimization.']
  shifts_per_day varchar [note: 'Number of work shifts operated per day (1 for single shift, 2 for double shift operations).']
  specialty_certifications varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of specialty certifications held by the service department, such as EV, ADAS, body shop, OBD (On-Board Diagnostics), TPMS (Tire Pressure Monitoring System), OTA (Over-the-Air Update) programming. Used for service capability assessment and customer routing.']
  target_capacity_utilization_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Target utilization rate for optimal service department performance, typically 80-85%. Used for capacity planning and performance scorecards.']
  total_service_bays varchar [note: 'Total number of physical service bays available at the dealer service department for vehicle maintenance and repair work.']
  total_technician_headcount varchar [note: 'Total number of certified technicians employed at the dealer service department across all skill levels.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this service capacity configuration record was last modified. Used for change tracking and audit trails.']
  warranty_work_capacity_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of total service capacity allocated for warranty and recall work. Typically 20-30% of total capacity.']

  Note: 'Dealer service department capacity configuration including number of service bays, technician headcount by skill level (A/B/C tech), available labor hours per day, specialty certifications (EV, ADAS, body shop), loaner fleet size, and current utilization rate. Used for service appointment scheduling optimization and capacity planning.'
}

Table dealer.floor_plan {
  floor_plan_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the floor plan financing record.']
  fi_contract_id varchar [note: 'FK to the F&I contract for dealer floor plan financing.']
  floor_dealer_dealership_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the dealer holding this floor plan financing arrangement.']
  floor_dealership_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the dealer holding this floor plan financing arrangement.']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.sku. Business justification: Floor‑plan financing is calculated per SKU to reflect cost, interest, and regulatory limits.']
  vin_registry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to vehicle.vin_registry. Business justification: Floor plan financing is per‑vehicle; linking to VIN registry enables accurate interest calculation and balance tracking.']
  account_number varchar [note: 'Unique account number assigned by the financing institution to the dealer\'s floor plan credit facility.']
  aged_inventory_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator flagging vehicles that have exceeded the aging threshold defined in the floor plan agreement (typically 90, 120, or 180 days). True indicates aged inventory requiring attention.']
  aging_threshold_days varchar [note: 'Number of days after which inventory is considered aged per the floor plan agreement terms. Triggers curtailment requirements or OEM assistance programs.']
  audit_status varchar [note: 'Result status of the most recent floor plan audit. Passed indicates compliance; failed indicates discrepancies; pending indicates audit in progress; not_required indicates no audit needed; exception indicates special circumstances.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this floor plan financing record was first created in the system.']
  credit_line_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total credit line amount approved by the financing institution for the dealer\'s floor plan facility. Represents the maximum borrowing capacity.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for all monetary amounts in this floor plan record (e.g., USD, EUR, CAD).']
  curtailment_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Required curtailment payment amount for aged inventory, typically a percentage of the original financed amount after a specified aging period (e.g., 90 or 120 days).']
  curtailment_due_date date [note: 'Scheduled date by which the dealer must make a curtailment payment (partial paydown) on aged inventory per the floor plan agreement terms.']
  curtailment_paid_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator of whether the required curtailment payment has been made by the dealer. True indicates paid; False indicates outstanding.']
  daily_interest_amount decimal(10,2) [note: 'Daily interest accrual amount calculated based on the outstanding balance and interest rate. Used for dealer financial health monitoring.']
  days_in_inventory varchar [note: 'Number of days the vehicle has been in dealer inventory under floor plan financing, calculated from financing start date to current date or sale date.']
  dealer_invoice_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Original dealer invoice amount for the vehicle, typically the basis for the floor plan financed amount.']
  default_date date [note: 'Date when the dealer was declared in default on floor plan obligations for this vehicle. Null if no default has occurred.']
  default_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator of whether the dealer has defaulted on floor plan payments for this vehicle. True indicates default; False indicates current status.']
  dms_floor_plan_reference varchar [note: 'External identifier for this floor plan record in the dealer\'s DMS (e.g., CDK Global). Used for system integration and reconciliation.']
  financing_end_date date [note: 'Date when floor plan financing for this vehicle unit was terminated, either through retail sale and payoff or other resolution.']
  financing_institution_code varchar [note: 'Internal or industry-standard code identifying the financing institution for system integration and reporting.']
  financing_institution_name varchar [note: 'Name of the financial institution or captive finance company providing the floor plan credit line (e.g., OEM captive finance, third-party lender).']
  financing_start_date date [note: 'Date when floor plan financing for this vehicle unit commenced. Typically the date the vehicle was received into dealer inventory and financed.']
  floor_plan_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the floor plan financing record. Active indicates ongoing financing; paid_off indicates vehicle sold and financing settled; defaulted indicates payment failure; suspended indicates temporary hold; pending_audit indicates under review; curtailed indicates partial paydown required.']
  interest_rate_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Annual interest rate percentage charged by the financing institution on the outstanding floor plan balance for this vehicle.']
  last_audit_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent floor plan audit conducted by the financing institution or OEM to verify physical inventory and compliance with financing terms.']
  msrp_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Manufacturer Suggested Retail Price for the financed vehicle. Used for margin analysis and dealer financial health assessment.']
  next_audit_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next floor plan audit. Used for audit planning and dealer notification.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional notes, comments, or special circumstances related to this floor plan financing record (e.g., audit exceptions, payment arrangements, OEM approvals).']
  oem_assistance_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Dollar amount of OEM assistance provided for this floor plan record, such as interest rate buy-down or direct payment subsidy.']
  oem_assistance_program_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying any OEM floor plan assistance program applied to this vehicle (e.g., interest subsidy, extended aging allowance, special financing terms for slow-moving models).']
  outstanding_balance_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Current outstanding principal balance owed to the financing institution for this specific vehicle unit. Updated upon curtailment payments and final payoff.']
  payoff_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total amount paid to the financing institution to settle the floor plan obligation for this vehicle, including principal and accrued interest.']
  payoff_date date [note: 'Date when the floor plan financing for this vehicle was fully paid off, typically upon retail sale or dealer buyout.']
  per_unit_floor_plan_cost decimal(18,2) [note: 'The financed amount for this individual vehicle unit, typically equal to dealer invoice or a percentage thereof. Basis for interest calculation.']
  sap_document_number varchar [note: 'SAP financial document number associated with the floor plan financing transaction in the OEM\'s ERP system (SAP S/4HANA FI module).']
  total_interest_paid decimal(18,2) [note: 'Cumulative interest amount paid to the financing institution over the life of the floor plan financing for this vehicle unit.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this floor plan financing record was last modified. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.']
  yard_storage_cost_per_day decimal(18,2) [note: 'Daily cost for yard storage under the floor plan.']

  Note: 'Dealer vehicle floor plan financing records tracking the financing of new vehicle inventory. Captures financing institution, credit line amount, outstanding balance, per-unit floor plan cost, curtailment schedule, aged inventory flags, and floor plan audit dates. Critical for dealer financial health monitoring and OEM floor plan assistance program administration.'
}

Table dealer.demo_vehicle {
  demo_vehicle_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the demo vehicle record. Primary key.']
  demo_dealer_dealership_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the dealer location where this demo vehicle is assigned.']
  demo_dealership_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the dealer location where this demo vehicle is assigned.']
  demo_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the salesperson to whom this demo vehicle is assigned.']
  primary_demo_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the salesperson to whom this demo vehicle is assigned.']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.sku. Business justification: Demo vehicle program tracks specific SKU to manage feature availability, warranty coverage, and cost recovery.']
  vin_registry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to vehicle.vin_registry. Business justification: Demo vehicle assignment and usage reporting require linking to the VIN registry for compliance and mileage tracking.']
  accident_count varchar [note: 'Number of reported accidents or incidents involving this demo vehicle during the demo period.']
  assigned_salesperson_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the salesperson or sales manager assigned to this demo vehicle.']
  assignment_type varchar [note: 'Type of assignment indicating how the demo vehicle is being used.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this demo vehicle record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary amounts.']
  current_odometer_km varchar [note: 'Most recent odometer reading in kilometers for the demo vehicle.']
  demo_designation_date date [note: 'Date when the vehicle was officially designated as a demo unit.']
  demo_end_date date [note: 'Scheduled or actual date when the demo vehicle program period ends or ended.']
  demo_period_months varchar [note: 'Duration of the demo vehicle program assignment in months.']
  demo_start_date date [note: 'Date when the demo vehicle program period began for this unit.']
  demo_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the demo vehicle program assignment.']
  demo_usage_type varchar [note: 'Primary usage type for this demo vehicle.']
  disposition_date date [note: 'Date when the demo vehicle was disposed of or transitioned out of the demo program.']
  disposition_type varchar [note: 'Final disposition of the demo vehicle at the end of the demo program period.']
  floor_plan_interest_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total floor plan interest cost incurred by the dealer for this demo vehicle during the demo period.']
  home_demo_eligible_flag boolean [note: 'Whether this demo vehicle is eligible for home test drive delivery.']
  incentive_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'OEM incentive amount provided to the dealer for maintaining this demo vehicle.']
  insurance_policy_number varchar [note: 'Insurance policy number covering this demo vehicle during the demo period.']
  mileage_allowance_km varchar [note: 'Maximum allowed mileage in kilometers for the demo vehicle during the demo period per OEM policy.']
  mileage_overage_km varchar [note: 'Calculated mileage overage in kilometers beyond the allowance, if any.']
  odometer_at_designation_km varchar [note: 'Odometer reading in kilometers when the vehicle was designated as a demo unit.']
  oem_program_code varchar [note: 'OEM-assigned program code for the demo vehicle program under which this vehicle is registered.']
  pdi_completed_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether Pre-Delivery Inspection was completed for this demo vehicle.']
  pdi_completion_date date [note: 'Date when Pre-Delivery Inspection was completed.']
  recall_campaign_numbers varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of recall campaign numbers applicable to this demo vehicle.']
  sale_price_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Actual sale price if the demo vehicle was converted to a used vehicle sale.']
  service_record_count varchar [note: 'Total number of service or maintenance records for this demo vehicle during the demo period.']
  stock_number varchar [note: 'Dealer-assigned inventory stock number for this demo vehicle.']
  test_drive_count varchar [note: 'Total number of customer test drives conducted with this demo vehicle.']
  transmission_type varchar [note: 'Type of transmission installed in the demo vehicle.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this demo vehicle record was last updated.']

  Note: 'Dealer demonstrator vehicle program records tracking vehicles designated as demos for test drives and sales staff use. Captures VIN, demo designation date, assigned salesperson or manager, mileage allowance, demo period end date, demo status, and disposition (converted to used sale, returned to stock, auctioned). Governed by OEM demo vehicle policy.'
}

Table dealer.dealer_test_drive {
  dealer_test_drive_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the dealer_test_drive data product (auto-inserted pre-linking).']
  connected_vehicle_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to mobility.connected_vehicle. Business justification: Test Drive Telemetry Tracking captures vehicle performance data during customer test drives for quality analysis.']
  dealership_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to dealer.dealership. Business justification: Dealer test drives belong to a dealership; add dealership_id FK to link to dealership and remove redundant dealer_id column.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Test‑drive assignments are recorded to the employee conducting the drive for compliance and sales tracking.']
  party_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.party. Business justification: Test‑drive records must reference the interested customer to track conversion rates and follow‑up communications.']
  completed_at timestamp
  created_timestamp timestamp
  customer_name varchar [note: 'Name of the customer']
  customer_phone varchar [note: 'Customer phone number']
  distance_driven_km varchar
  duration_minutes varchar [note: 'Duration of test drive in minutes']
  end_mileage varchar [note: 'Odometer reading at end']
  end_timestamp timestamp
  feedback_notes varchar
  feedback_score varchar
  home_test_drive_flag boolean [note: 'Whether this is a home/doorstep test drive delivery.']
  mileage_driven varchar
  outcome varchar [note: 'Outcome (interested, quote requested, purchased, not interested)']
  outcome_notes varchar
  route_type varchar [note: 'Route type (city, highway, mixed)']
  scheduled_at timestamp
  scheduled_timestamp timestamp
  start_mileage varchar [note: 'Odometer reading at start']
  start_timestamp timestamp
  test_drive_date date [note: 'Date of the test drive']
  test_drive_number varchar
  test_drive_status varchar
  updated_timestamp timestamp
  vehicle_model varchar
  vin varchar [note: 'VIN of the demo vehicle used']

  Note: 'Records of customer test drive events conducted at a dealership. Captures test drive date/time, VIN driven, customer identifier, salesperson, duration, route type, customer feedback captured, and follow-up action scheduled. Supports sales funnel tracking and connects to lead management in Salesforce Automotive Cloud.'
}

Table dealer.dealer_parts_order {
  dealer_parts_order_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the dealer_parts_order data product (auto-inserted pre-linking).']
  aftersales_parts_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to aftersales.parts_order. Business justification: Decision: Dealer parts order must reference the aftersales parts order entity that holds supplier, line‑item, and delivery details.']
  compliance_document_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.compliance_document. Business justification: Parts orders must reference the compliance document certifying part suitability for regulated vehicles, required for audit trails.']
  dealership_id varchar [note: 'Dealership placing the order']
  party_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.party. Business justification: Parts orders are often placed for a specific customer\'s vehicle; linking enables parts‑to‑customer analytics and service cost allocation.']
  supply_purchase_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.supply_purchase_order. Business justification: Parts order processing needs the originating purchase order to reconcile supplier deliveries with dealer demand.']
  actual_delivery_date date [note: 'Actual delivery date']
  created_timestamp timestamp
  dangerous_goods_flag boolean [note: 'Whether the parts order contains dangerous goods.']
  expected_delivery_date date
  line_count varchar [note: 'Number of line items in the order']
  order_date date [note: 'Date the parts order was placed']
  order_number varchar [note: 'Parts order number']
  order_quantity varchar
  order_status varchar [note: 'Status (submitted, confirmed, shipped, received, cancelled)']
  order_type varchar [note: 'Type (stock, emergency, VOR, campaign)']
  part_number varchar
  parts_order_number varchar
  parts_order_status varchar
  requested_delivery_date date [note: 'Requested delivery date']
  required_date date
  returnable_packaging_flag boolean [note: 'Whether the parts order uses returnable packaging containers.']
  total_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total order amount']
  total_order_amount decimal(18,2)
  updated_timestamp timestamp

  Note: 'Dealer parts replenishment orders placed with the OEM parts distribution center or aftermarket suppliers. Tracks order date, order type (emergency, stock, special order), line items with OEM part numbers, quantities ordered, quantities received, backorder status, estimated arrival date, and order total. Sourced from CDK Global DMS parts ordering module. This product is the single source of truth (SSOT) for its concept in the ECM Lakehouse.'
}

Table dealer.dealer_warranty_claim {
  dealer_warranty_claim_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the dealer_warranty_claim data product (auto-inserted pre-linking).']
  aftersales_warranty_claim_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to aftersales.warranty_claim. Business justification: Report: Dealer‑submitted warranty claim reports need to point to the authoritative aftersales warranty claim record for audit and reimbursement.']
  claim_processor_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Warranty claims are processed by a specific employee; required for claim audit trails and productivity metrics.']
  connected_vehicle_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to mobility.connected_vehicle. Business justification: Warranty Claim Validation requires telematics evidence from the connected vehicle to confirm usage and fault conditions.']
  party_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.party. Business justification: Warranty claim handling and compliance reporting require the claimant party to be identified.']
  procurement_supplier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.supplier. Business justification: Warranty Claim Processing requires identifying the supplier responsible for the defective component to route claims and track warranty liabilities.']
  vin_registry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to vehicle.vin_registry. Business justification: Warranty claim processing needs the VIN registry reference to verify coverage and claim eligibility.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date claim was approved']
  approved_amount decimal(18,2)
  causal_part_number varchar [note: 'Part number that caused the failure']
  claim_amount decimal(18,2)
  claim_date date [note: 'Date claim was submitted']
  claim_number varchar [note: 'Warranty claim number']
  claim_status varchar [note: 'Status (submitted, under-review, approved, rejected, paid)']
  claim_submission_date date
  claim_type varchar [note: 'Type (new vehicle, powertrain, extended, goodwill)']
  claimed_amount decimal(18,2)
  created_timestamp timestamp
  failure_code varchar [note: 'Failure code identifying the defect']
  failure_description varchar
  home_pickup_flag boolean [note: 'Whether vehicle was picked up from customer home for warranty work.']
  labor_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Labor amount claimed']
  labor_hours decimal(6,2)
  mileage varchar
  mileage_at_claim varchar
