  mileage_at_failure varchar [note: 'Vehicle mileage when failure occurred']
  parts_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Parts amount claimed']
  repair_date date [note: 'Date repair was performed']
  total_claim_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total claim amount']
  updated_timestamp timestamp
  warranty_claim_number varchar

  Note: 'Warranty claims submitted by the dealership to the OEM for reimbursement of warranty repair labor and parts. Captures VIN, repair order reference, failure date, mileage at failure, DTC (Diagnostic Trouble Code), causal part number, labor operation codes, claimed labor hours, claimed parts cost, submission date, OEM adjudication status (approved, rejected, partially approved), and reimbursement amount. This product is the single source of truth (SSOT) for its concept in the ECM Lakehouse.'
}

Table dealer.recall_completion {
  recall_completion_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the recall completion record. Primary key for the recall completion transaction.']
  recall_customer_party_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the customer who owns the vehicle at the time of recall completion.']
  recall_dealer_dealership_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the dealer location that performed the recall remediation work.']
  recall_dealership_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the dealer location that performed the recall remediation work.']
  recall_party_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the customer who owns the vehicle at the time of recall completion.']
  repair_order_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the repair order under which the recall work was performed.']
  technician_employee_id varchar [note: 'Employee identifier of the technician who performed the recall remediation work. Used for quality tracking and reimbursement validation.']
  vin_registry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to vehicle.vin_registry. Business justification: Recall completion tracking must reference the VIN registry to ensure the correct vehicle is marked as completed.']
  campaign_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the recall or campaign. Safety recalls are NHTSA-mandated, service campaigns are voluntary OEM actions, TSB (Technical Service Bulletin) are informational fixes.']
  completion_date date [note: 'Date when the recall remediation work was completed and the vehicle was returned to the customer. Used for NHTSA compliance reporting and OEM recall closure tracking.']
  completion_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the recall completion transaction, typically generated by the DMS or OEM recall management system.']
  completion_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the recall completion work. Completed indicates full remediation; incomplete or parts_pending indicate work not yet finished.']
  completion_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise date and time when the recall work was marked complete in the DMS system.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this recall completion record was first created in the data system. Used for audit trail and data lineage.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary amounts in this record (e.g., USD, EUR, CAD).']
  customer_notification_date date [note: 'Date when the customer was notified that the recall work was completed. Used for customer satisfaction tracking and communication audit.']
  customer_notification_method varchar [note: 'Method used to notify the customer of recall completion: email, SMS, phone call, postal mail, or in-person at dealer.']
  dms_transaction_reference varchar [note: 'Unique transaction identifier from the CDK Global DMS system. Used for data lineage and reconciliation between DMS and OEM systems.']
  home_service_completion_flag boolean [note: 'Whether the recall was completed via home/mobile service.']
  labor_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total labor cost for the recall work, calculated based on labor hours and dealer labor rate. Used for OEM reimbursement to dealer.']
  labor_hours decimal(6,2) [note: 'Total labor hours spent on the recall remediation work. Used for dealer reimbursement calculation and efficiency analysis.']
  model varchar [note: 'Specific model designation of the vehicle that received recall work.']
  model_year varchar [note: 'Model year of the vehicle that received the recall work. Used for recall eligibility verification and reporting.']
  nameplate varchar [note: 'Brand or nameplate of the vehicle (e.g., F-150, Camry, Silverado). Used to segment recall completion rates by product line.']
  nhtsa_report_date date [note: 'Date when the recall completion was reported to NHTSA. Required for compliance tracking and audit trails.']
  nhtsa_report_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this recall completion has been reported to NHTSA as required for safety recalls. True means reported; false means not yet reported or not required.']
  odometer_reading varchar [note: 'Vehicle odometer reading at the time of recall completion, typically in kilometers or miles. Used for warranty validation and vehicle history tracking.']
  odometer_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the odometer reading: km (kilometers) or mi (miles).']
  parts_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total cost of parts used in the recall remediation. OEM reimburses dealer for parts at wholesale cost.']
  parts_quantity_list varchar [note: 'Comma-separated or pipe-separated list of quantities corresponding to parts_used_list. Used for parts consumption analysis.']
  parts_used_list varchar [note: 'Comma-separated or pipe-separated list of part numbers used in the recall remediation. Used for parts consumption tracking and inventory replenishment.']
  post_repair_inspection_notes varchar [note: 'Technician notes from the post-repair inspection confirming that the recall remediation was successful and the vehicle is safe to operate.']
  powertrain_type varchar [note: 'Type of powertrain: ICE (Internal Combustion Engine), HEV (Hybrid Electric Vehicle), PHEV (Plug-in Hybrid Electric Vehicle), BEV (Battery Electric Vehicle), FCEV (Fuel Cell Electric Vehicle). Some recalls are powertrain-specific.']
  pre_repair_inspection_notes varchar [note: 'Technician notes from the pre-repair inspection documenting the condition of the vehicle and the recall-related issue before remediation.']
  quality_verification_date date [note: 'Date when the quality verification or audit of the recall work was performed.']
  quality_verification_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the recall work was subject to quality verification or audit by the dealer service manager or OEM field representative. True means verified; false means not verified.']
  quality_verifier_name varchar [note: 'Name of the person who performed the quality verification or audit of the recall work (typically service manager or OEM field representative).']
  recall_campaign_number varchar [note: 'Official recall or service campaign number issued by NHTSA or the OEM. Used to track which specific safety recall or service action was completed.']
  reimbursement_claim_number varchar [note: 'Claim number submitted by the dealer to the OEM for reimbursement of recall work. Links to the OEM financial system for payment processing.']
  reimbursement_paid_date date [note: 'Date when the OEM paid the dealer for the recall work. Used for dealer cash flow analysis and OEM financial reporting.']
  reimbursement_status varchar [note: 'Status of the dealer reimbursement claim. Pending indicates claim submitted but not yet reviewed; approved means claim validated; paid means funds transferred to dealer.']
  remedy_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the remediation work performed to address the recall. Includes parts replaced, adjustments made, software updates applied, etc.']
  sublet_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Cost of any sublet or outsourced work required to complete the recall (e.g., specialized welding, paint work). Reimbursable by OEM if pre-approved.']
  technician_certification_number varchar [note: 'Certification or license number of the technician. Some recalls require certified technicians to perform the work.']
  technician_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the technician who completed the recall work. Used for performance tracking and quality audits.']
  total_reimbursement_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total amount claimed by the dealer for reimbursement from the OEM, including labor, parts, and sublet costs.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this recall completion record was last modified. Used for change tracking and audit trail.']

  Note: 'Tracks the completion of NHTSA safety recalls and OEM service campaigns at the dealer level. Records recall/campaign number, VIN remediated, completion date, technician, parts used, labor time, reimbursement claimed, and completion status. Supports NHTSA compliance reporting and OEM recall closure rate monitoring.'
}

Table dealer.dms_integration_log {
  dms_integration_log_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for each DMS integration event log entry. Primary key for the integration log table.']
  dms_dealer_dealership_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the dealership for which this integration event occurred. Links to the dealer master data.']
  dms_dealership_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the dealership for which this integration event occurred. Links to the dealer master data.']
  authentication_method varchar [note: 'Authentication mechanism used to verify dealer DMS identity and authorize data access during synchronization.']
  business_impact_severity varchar [note: 'Assessment of business impact severity if this sync event failed. Critical failures affect customer-facing operations; low impact affects reporting only.']
  compression_enabled_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether data compression was enabled for this synchronization event to optimize bandwidth usage.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this integration log record was first created in the OEM data platform. Used for audit trail and data lineage.']
  data_freshness_lag_minutes varchar [note: 'Time difference in minutes between when data was last updated in the OEM system and when it was synchronized to the dealer DMS. Key metric for monitoring data currency.']
  dealer_code varchar [note: 'OEM-assigned unique code identifying the dealership. Used for business reporting and dealer network management.']
  dealer_name varchar [note: 'Legal business name of the dealership. Denormalized for reporting convenience and to support historical tracking if dealer names change.']
  delivery_event_type varchar [note: 'Type of delivery-related event logged from DMS.']
  dms_endpoint_url varchar [note: 'The specific CDK Global DMS API endpoint URL used for this synchronization event. Identifies the dealer system instance and integration point.']
  dms_transaction_reference varchar [note: 'Unique transaction identifier generated by the CDK Global DMS for this sync event. Used for end-to-end traceability and support escalation.']
  dms_version varchar [note: 'Version number of the CDK Global DMS software running at the dealership. Used to track compatibility and feature availability.']
  encryption_protocol varchar [note: 'Security protocol used to encrypt data during transmission between OEM platform and dealer DMS. Ensures data confidentiality and integrity.']
  error_category varchar [note: 'Classification of the error type encountered during synchronization. Used for root cause analysis and troubleshooting dealer connectivity issues.']
  error_message varchar [note: 'Detailed error message or description returned by the DMS or integration middleware. Provides technical details for troubleshooting failed sync events.']
  escalation_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this failed sync event requires escalation to dealer support or OEM integration team for manual intervention.']
  escalation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this sync failure was escalated to support teams. Null if no escalation occurred or sync succeeded.']
  integration_batch_reference varchar [note: 'Unique batch identifier assigned to group related synchronization events. Used to correlate multiple sync operations within a single integration cycle.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this integration log record was last updated. Tracks changes to sync status, retry attempts, or resolution information.']
  max_retry_attempts varchar [note: 'Maximum number of retry attempts configured for this sync event type before escalation or manual intervention is required.']
  next_retry_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Scheduled date and time for the next automatic retry attempt if the current sync failed. Null if no retry is scheduled or sync succeeded.']
  oem_transaction_reference varchar [note: 'Unique transaction identifier generated by the OEM integration platform for this sync event. Used for internal traceability and audit.']
  payload_size_kb decimal(10,2) [note: 'Size of the data payload transmitted during this sync event measured in kilobytes. Used to monitor bandwidth utilization and identify large transfers.']
  record_count_failed varchar [note: 'Number of records that failed to synchronize due to validation errors, connectivity issues, or data quality problems.']
  record_count_received varchar [note: 'Total number of records successfully received and acknowledged by the dealer DMS. Used to validate completeness of data transfer.']
  record_count_sent varchar [note: 'Total number of records transmitted from the OEM platform to the dealer DMS during this sync event. Applicable for push operations.']
  region_code varchar [note: 'Geographic region code where the dealership is located. Used for regional performance analysis and connectivity monitoring.']
  resolution_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes documenting the root cause and resolution steps taken for failed sync events. Used for knowledge management and trend analysis.']
  resolution_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when a failed sync event was resolved and data synchronization completed successfully. Used to calculate mean time to resolution.']
  retry_attempt_number varchar [note: 'Sequential number indicating which retry attempt this represents for a failed synchronization. Zero indicates the initial attempt.']
  sla_compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this synchronization event met the defined SLA target for duration and success rate. Used for dealer performance scorecards.']
  sla_target_duration_seconds varchar [note: 'Target synchronization duration in seconds as defined in the dealer integration SLA. Used to measure performance against contractual commitments.']
  sync_duration_seconds varchar [note: 'Total elapsed time in seconds from sync initiation to completion or failure. Used to monitor DMS performance and identify slow connections.']
  sync_event_type varchar [note: 'Type of data synchronization event between OEM platform and CDK Global DMS. Indicates the direction and nature of data flow (push from OEM or pull from dealer).']
  sync_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the synchronization event indicating whether data transfer completed successfully, partially succeeded, or failed.']
  sync_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the synchronization event was initiated. This is the principal business event timestamp for the integration log entry.']

  Note: 'Business-level integration event log tracking data synchronization events between the OEM data platform and CDK Global DMS at each dealership. Captures sync event type (inventory push, RO pull, sales pull, parts pull), dealership, sync timestamp, record count, sync status (success, partial, failed), and error category. This is a BUSINESS operational record — not an IT log — used to monitor dealer data freshness and DMS connectivity health for business operations.'
}

Table dealer.facility_standard {
  facility_standard_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the facility standard compliance record. Primary key for this entity.']
  assessor_employee_id varchar [note: 'Employee identifier for the OEM assessor. Links to HR system for auditor qualification tracking.']
  facility_dealer_dealership_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the dealership being assessed for facility standards compliance.']
  facility_dealership_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the dealership being assessed for facility standards compliance.']
  franchise_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the franchise agreement under which this facility standard assessment is conducted. Links to contract terms and compliance obligations.']
  allocation_eligibility_impact_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether non-compliance with this facility standard affects the dealer\'s vehicle allocation eligibility. True = may reduce allocation, False = no allocation impact.']
  assessment_date date [note: 'Date when the facility standards assessment was conducted. Primary business event timestamp for this compliance record.']
  assessment_method varchar [note: 'Methodology used to conduct the facility standards assessment. On_site = physical inspection, remote = video/virtual tour, hybrid = combination, photographic_evidence = dealer-submitted photos reviewed by OEM, self_assessment = dealer self-evaluation with OEM verification.']
  assessment_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the facility standards assessment. Unique reference number used in communications and reporting.']
  assessment_report_url varchar [note: 'Link to the detailed facility standards assessment report document stored in the document management system. Contains photos, detailed findings, and recommendations.']
  assessment_type varchar [note: 'Purpose and context of the facility standards assessment. Initial = new dealer onboarding, annual = scheduled yearly review, renewal = franchise renewal evaluation, spot_check = unscheduled compliance verification, post_remediation = follow-up after deficiency correction, franchise_application = pre-approval assessment.']
  assessor_certification_number varchar [note: 'Professional certification number of the assessor, demonstrating qualification to conduct facility standards audits.']
  assessor_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the OEM representative or third-party auditor who conducted the facility standards assessment.']
  compliance_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Quantitative compliance rating expressed as a percentage (0.00 to 100.00). Calculated based on weighted assessment criteria and deficiency severity. Used for franchise renewal decisions and dealer performance scorecards.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Current compliance state for this facility standard. Compliant = meets all requirements, non_compliant = fails to meet standards, conditional = compliant with minor exceptions, pending_review = assessment submitted awaiting approval, remediation_required = deficiencies identified requiring correction, waived = exemption granted by OEM.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this facility standard compliance record was first created in the system. Audit trail for record lifecycle tracking.']
  critical_deficiency_count varchar [note: 'Number of critical deficiencies identified. Critical deficiencies are those that pose safety risks, violate regulatory requirements, or severely impact brand image.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary amounts in this record.']
  dealer_response_notes varchar [note: 'Dealer\'s formal response to the assessment findings, including planned remediation actions, timelines, and any disputes or clarifications.']
  deficiency_count varchar [note: 'Total number of deficiency items identified during the assessment. Includes all severity levels (critical, major, minor).']
  deficiency_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative description of all deficiency items identified during the assessment. Includes specific observations, locations, and non-compliance details.']
  dms_integration_status varchar [note: 'Status of data synchronization with the dealer\'s DMS system (CDK Global). Indicates whether facility standards compliance data has been successfully shared with the dealer\'s operational system.']
  dms_last_sync_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent successful data synchronization with the dealer\'s DMS system.']
  estimated_investment_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Estimated cost for the dealer to achieve full compliance with this facility standard. Used for facility investment planning and franchise renewal negotiations.']
  franchise_renewal_impact_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this facility standard assessment result will be considered in the next franchise renewal decision. True = impacts renewal, False = informational only.']
  incentive_eligibility_impact_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether non-compliance with this facility standard affects the dealer\'s eligibility for OEM incentive programs. True = may disqualify from incentives, False = no incentive impact.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this facility standard compliance record was most recently updated. Audit trail for change tracking.']
  major_deficiency_count varchar [note: 'Number of major deficiencies identified. Major deficiencies significantly impact customer experience or operational efficiency but are not safety-critical.']
  minor_deficiency_count varchar [note: 'Number of minor deficiencies identified. Minor deficiencies are cosmetic or procedural issues with minimal business impact.']
  next_assessment_due_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next facility standards assessment. Frequency determined by standard type, compliance history, and franchise agreement terms.']
  oem_reviewer_notes varchar [note: 'Internal OEM notes regarding the assessment, remediation progress, or special considerations. Used for franchise management and dealer relationship tracking.']
  passing_threshold_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Minimum compliance score required to achieve compliant status for this standard type. Threshold may vary by standard category and dealer tier.']
  photographic_evidence_url varchar [note: 'Link to photographic documentation of facility conditions at time of assessment. Used for compliance verification and historical tracking.']
  remediation_completion_date date [note: 'Date when all required corrective actions were completed by the dealer. Verified through post-remediation assessment or photographic evidence.']
  remediation_deadline date [note: 'Date by which all identified deficiencies must be corrected. Failure to meet this deadline may result in franchise penalties or suspension.']
  remediation_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether corrective action is required to address identified deficiencies. True = remediation mandatory, False = no action required or waived.']
  remediation_status varchar [note: 'Current state of corrective action for identified deficiencies. Tracks progress from identification through verification of completion.']
  remediation_verification_date date [note: 'Date when OEM verified that remediation actions were completed satisfactorily. May involve on-site re-inspection or remote verification.']
  standard_category varchar [note: 'High-level grouping of the facility standard type for reporting and analysis purposes.']
  standard_type varchar [note: 'Category of facility standard being assessed. CI = Corporate Identity (brand visual standards), signage (exterior/interior signage compliance), showroom (sales floor layout and presentation), service_drive (service reception area standards), ev_charging (Electric Vehicle charging infrastructure), parts_department (parts storage and display), body_shop (collision repair facility standards), customer_lounge (waiting area amenities), digital_experience (digital tools and kiosks). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: CI|signage|showroom|service_drive|ev_charging|parts_department|body_shop|customer_lounge|digital_experience — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  waiver_expiry_date date [note: 'Date when the granted waiver expires and full compliance is required. Null indicates permanent waiver.']
  waiver_granted_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the OEM granted a waiver or exemption for this facility standard. True = waiver approved, False = no waiver.']
  waiver_reason varchar [note: 'Business justification for granting a facility standards waiver. May include market conditions, dealer financial hardship, facility constraints, or strategic considerations.']
  yard_management_standard varchar [note: 'Required yard management standard for the facility.']

  Note: 'OEM facility and brand standards compliance records for each dealership. Tracks facility standard type (CI — Corporate Identity, signage, showroom, service drive, EV charging infrastructure), assessment date, compliance status, deficiency items, remediation deadline, and compliance score. Used for franchise renewal decisions and facility investment planning.'
}

Table dealer.region {
  region_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the dealer region. Primary key for the dealer region entity.']
  jurisdiction_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.jurisdiction. Business justification: Regional performance and compliance metrics are governed by jurisdiction; the link supports region‑level regulatory analytics.']
  allocation_quota_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of total OEM production allocated to this region. Sum of all region percentages equals 100%. Used for production planning and dealer inventory distribution.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date when the current region configuration or most recent change was formally approved by management.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or employee ID of the executive who approved the current region configuration or most recent change.']
  city_list varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of major cities included in this dealer region for reference and marketing purposes.']
  competitive_intensity_rating varchar [note: 'Assessment of competitive pressure from other OEMs in this region. Influences incentive programs and marketing investment.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code representing the primary country for this region (e.g., USA, CAN, MEX).']
  county_region_list varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of county or regional administrative divisions included in this dealer region for granular territory definition.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this dealer region record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage.']
  dealer_count varchar [note: 'Total number of active dealerships assigned to this region. Used for capacity planning and regional performance benchmarking.']
  district_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the district within the region. Districts are sub-regional organizational units that group dealerships for field management.']
  district_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the district within the region.']
  dms_integration_status varchar [note: 'Status of integration between this region and the CDK Global DMS. Active indicates successful data synchronization; error indicates integration issues requiring attention.']
  dms_last_sync_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent successful data synchronization between this region and the CDK Global DMS. Used for monitoring integration health.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when this region configuration ceased to be effective. Null for currently active regions. Used for historical analysis and territory reorganization tracking.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when this region configuration became effective. Used for historical tracking and territory change management.']
  geographic_boundary_description varchar [note: 'Textual description of the geographic boundaries defining this region (e.g., bordered by Interstate 5 to the west, state line to the east). Used when precise coordinates are not available.']
  home_delivery_available_flag boolean [note: 'Whether home delivery service is available in this region.']
  household_count_estimate varchar [note: 'Estimated number of households within the region. Used for market penetration and vehicle ownership forecasting.']
  incentive_program_eligibility_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether dealers in this region are eligible for special OEM incentive programs. True if eligible, false otherwise.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this dealer region record was most recently updated. Used for change tracking and data quality monitoring.']
  last_review_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent formal review of this regions boundaries, performance targets, and organizational alignment.']
  market_segment_classification varchar [note: 'Classification of the regions primary market segment based on population density and economic characteristics. Influences product mix and marketing strategy.']
  modification_reason varchar [note: 'Business justification for the most recent change to this regions configuration (e.g., market consolidation, dealer network expansion, territory realignment).']
  next_review_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next formal review of this regions configuration and performance. Used for governance and planning cycles.']
  performance_benchmark_group varchar [note: 'Code identifying the peer group used for performance benchmarking. Regions in the same group are compared for scorecard evaluation.']
  population_estimate varchar [note: 'Estimated total population within the geographic boundaries of this region. Used for market sizing and sales potential analysis.']
  postal_code_range varchar [note: 'Description of postal code ranges covered by this region (e.g., 90000-93999 for Southern California). Used for territory assignment and customer routing.']
  region_code varchar [note: 'Short alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the region within the OEM organizational hierarchy. Used for reporting and system integration.']
  region_name varchar [note: 'Full business name of the dealer region (e.g., Northeast Region, California Coastal, Mid-Atlantic).']
  region_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the dealer region. Active regions are operational; inactive regions are temporarily suspended; pending regions are being established; consolidating regions are merging with others; dissolved regions have been permanently closed.']
  region_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the region based on organizational structure: geographic (physical territory), market (customer segment focus), strategic (special initiative), or operational (functional alignment).']
  regional_manager_email varchar [note: 'Primary email address of the regional manager for business communication and escalation.']
  regional_manager_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the regional manager assigned to oversee dealer performance, allocation, and field operations in this region.']
  regional_manager_phone varchar [note: 'Primary phone number of the regional manager for dealer support and escalation.']
  sales_potential_index decimal(5,2) [note: 'Indexed score (0-100) representing the relative sales potential of this region compared to national average. Used for allocation planning and performance benchmarking.']
  salesforce_region_code varchar [note: 'External identifier for this region in the Salesforce Automotive Cloud system. Used for cross-system reconciliation and dealer portal integration.']
  sap_sales_org_code varchar [note: 'SAP S/4HANA sales organization code mapped to this dealer region. Used for order processing, billing, and financial reporting integration.']
  special_program_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes describing any special programs, initiatives, or considerations applicable to this region (e.g., EV infrastructure pilot, fleet sales focus).']
  state_province_list varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of state or province codes covered by this region (e.g., CA,NV,AZ for a Western US region).']
  vehicle_allocation_priority varchar [note: 'Priority ranking (1=highest) for vehicle allocation to this region during supply constraints. Based on market size, strategic importance, and historical performance.']
  zone_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the parent zone to which this region belongs. Zones are higher-level organizational units that aggregate multiple regions.']
  zone_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the parent zone (e.g., Eastern Zone, Western Zone, Central Zone).']

  Note: 'OEM regional and zone organizational hierarchy for dealer network management. Defines region name, zone, district, regional manager assignment, geographic boundaries, and the set of dealerships within each region. Used for regional performance aggregation, allocation management, and field sales team alignment.'
}

Table dealer.used_vehicle_appraisal {
  used_vehicle_appraisal_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the used vehicle appraisal record. Primary key for this entity.']
  appraiser_employee_id varchar [note: 'Employee identifier of the dealership staff member who conducted the appraisal.']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.sku. Business justification: Appraisal systems need the SKU to derive baseline market value, warranty status, and compliance data.']
  used_customer_party_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the customer who owns the vehicle being appraised for trade-in or sale.']
  used_dealer_dealership_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the dealership where the appraisal was conducted.']
  used_dealership_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the dealership where the appraisal was conducted.']
  used_party_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the customer who owns the vehicle being appraised for trade-in or sale.']
  accident_history_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the vehicle has a reported accident history based on vehicle history report or customer disclosure.']
  appraisal_date date [note: 'Date when the vehicle appraisal was conducted at the dealership.']
  appraisal_number varchar [note: 'Unique business identifier for the appraisal transaction, typically generated by the DMS (Dealer Management System) or appraisal tool.']
  appraisal_source_channel varchar [note: 'Channel through which the appraisal was initiated: walk_in (customer walked into dealership), appointment (scheduled appraisal), online_submission (customer submitted online), phone_inquiry (customer called), service_drive (initiated during service visit).']
  appraisal_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the appraisal: pending (in progress), completed (appraisal finished), accepted (customer accepted trade-in offer), declined (customer declined offer), expired (offer validity period lapsed).']
  appraisal_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise date and time when the appraisal was completed, including time zone information.']
  appraisal_tool_used varchar [note: 'Third-party valuation tool or system used to determine vehicle value: Manheim MMR (Manheim Market Report), Black Book, KBB (Kelley Blue Book), NADA (National Automobile Dealers Association), Galves, or internal proprietary tool.']
  appraised_value_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Final appraised value determined by the dealer after considering market value, vehicle condition, and local market factors.']
  appraiser_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the dealership employee who performed the appraisal.']
  cpo_listing_id varchar [note: 'FK to the resulting CPO listing if the appraised vehicle was certified.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this appraisal record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary amounts in this appraisal (e.g., USD, CAD, EUR).']
  customer_requested_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Trade-in value amount initially requested or expected by the customer, if disclosed during appraisal.']
  disposition_decision varchar [note: 'Final decision on the disposition of the appraised vehicle: accepted_trade (customer accepted trade-in offer), declined_trade (customer declined offer), wholesale (vehicle to be sent to auction), retail_inventory (vehicle to be added to dealer used inventory), pending (decision not yet made).']
  equity_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Calculated equity in the vehicle (appraised value minus payoff amount). Positive equity means the vehicle is worth more than owed; negative equity means the customer owes more than the vehicle is worth.']
  exterior_condition_notes varchar [note: 'Detailed notes on the exterior condition, including paint quality, body damage, dents, scratches, rust, and glass condition.']
  home_appraisal_flag boolean [note: 'Whether the appraisal was conducted at the customer home.']
  interior_condition_notes varchar [note: 'Detailed notes on the interior condition, including upholstery wear, dashboard condition, electronics functionality, and cleanliness.']
  linked_deal_number varchar [note: 'Deal or sales transaction number in the DMS (Dealer Management System) if this appraisal was linked to a new or used vehicle purchase.']
  market_value_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Estimated market value of the vehicle based on the appraisal tool, representing the fair market price for retail or wholesale.']
  mechanical_condition_notes varchar [note: 'Detailed notes on the mechanical condition of the vehicle, including engine, transmission, brakes, suspension, and other systems.']
  model_year varchar [note: 'Model year of the vehicle being appraised, representing the year designation assigned by the manufacturer.']
  odometer_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the odometer reading: miles or kilometers.']
  offer_expiration_date date [note: 'Date when the trade-in offer expires and is no longer valid, typically 3-7 days from appraisal date.']
  payoff_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Outstanding loan or lease payoff amount owed by the customer on the vehicle being appraised, if applicable. This is customer financial information.']
  reconditioning_cost_estimate decimal(15,2) [note: 'Estimated cost to recondition the vehicle for resale, including repairs, detailing, and PDI (Pre-Delivery Inspection) work.']
  retail_estimate_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Estimated retail asking price if the vehicle were to be added to dealer used vehicle inventory.']
  service_records_available_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the customer provided documented service and maintenance records for the vehicle.']
  tire_condition varchar [note: 'Assessment of tire condition and remaining tread life: excellent (like new), good (adequate tread), fair (moderate wear), poor (low tread), replacement_needed (below safe threshold).']
  title_status varchar [note: 'Legal title status of the vehicle: clean (no issues), salvage (total loss), rebuilt (repaired salvage), lemon (manufacturer buyback), flood (flood damage), hail (hail damage).']
  trade_in_allowance_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Trade-in allowance amount offered to the customer, which may differ from appraised value due to negotiation or deal structuring in F&I (Finance and Insurance) process.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this appraisal record was last modified or updated.']
  vin varchar [note: '17-character Vehicle Identification Number uniquely identifying the vehicle being appraised. VIN is considered PII as it can be traced to an individual owner.']
  wholesale_estimate_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Estimated wholesale auction value if the vehicle were to be sent to wholesale rather than retailed.']

  Note: 'Trade-in and used vehicle appraisal records conducted at the dealership. Captures appraisal date, VIN, year/make/model, mileage, condition grade, appraiser, appraisal tool used (e.g., Manheim, Black Book, KBB), appraised value, trade-in allowance offered, and disposition decision (accepted trade, declined, wholesale). Supports F&I deal structuring and used vehicle inventory acquisition.'
}

Table dealer.dealership_quality_assessment {
  dealership_quality_assessment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key']
  dealership_id varchar [note: 'FK to dealership']
  region_id varchar [note: 'Region the dealership belongs to for assessment grouping']
  standard_id varchar [note: 'FK to quality standard']
  assessment_date date [note: 'Date of assessment']
  assessment_type varchar [note: 'Type of assessment (initial, periodic, follow-up)']
  assessor_name varchar [note: 'Name of assessor']
  audit_duration_hours decimal(5,1) [note: 'Duration of the quality assessment audit in hours']
  certification_expiry_date date [note: 'Certification expiry date']
  compliance_score decimal [note: 'Overall compliance score']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Compliance status']
  corrective_action_count varchar [note: 'Number of corrective actions required']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Record creation timestamp']
  customer_experience_score decimal [note: 'Customer experience score']
  deficiency_count varchar [note: 'Number of deficiencies found']
  delivery_process_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Score for the dealership delivery process quality.']
  digital_readiness_score decimal [note: 'Score for digital/online capabilities assessment']
  environmental_compliance_score decimal [note: 'Score for environmental/sustainability compliance']
  ev_readiness_score decimal [note: 'Score for EV sales and service readiness']
  facility_score decimal [note: 'Facility condition score']
  findings_summary varchar [note: 'Summary of findings']
  improvement_plan_due_date date [note: 'Due date for improvement plan']
  is_certified boolean [note: 'Whether dealership is certified']
  next_assessment_date date [note: 'Next scheduled assessment date']
  overall_rating varchar [note: 'Overall rating (gold, silver, bronze)']
  parts_score decimal [note: 'Parts department score']
  previous_assessment_date date [note: 'Date of previous assessment']
  process_score decimal [note: 'Process adherence score']
  sales_score decimal [note: 'Sales department score']
  service_score decimal [note: 'Service department score']
  training_compliance_score decimal [note: 'Score for staff training compliance']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of last update to assessment record']

  Note: 'This association captures each compliance assessment linking a dealership to a quality standard. It records when the assessment occurred, its type, the resulting compliance status, score, and any deficiencies identified.. Existence Justification: Dealerships must comply with multiple quality standards, and each quality standard applies to many dealerships. The compliance process is captured in assessment records that include dates, types, scores, and deficiency counts, which are actively created, updated, and deleted by quality teams.'
}

Table aftersales.feature_content {
  feature_content_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the feature content record. Primary key.']
  aftersales_trim_level_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.trim_level. Business justification: Feature content is defined per trim level; linking provides a proper hierarchy and removes any need for duplicate trim identifiers in feature_content.']
  design_specification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to engineering.design_specification. Business justification: Feature‑to‑Design traceability matrix requires linking each product feature to its engineering design specification for validation and regulatory compliance.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this feature content record was approved for publication. Used for audit trail and compliance verification.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'User ID or name of the product manager or marketing manager who approved this feature content for publication. Used for accountability and content governance.']
  availability_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the feature is included as standard equipment, available as a standalone option, only available within a package, or not available for a given trim or market.']
  content_source_code varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of this feature content record in the source system, used for traceability and data lineage.']
  content_source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the source system from which this feature content record originates, such as PLM (Product Lifecycle Management), PIM (Product Information Management), or marketing content management system.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this feature content record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage.']
  customer_demand_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Quantitative score (0-100) representing customer demand or take-rate for this feature based on market research, configurator analytics, and sales data. Used for product planning and inventory optimization.']
  dealer_cost_impact_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The incremental dealer invoice cost when this feature is included. Used for dealer margin calculations and incentive program design.']
  ecu_dependency_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this feature requires specific ECU (Electronic Control Unit) hardware or software configuration. Used for manufacturing planning and service diagnostics.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'The date when this feature content record is no longer effective. Null if the record is currently active with no planned end date.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'The date when this feature content record becomes effective and available for use in configurators, ordering systems, and marketing materials.']
  feature_category varchar [note: 'High-level classification of the feature grouping for marketing segmentation and consumer navigation. Categories align with consumer decision-making patterns.']
  feature_code varchar [note: 'Standardized alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the feature within the product catalog system. Used for cross-system integration and configurator mapping.']
  feature_description_long varchar [note: 'Comprehensive consumer-facing description of the feature including benefits, technical highlights, and usage scenarios. Used in brochures, website detail pages, and dealer training materials.']
  feature_description_short varchar [note: 'Brief consumer-facing description of the feature, typically 50-100 characters, used in configurator tooltips and mobile applications.']
  feature_name varchar [note: 'Consumer-facing marketing name of the feature as displayed in brochures, window stickers, and online configurators.']
  feature_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the feature content record. Active features are available for configuration; discontinued features are retained for historical reference; pending features are awaiting approval; under_review features are being evaluated for changes.']
  feature_subcategory varchar [note: 'Detailed sub-classification within the feature category for granular filtering and reporting (e.g., within safety: active safety, passive safety, driver assistance).']
  field_service_info varchar [note: 'Field service related information, consolidating field service concepts.']
  fuel_economy_impact_percent decimal(18,2) [note: 'The estimated percentage impact on fuel economy (positive or negative) when this feature is included. Used for EPA fuel economy label calculations and CAFE compliance.']
  installation_time_minutes varchar [note: 'Standard time in minutes required to install this feature on the production line. Used for manufacturing capacity planning and line balancing in MES (Manufacturing Execution System).']
  is_adas_feature boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this feature is classified as an ADAS (Advanced Driver Assistance Systems) technology, such as adaptive cruise control, lane keeping assist, or automated emergency braking.']
  is_ev_specific boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this feature is specific to electric vehicle (EV) or plug-in hybrid electric vehicle (PHEV) powertrains, such as regenerative braking display, charging port, or battery thermal management.']
  is_hev_specific boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this feature is specific to hybrid electric vehicle (HEV) powertrains, such as hybrid system display, EV mode selector, or hybrid battery cooling.']
  is_regulatory_mandated boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this feature is mandated by regulatory requirements such as NHTSA Federal Motor Vehicle Safety Standards (FMVSS), EPA emissions standards, or regional safety regulations (e.g., backup camera, tire pressure monitoring system).']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this feature content record was last modified. Used for change tracking and synchronization with downstream systems.']
  market_availability_region varchar [note: 'Geographic region or market where this feature is available, using standardized region codes (e.g., NAM for North America, EUR for Europe, CHN for China). Multiple regions may be pipe-separated.']
  marketing_priority_rank varchar [note: 'Numerical ranking indicating the marketing priority of this feature for promotional campaigns and sales emphasis. Lower numbers indicate higher priority.']
  model_year_discontinued varchar [note: 'The model year (MY) when this feature was discontinued or phased out. Null if the feature is still active in the current lineup.']
  model_year_introduced varchar [note: 'The model year (MY) when this feature was first introduced into the product lineup. Used for product evolution tracking and historical analysis.']
  msrp_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the MSRP impact amount (e.g., USD, EUR, JPY).']
  msrp_impact_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The incremental MSRP (Manufacturer Suggested Retail Price) value added when this feature is included as an option. Zero for standard features. Used for pricing calculations and configurator display.']
  ota_updateable_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this feature can be enabled, updated, or modified via OTA (Over-the-Air) software updates post-production.']
  regulatory_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific regulatory standard or requirement that mandates this feature, such as FMVSS number, EPA regulation code, or Euro NCAP protocol. Populated only when is_regulatory_mandated is true.']
  safety_rating_impact varchar [note: 'Indicates the level of impact this feature has on vehicle safety ratings such as NCAP (New Car Assessment Programme) star ratings or IIHS Top Safety Pick awards.']
  supplier_name varchar [note: 'Name of the primary supplier or OEM (Original Equipment Manufacturer) partner providing the component or system that delivers this feature. Used for supply chain traceability and warranty management.']
  technology_level varchar [note: 'Classification of the feature\'s technology sophistication level, used for market positioning and trim-level differentiation strategies.']
  warranty_extension_months varchar [note: 'Number of additional warranty months provided specifically for this feature beyond the base vehicle warranty. Zero if no extension applies.']
  weight_impact_kg decimal(8,2) [note: 'The incremental vehicle weight in kilograms added by this feature. Used for CAFE (Corporate Average Fuel Economy) calculations, performance modeling, and engineering analysis.']

  Note: 'Defines the commercial feature content associated with a trim level or option package — the consumer-facing feature descriptions used in marketing, window stickers, and configurators. Captures feature code, feature name, feature category (safety/technology/comfort/performance/exterior/interior), feature description, standard vs. optional flag, ADAS (Advanced Driver Assistance Systems) classification flag, EV/HEV-specific flag, regulatory-mandated flag, and content source system reference. Bridges the commercial product catalog to consumer-facing communications.'
}

Table aftersales.program_milestone {
  program_milestone_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the program milestone record. Primary key for the program milestone entity.']
  aftersales_model_year_program_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent MY (Model Year) program to which this milestone belongs. Links to the program master entity.']
  nameplate_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the vehicle nameplate (brand/model) associated with this program milestone. Links to nameplate master data in the product domain.']
  plant_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the manufacturing plant where this milestone applies. Relevant for production-related milestones (SOP, Job 1, EOP). Null for program-level milestones. Links to plant master data.']
  predecessor_milestone_program_milestone_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the immediately preceding milestone that must be achieved before this milestone can begin. Null for the first milestone in the program sequence. Enables critical path analysis.']
  program_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the employee or program manager accountable for delivering this milestone. Links to workforce/employee master data.']
  program_responsible_person_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the employee or program manager accountable for delivering this milestone. Links to workforce/employee master data.']
  actual_date date [note: 'Actual date the milestone was achieved. Populated when milestone_status transitions to achieved. Used for variance analysis and lessons learned.']
  baseline_date date [note: 'Approved baseline date after program approval or re-baseline. Used as the official target for performance measurement and variance calculation.']
  budget_impact_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Estimated financial impact (in base currency) if this milestone is delayed. Used for prioritization and executive escalation. Null if no quantified impact.']
  completion_percentage decimal(18,2) [note: 'Estimated percentage of work completed toward achieving this milestone (0.00 to 100.00). Updated during program reviews. 100.00 when milestone_status is achieved.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this milestone record was first created in the system. Audit trail for data lineage.']
  critical_path_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this milestone is on the program critical path. True if any delay to this milestone will delay the overall program SOP date. Used for prioritization and risk management.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for budget_impact_amount (e.g., USD, EUR, JPY). Null if budget_impact_amount is null.']
  deliverable_completed_count varchar [note: 'Number of deliverables that have been completed and approved. Used to calculate completion_percentage and assess readiness for gate review.']
  deliverable_count varchar [note: 'Number of formal deliverables (documents, approvals, test results) required to achieve this milestone. Used to track completion progress.']
  external_dependency_description varchar [note: 'Description of external dependencies, including supplier names, partner organizations, or regulatory agencies. Null if external_dependency_flag is false.']
  external_dependency_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this milestone depends on external parties (suppliers, partners, regulatory agencies) for completion. True if external coordination required, false if fully internal.']
  field_service_info varchar [note: 'Field service related information, consolidating field service concepts.']
  forecast_date date [note: 'Current forecast date for milestone achievement based on latest program status. Updated regularly during program reviews. Null if milestone already achieved.']
  gate_review_comments varchar [note: 'Executive comments, conditions, or action items resulting from the gate review meeting. Captures key decisions and follow-up requirements.']
  gate_review_date date [note: 'Date the formal gate review meeting was held. Null if gate_review_required is false or review not yet conducted.']
  gate_review_outcome varchar [note: 'Result of the formal gate review meeting: approved (proceed to next phase), approved_with_conditions (proceed with action items), deferred (reschedule review), rejected (do not proceed). Null if gate_review_required is false or review not yet held.']
  gate_review_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a formal gate review (executive approval meeting) is required before this milestone can be marked as achieved. True for major program gates (e.g., Program Approval, SOP), false for intermediate checkpoints.']
  milestone_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code identifying the milestone type (e.g., PROG_APPR, DESIGN_FREEZE, PPAP_COMP, SOP, JOB1, EOP). Used for system integration and reporting.']
  milestone_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the milestone (e.g., Program Approval, Design Freeze, PPAP Completion, Start of Production, Job 1, End of Production).']
  milestone_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the milestone: pending (not started), in_progress (activities underway), achieved (completed on time), delayed (missed target), waived (skipped by exception), cancelled (no longer required).']
  milestone_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the milestone by functional category: program_gate (strategic approval), design_gate (engineering freeze), quality_gate (PPAP/APQP), production_gate (SOP/EOP), regulatory_gate (homologation), commercial_gate (launch readiness).']
  mitigation_plan varchar [note: 'Action plan to address identified risks and recover schedule. Includes specific actions, owners, and target dates. Null if risk_level is low.']
  model_year varchar [note: 'Model Year for which this program milestone applies (e.g., 2024, 2025). Aligns with automotive industry MY planning cycles.']
  modified_by varchar [note: 'User ID or system account that last modified this milestone record. Audit trail for data governance.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this milestone record was last updated. Audit trail for change tracking.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional notes, context, or historical information about this milestone. Free-text field for program team collaboration.']
  planned_date date [note: 'Originally planned target date for achieving this milestone, as defined in the program master schedule. Baseline for variance tracking.']
  program_phase varchar [note: 'High-level program phase in which this milestone occurs: concept (initial planning), design (engineering), development (prototyping), validation (testing/PPAP), launch (SOP/Job 1), production (volume ramp), end_of_life (EOP). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: concept|design|development|validation|launch|production|end_of_life — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  regulatory_body varchar [note: 'Name of the regulatory authority or governing body requiring this milestone (e.g., NHTSA, EPA, Euro NCAP, CARB, UNECE). Null if regulatory_requirement_flag is false.']
  regulatory_requirement_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this milestone is driven by regulatory or homologation requirements (e.g., NHTSA, EPA, Euro NCAP, WLTP certification). True for regulatory gates, false for internal program milestones.']
  responsible_function varchar [note: 'Primary functional organization accountable for achieving this milestone: engineering (design/CAE), manufacturing (plant readiness), quality (PPAP/APQP), supply_chain (supplier readiness), regulatory (homologation), commercial (launch), program_management (gate reviews). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: engineering|manufacturing|quality|supply_chain|regulatory|commercial|program_management — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  risk_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of risks, issues, or blockers that may prevent timely achievement of this milestone. Updated during program status reviews.']
  risk_level varchar [note: 'Current risk assessment for achieving this milestone on time: low (on track, no issues), medium (minor concerns), high (significant risk of delay), critical (delay highly likely or already occurred).']
  variance_days varchar [note: 'Number of calendar days between baseline_date and actual_date. Positive values indicate delay, negative values indicate early completion. Null if milestone not yet achieved.']
  waiver_approved_by varchar [note: 'Reference to the executive who approved the milestone waiver. Links to workforce/employee master data. Null if milestone not waived.']
  waiver_approved_date date [note: 'Date the milestone waiver was formally approved. Null if milestone not waived.']
  waiver_reason varchar [note: 'Business justification for waiving this milestone. Populated when milestone_status is set to waived. Requires executive approval.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User ID or system account that created this milestone record. Audit trail for data governance.']

  Note: 'Tracks key program milestones and gate reviews for each MY program (e.g., Program Approval, Design Freeze, PPAP completion, SOP, Job 1, EOP). Captures milestone type, planned date, actual date, milestone status (pending/achieved/delayed/waived), responsible function, gate review outcome, and variance days. Enables program management tracking, cross-functional alignment, and executive reporting on program health.'
}

Table aftersales.product_engineering_change {
  product_engineering_change_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the product_engineering_change data product (auto-inserted pre-linking).']
  aftersales_model_year_program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.model_year_program. Business justification: Engineering changes are scoped to a model year program; linking provides traceability and prevents orphan change records.']
  engineer_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Engineering change requests need the responsible engineer employee for traceability in change management system.']
  nameplate_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to aftersales.aftersales_nameplate. Business justification: Engineering changes (ECNs/ECOs) in the aftersales domain impact specific vehicle nameplates. Adding nameplate_id FK to aftersales_nameplate enables direct traceability from a commercial product engine']
  affected_part_numbers varchar [note: 'Part numbers affected by the change']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date the engineering change was formally approved by the change control board']
  change_description varchar [note: 'Description of the engineering change']
  change_number varchar [note: 'Engineering change number']
  change_reason varchar
  change_status varchar
  change_title varchar
  change_type varchar [note: 'Type (design, material, process, supplier)']
  created_timestamp timestamp
  disposition varchar
  effective_date date [note: 'Date change becomes effective']
  field_service_info varchar [note: 'Field service related information, consolidating field service concepts.']
  implementation_cost_estimate decimal(18,2) [note: 'Estimated cost to implement the engineering change across affected vehicles and service parts']
  priority varchar
  priority_level varchar [note: 'Priority classification of the change (critical, high, medium, low) indicating urgency of implementation']
  product_engineering_change_description varchar [note: 'Description of the entity']
  product_engineering_change_status varchar [note: 'Status (proposed, approved, implemented, closed)']
  reason_for_change varchar [note: 'Reason for the engineering change']
  service_impact varchar [note: 'Impact on aftersales service operations']
  superseded_part_number varchar [note: 'Part number being superseded']
  updated_timestamp timestamp

  Note: 'Commercial product domain record of engineering changes (ECNs/ECOs) that impact the commercial product catalog — affecting BOM structures, SKU configurations, option availability, or MSRP pricing. Captures change request number, change type (cost reduction/quality improvement/regulatory/customer-driven), affected SKUs and BOM lines, effective MY and date, commercial impact assessment (price change/feature change/availability change), approval status, and Teamcenter PLM reference. Distinct from the engineering domain\'s full ECN lifecycle — this is the commercial impact view.'
}

Table aftersales.option_constraint {
  option_constraint_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the option constraint rule. Primary key.']
  aftersales_option_package_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the option or package that triggers this constraint rule. The \'if\' side of the constraint logic.']
  aftersales_trim_level_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific trim level to which this constraint applies. Nullable if constraint applies across all trims for the nameplate/MY.']
  nameplate_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the vehicle nameplate (brand/model family) to which this constraint applies.']
  primary_aftersales_option_package_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the option or package that triggers this constraint rule. The \'if\' side of the constraint logic.']
  target_option_aftersales_option_package_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the option or package that is affected by this constraint rule. The \'then\' side of the constraint logic.']
  target_option_option_package_aftersales_option_package_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the option or package that is affected by this constraint rule. The \'then\' side of the constraint logic.']
  applies_to_fleet boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this constraint applies to fleet orders. Some constraints are relaxed or different for high-volume fleet customers.']
  applies_to_retail boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this constraint applies to retail customer orders through dealer networks.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Workflow approval status for this constraint rule. Only approved constraints are activated in production ordering systems.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this constraint rule was approved for production use.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'User identifier or name of the product manager or engineering lead who approved this constraint for production use.']
  bom_impact varchar [note: 'Indicates the severity of Bill of Materials changes triggered by this constraint. Critical impacts may require PPAP or APQP review.']
  change_request_number varchar [note: 'Reference to the Engineering Change Request or Product Change Notice that authorized the creation or modification of this constraint.']
  configurator_display_order varchar [note: 'Numeric sequence controlling the order in which constraint messages are displayed in consumer and dealer configurators.']
  constraint_code varchar [note: 'Business-readable code identifying this constraint rule, often used in dealer ordering systems and configurators.']
  constraint_description varchar [note: 'Detailed business explanation of why this constraint exists, including engineering, regulatory, or commercial rationale.']
  constraint_priority varchar [note: 'Numeric priority for resolving conflicts when multiple constraints apply to the same option combination. Lower numbers indicate higher priority.']
  constraint_source varchar [note: 'The business function or domain that originated this constraint rule (e.g., engineering for technical incompatibility, regulatory for compliance, commercial for pricing strategy).']
  constraint_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the constraint rule. Active constraints are enforced in ordering systems; inactive/deprecated constraints are retained for historical reference.']
  constraint_type varchar [note: 'The type of constraint relationship: requires (source option mandates target), excludes (source option prohibits target), includes (source option automatically bundles target), upgrades (source option supersedes target), replaces (source option substitutes target), prerequisite (target requires source to be selected first).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this constraint record was first created in the system.']
  effective_date date [note: 'The date from which this constraint rule becomes active and enforceable in dealer ordering and consumer configurator systems.']
  error_message varchar [note: 'User-facing error or warning message displayed in dealer ordering systems or consumer configurators when this constraint is violated.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'The date after which this constraint rule is no longer active. Nullable for constraints with indefinite validity.']
  field_service_info varchar [note: 'Field service related information, consolidating field service concepts.']
  fmea_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier to the FMEA document that analyzed the risk associated with this option constraint.']
  homologation_region varchar [note: 'Regulatory region code (e.g., EPA, CARB, EURO6, UNECE) for which this constraint is applicable. Some option combinations are prohibited or required by regional emissions or safety standards.']
  is_hard_constraint boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this is a hard constraint (cannot be violated under any circumstances) or a soft constraint (generates warning but can be overridden).']
  last_modified_by varchar [note: 'User identifier or name of the person who last modified this constraint rule.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to this constraint record.']
  lead_time_impact_days varchar [note: 'The number of additional production days required when this constraint is triggered, impacting On-Time Delivery (OTD) commitments.']
  manufacturing_complexity_score varchar [note: 'Numeric score (1-10) indicating the manufacturing complexity introduced by this constraint. Higher scores may impact production scheduling and Just-in-Sequence (JIS) logistics.']
  market_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO country or regional market code indicating where this constraint applies. Constraints may vary by market due to regulatory or feature availability differences.']
  model_year varchar [note: 'The model year for which this option constraint is applicable. Constraints may vary by MY due to engineering changes or regulatory updates.']
  msrp_impact_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The incremental MSRP change (positive or negative) resulting from this constraint being applied. Used for dynamic pricing in configurators.']
  msrp_impact_currency varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the MSRP impact amount.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional context, special instructions, or historical notes about this constraint rule.']
  override_allowed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether authorized users (e.g., fleet sales managers, product planners) can override this constraint in special circumstances.']
  override_approval_level varchar [note: 'The minimum authorization level required to override this constraint, if override_allowed is true.']
  ppap_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this constraint triggers a PPAP requirement due to significant engineering or supplier changes.']
  supplier_constraint boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this constraint is driven by supplier capacity, tooling, or material availability limitations.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User identifier or name of the product planner, engineer, or system administrator who created this constraint rule.']

  Note: 'Defines the business rules governing option and package compatibility — which options require, exclude, or are included with other options on a given trim level and MY. Captures constraint type (requires/excludes/includes/upgrades), source option/package, target option/package, applicable trim levels, applicable markets, effective date range, and constraint priority. Enforces valid vehicle configurations in dealer ordering systems and consumer configurators, preventing invalid build combinations.'
}

Table aftersales.fleet_spec {
  fleet_spec_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the fleet specification record. Primary key.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Fleet specification contracts are budgeted to a dedicated cost_center for tracking fleet‑specific expenses.']
  fleet_account_manager_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the sales or account manager responsible for this fleet specification and customer relationship. Primary point of contact for commercial and operational matters.']
  fleet_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the sales or account manager responsible for this fleet specification and customer relationship. Primary point of contact for commercial and operational matters.']
  nameplate_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the vehicle nameplate (model line) that this fleet specification is based on.']
  primary_customer_fleet_account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the fleet customer organization that this specification was negotiated for. Links to customer master data and account management.']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Fleet profitability (net price, discounts) is measured via a profit_center linked to each fleet_spec.']
  actual_volume_ytd varchar [note: 'Actual number of units ordered or delivered under this fleet specification in the current fiscal year. Used to track performance against volume commitments.']
  annual_volume_commitment varchar [note: 'Number of units the fleet customer has committed to purchase annually under this specification. Drives production planning and supplier commitments.']
  approved_option_packages varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of option package codes that are pre-approved for inclusion in this fleet specification. May include safety, technology, or utility packages negotiated at fleet pricing.']
  approved_trim_levels varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of trim level codes that are approved for this fleet specification. Defines the range of configurations available to the fleet customer.']
  contract_number varchar [note: 'Reference to the master fleet sales agreement or contract under which this specification is governed. Links to legal and commercial terms.']
  created_by_user varchar [note: 'User ID or username of the person who created this fleet specification record. Audit trail for accountability.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this fleet specification record was first created in the system. Audit trail for record lifecycle.']
  delivery_lead_time_days varchar [note: 'Committed lead time in days from order placement to vehicle delivery for this fleet specification. Service level commitment tied to production scheduling and logistics.']
  delivery_location_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the primary delivery location or distribution center for vehicles under this fleet specification. May reference a fleet customer facility or a designated delivery hub.']
  distribution_channel_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the distribution channel through which vehicles under this fleet specification are sold. Fleet sales typically use a dedicated channel distinct from retail.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date after which this fleet specification is no longer valid for new orders. May be extended through contract renewal or renegotiation.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date from which this fleet specification becomes valid and can be used for order placement. Aligns with contract execution and product availability.']
  emissions_standard varchar [note: 'Emissions standard that vehicles under this fleet specification must comply with. Examples: EPA Tier 3, Euro 6d, CARB LEV III.']
  field_service_info varchar [note: 'Field service related information, consolidating field service concepts.']
  fleet_customer_segment varchar [note: 'Classification of the fleet customer type. Determines applicable pricing structures, compliance requirements, and service level agreements.']
  fleet_discount_percentage decimal(18,2) [note: 'Negotiated discount percentage applied to the base MSRP for fleet orders. Confidential commercial term tied to volume commitments and customer segment.']
  fleet_msrp_base decimal(15,2) [note: 'Base MSRP for the fleet specification before discounts and volume adjustments. Represents the starting price point for fleet negotiations.']
  fleet_net_price decimal(18,2) [note: 'Final negotiated net price per unit after all discounts and adjustments. Confidential commercial term used for fleet order processing.']
  fleet_priority_rank varchar [note: 'Priority ranking for production scheduling and allocation when demand exceeds capacity. Higher priority fleet specs receive preferential treatment in build sequence planning.']
  fleet_spec_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the fleet specification, used in contracts and ordering systems. Unique alphanumeric code assigned to each negotiated fleet configuration.']
  homologation_region varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO country code or region code indicating the regulatory homologation region for vehicles under this fleet specification. Determines emissions, safety, and certification requirements.']
  last_modified_by_user varchar [note: 'User ID or username of the person who last modified this fleet specification record. Audit trail for change accountability.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this fleet specification record was last updated. Audit trail for change tracking and version control.']
  maximum_order_quantity varchar [note: 'Maximum number of units that can be ordered under this fleet specification within the contract period. Caps exposure and production allocation.']
  minimum_order_quantity varchar [note: 'Minimum number of units that must be ordered under this fleet specification to qualify for the negotiated pricing and terms. Enforces volume commitment.']
  model_year varchar [note: 'The model year for which this fleet specification is valid. Aligns with product lifecycle and homologation cycles.']
  payment_terms_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the negotiated payment terms for this fleet specification. Examples: Net 30, Net 60, progress billing, lease arrangement.']
  price_currency_code decimal(18,2) [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for all pricing fields in this fleet specification.']
  primary_use_case varchar [note: 'Description of the primary operational use case for vehicles under this fleet specification. Examples: patrol vehicles, delivery vans, executive transport, utility service trucks.']
  sales_organization_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the sales organization responsible for managing this fleet specification and customer relationship.']
  service_contract_included_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a prepaid service or maintenance contract is included as part of this fleet specification. Common for government and corporate fleet agreements.']
  spec_approval_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the fleet specification. Controls whether the spec can be used for order entry and production planning. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|pending_approval|approved|active|suspended|expired|cancelled — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
