  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'Fiscal year to which the billing run and generated invoices are assigned for financial reporting and period closing.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes and comments about the billing run execution, including special instructions, known issues, or manual interventions performed.']
  output_format varchar [note: 'Primary output format for invoices generated in this run: pdf (electronic document), xml (structured data), edi (EDI 810 invoice), print (physical mail), or email (direct delivery).']
  posting_date date [note: 'Date on which the billing run invoices are posted to the financial ledger in SAP FI, determining the accounting period for revenue recognition.']
  print_queue_name varchar [note: 'Name of the SAP output device or print queue to which invoices were sent for physical printing and mailing.']
  reversal_date date [note: 'Date on which the billing run was reversed and credit memos were issued to cancel the original invoices.']
  reversal_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this billing run was subsequently reversed or cancelled, requiring credit memo generation and financial adjustment.']
  reversal_reason_code varchar [note: 'Code indicating the reason for billing run reversal (e.g., pricing error, wrong billing date, duplicate run, system error).']
  run_number varchar [note: 'Human-readable business identifier for the billing run, typically formatted as BR-YYYYMMDD-NNNN for traceability and audit purposes.']
  run_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the billing run: scheduled (queued for execution), in_progress (actively processing), completed (successfully finished), failed (terminated with errors), cancelled (aborted by user), or partially_completed (finished with some errors).']
  run_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the billing run execution mode: periodic (scheduled recurring), manual (user-initiated), correction (error fix), rerun (retry after failure), ad_hoc (one-time special), or final (end-of-period closing).']
  sales_organization_code varchar [note: 'SAP SD sales organization responsible for the billing run, representing the commercial unit (e.g., North America Automotive, Europe Commercial Vehicles).']
  scheduled_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Planned date and time when the billing run was scheduled to begin execution, used for batch job planning and SLA tracking.']
  selection_criteria varchar [note: 'Business rules and filters applied to select billing-eligible documents for this run (e.g., customer group, sales order type, delivery date range, billing block status).']
  total_billed_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Sum of all net invoice amounts generated in this billing run, representing the total revenue recognized in the batch.']
  total_discount_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Sum of all discounts, rebates, and allowances applied across invoices in this run, used for pricing analytics and margin analysis.']
  total_gross_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Sum of all gross invoice amounts before discounts and taxes across all invoices in this run, representing the list-price revenue.']
  total_invoices_failed varchar [note: 'Number of invoice generation attempts that failed due to data errors, validation failures, or system issues during this run.']
  total_invoices_generated varchar [note: 'Total number of invoice documents successfully created during this billing run, used for volume tracking and reconciliation.']
  total_tax_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Sum of all tax amounts (VAT, sales tax, GST) across all invoices generated in this run, used for tax reporting and reconciliation.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the billing run record was last modified, capturing status changes, error updates, and completion events.']
  warning_count varchar [note: 'Total number of warnings generated during the billing run, indicating non-critical issues that did not prevent invoice creation but require review.']

  Note: 'Batch billing run record representing a scheduled or ad-hoc execution of the invoice generation process for a defined set of billing accounts, order types, or billing dates. Captures run ID, run type (periodic/manual/correction), billing date, billing category (vehicle_sales/parts/service/fleet/export), number of invoices generated, total billed amount, run status (scheduled/in_progress/completed/failed/cancelled), error count, run initiated by, and SAP SD billing due list execution reference (VF04). Provides operational traceability for mass billing cycles.'
}

Table billing.write_off {
  write_off_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the bad debt write-off record. Primary key.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the billing account from which the receivable is being written off.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Cost‑center linkage for write‑offs supports expense analysis of bad‑debt.']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Write‑off entries must be posted to a GL account; replaces denormalized code.']
  invoice_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the primary invoice associated with this write-off. Links to the original billing document.']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Profit‑center association for write‑offs enables profitability impact assessment.']
  vin_registry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to vehicle.vin_registry. Business justification: Write‑offs of vehicle invoices must be tied to the underlying vehicle for loss analysis and compliance reporting.']
  write_approval_authority_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the employee or manager who authorized the write-off. Links to workforce/employee master data.']
  write_customer_party_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the retail customer or fleet account associated with the written-off receivable. Links to customer master data.']
  write_dealer_dealership_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the dealership if the write-off relates to dealer invoicing or dealer-originated receivables. Nullable for direct retail transactions.']
  write_dealership_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the dealership if the write-off relates to dealer invoicing or dealer-originated receivables. Nullable for direct retail transactions.']
  write_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the employee or manager who authorized the write-off. Links to workforce/employee master data.']
  write_party_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the retail customer or fleet account associated with the written-off receivable. Links to customer master data.']
  amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The total monetary value of the receivable being written off. Represents the net uncollectable balance.']
  approval_authority_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the individual who approved the write-off, captured for audit trail purposes.']
  approval_date date [note: 'The date on which the write-off was formally approved by the authorized authority.']
  approval_level varchar [note: 'The organizational level at which the write-off was approved, typically based on write-off amount thresholds. Values: manager, director, vp (vice president), cfo (chief financial officer).']
  business_unit_code varchar [note: 'The business unit or division responsible for the original sale (e.g., vehicle sales, parts, service). Used for divisional bad debt reporting.']
  collection_agency_code varchar [note: 'Reference to the third-party collection agency assigned to pursue recovery of the written-off debt. Nullable if no agency was engaged.']
  collection_status varchar [note: 'Current status of collection efforts for the written-off debt. Values: not_assigned (no collection action), assigned (sent to agency), in_progress (active collection), recovered (payment received), closed (collection efforts ceased).']
  created_by_user varchar [note: 'The user ID or username of the system user who created the write-off record. Used for audit trail and accountability.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The system timestamp when the write-off record was first created in the database. Used for audit trail and data lineage.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the write-off amount (e.g., USD, EUR, JPY).']
  days_past_due_at_write_off varchar [note: 'The number of days the receivable was overdue at the time of write-off. Used for aging analysis and credit policy evaluation.']
  fiscal_period varchar [note: 'The fiscal period (month or quarter) within the fiscal year when the write-off was recorded.']
  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'The fiscal year in which the write-off was recorded, used for financial reporting and period analysis.']
  legal_case_reference varchar [note: 'Reference number for any associated legal case, bankruptcy filing, or court proceeding related to the write-off. Nullable if no legal action was involved.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional comments, supporting documentation references, or special circumstances related to the write-off decision.']
  original_due_date date [note: 'The original payment due date of the invoice before it became delinquent and was subsequently written off.']
  original_invoice_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The original gross amount of the invoice before any payments, credits, or write-offs were applied. Provides context for the write-off magnitude.']
  outstanding_balance_before_write_off decimal(18,2) [note: 'The receivable balance that was outstanding immediately before the write-off was applied. May differ from original invoice amount if partial payments were received.']
  posting_date date [note: 'The accounting period date when the write-off was posted to the general ledger. May differ from write_off_date for period-end adjustments.']
  reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code indicating the primary reason for the write-off. Values: bankruptcy (customer filed for bankruptcy), insolvency (customer insolvent), uncontactable (customer cannot be reached), statute_of_limitations (debt legally uncollectable), commercial_settlement (negotiated settlement), fraud (fraudulent transaction).']
  reason_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative explanation of the circumstances leading to the write-off decision. Provides additional context beyond the reason code.']
  recovery_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The monetary amount recovered after the write-off was recorded. Null if no recovery has occurred. Used to track bad debt recovery income.']
  recovery_date date [note: 'The date on which a recovery payment was received for a previously written-off receivable. Null if no recovery has occurred.']
  recovery_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator of whether any portion of the written-off amount was subsequently recovered. True if recovery occurred, False otherwise.']
  sales_region_code varchar [note: 'The sales region or geographic territory associated with the original sale. Used for regional bad debt analysis.']
  sap_accounting_document_number varchar [note: 'The SAP accounting document number for the general ledger posting of the write-off. May differ from the write-off document number in multi-step posting scenarios.']
  sap_write_off_document_number varchar [note: 'The SAP FI document number generated when the write-off was posted to the financial system. Used for reconciliation and audit trail.']
  updated_by_user varchar [note: 'The user ID or username of the system user who last modified the write-off record. Used for audit trail and accountability.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The system timestamp when the write-off record was last modified. Used for audit trail and change tracking.']
  write_off_category varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the write-off is for the full outstanding balance or a partial amount. Values: full (entire balance written off), partial (portion of balance written off).']
  write_off_date date [note: 'The date on which the formal decision to write off the uncollectable receivable was executed. This is the business event date for the write-off.']
  write_off_number varchar [note: 'Business-readable unique identifier for the write-off transaction, typically following organizational numbering convention (e.g., WO-20240001234).']

  Note: 'Bad debt write-off record documenting the formal decision to write off an uncollectable receivable balance from a billing account. Captures write-off date, billing account reference, original invoice references, write-off amount, write-off reason (bankruptcy/insolvency/uncontactable/statute_of_limitations/commercial_settlement), approval authority, approval date, write-off category (full/partial), recovery flag, recovery amount if subsequently collected, and SAP FI write-off document reference. Supports credit risk management and bad debt provisioning.'
}

Table billing.intercompany_invoice {
  intercompany_invoice_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the intercompany invoice record. Primary key.']
  issuing_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to billing.billing_account. Business justification: Intercompany invoices should reference the issuing billing account; the code and name become redundant.']
  vin_registry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to vehicle.vin_registry. Business justification: Intercompany invoices for vehicle transfers reference the VIN; FK supports inter‑entity reconciliation and regulatory reporting.']
  adjustment_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Any adjustments applied to the gross amount, such as discounts, rebates, or transfer pricing adjustments. Positive values increase the invoice; negative values decrease it.']
  cost_center varchar [note: 'Cost center code in SAP CO that bears the cost or revenue associated with this intercompany transaction. Used for internal cost allocation.']
  created_by_user varchar [note: 'User ID or name of the person who created this intercompany invoice record in the system. Used for accountability and audit purposes.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this intercompany invoice record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code in which the intercompany invoice is denominated (e.g., USD, EUR, JPY, CNY).']
  dispute_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator (True/False) showing whether the receiving entity has raised a dispute on this intercompany invoice due to pricing, quantity, or quality discrepancies.']
  dispute_reason varchar [note: 'Free-text description of the reason for dispute if dispute_flag is True. Captures details such as pricing disagreement, quantity mismatch, or quality issue.']
  distribution_channel varchar [note: 'SAP SD distribution channel through which the intercompany transaction flows (e.g., wholesale, retail, direct). Defines the route-to-market.']
  division varchar [note: 'SAP SD division representing the product line or business unit (e.g., passenger vehicles, commercial vehicles, parts). Used for segment reporting.']
  due_date date [note: 'Date by which the receiving entity is expected to settle the intercompany invoice amount.']
  fiscal_period varchar [note: 'Fiscal period (month or quarter) within the fiscal year when the invoice was issued. Typically 1-12 for monthly periods.']
  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'Fiscal year in which the intercompany invoice was issued, used for financial reporting and period-based reconciliation.']
  gl_account_code varchar [note: 'General ledger account code to which the intercompany invoice revenue or expense is posted. Aligns with the corporate chart of accounts.']
  gross_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total invoice amount before taxes, adjustments, or deductions. Represents the base transfer price or service charge.']
  intercompany_invoice_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the intercompany invoice: draft (being prepared), issued (sent to receiving entity), acknowledged (received and accepted), disputed (under review for discrepancies), settled (payment completed), cancelled (voided).']
  invoice_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique invoice number assigned to this intercompany transaction. Typically follows format IC followed by 10 digits.']
  issue_date date [note: 'Date when the intercompany invoice was formally issued by the issuing entity to the receiving entity.']
  model_year varchar [note: 'Model year of the vehicle or product being transferred. Applicable primarily to vehicle_transfer and CKD/SKD supply transactions.']
  net_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Final payable amount after applying taxes and adjustments. Calculated as gross_amount + tax_amount + adjustment_amount.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or comments related to this intercompany invoice, such as special instructions, approvals, or clarifications.']
  payment_method varchar [note: 'Method by which the receiving entity will settle the invoice: wire_transfer (bank wire), ach (automated clearing house), check (paper check), netting (multilateral netting arrangement), offset (offset against reciprocal invoices).']
  payment_terms_code varchar [note: 'Code representing the payment terms agreed between issuing and receiving entities (e.g., NET30, NET60, immediate). Defines the payment schedule and discount eligibility.']
  posting_date date [note: 'Date when the intercompany invoice was posted to the general ledger in both issuing and receiving entities.']
  profit_center varchar [note: 'Profit center code in SAP CO (Controlling) that captures the profitability impact of this intercompany transaction for internal management reporting.']
  quantity varchar [note: 'Number of units (vehicles, kits, or service units) covered by this intercompany invoice. Typically 1 for vehicle transfers, multiple for CKD/SKD kits or shared services.']
  receiving_entity_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the Automotive legal entity or subsidiary that receives and must pay this intercompany invoice. Typically 2-letter country code followed by 4-digit entity identifier.']
  receiving_entity_name varchar [note: 'Full legal name of the receiving entity or subsidiary.']
  reference_document_number varchar [note: 'External reference document number such as a purchase order, delivery note, or contract number that supports this intercompany invoice. Used for traceability.']
  sales_organization varchar [note: 'SAP SD sales organization code responsible for the intercompany transaction. Represents the selling unit within the issuing entity.']
  sap_fi_document_number varchar [note: 'SAP FI document number that records this intercompany invoice in the general ledger. Used for reconciliation and audit trail.']
  sap_intercompany_clearing_account varchar [note: 'General ledger account number used for intercompany clearing and reconciliation in SAP FI. Tracks outstanding balances between entities.']
  settlement_date date [note: 'Date when the intercompany invoice was fully settled (payment received and cleared). Null if not yet settled.']
  tax_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total tax amount applicable to this intercompany transaction, including VAT, GST, or other indirect taxes based on jurisdictional requirements.']
  tax_treatment_code varchar [note: 'Code indicating the tax treatment applicable to this intercompany transaction, such as VAT exemption, reverse charge, or standard rate. Aligns with jurisdictional tax regulations.']
  transaction_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the intercompany transaction: vehicle_transfer (finished vehicle transfer between entities), ckd_supply (Completely Knocked Down kit supply), skd_supply (Semi Knocked Down kit supply), royalty (intellectual property or brand royalty charges), management_fee (corporate services allocation), shared_service (shared service center charges).']
  transfer_price_basis varchar [note: 'Methodology used to determine the transfer price for this intercompany transaction: cost_plus (cost plus markup), market_price (prevailing market rate), arm_length (arms-length principle per OECD), negotiated (bilaterally agreed rate), comparable_uncontrolled (comparable uncontrolled price method).']
  transfer_pricing_policy_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the internal transfer pricing policy document or agreement that governs this transaction. Used for audit and compliance verification.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the quantity: EA (each, for vehicles), KIT (for CKD/SKD kits), HR (hours, for shared services), LOT (batch, for bulk transfers).']
  updated_by_user varchar [note: 'User ID or name of the person who last modified this intercompany invoice record. Used for accountability and audit purposes.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this intercompany invoice record was last modified. Used for audit trail and change tracking.']
  vehicle_model_code varchar [note: 'Internal model code identifying the vehicle platform or variant being transferred. Used for vehicle_transfer and kit supply transactions.']

  Note: 'Internal billing document issued between Automotive legal entities or subsidiaries for intercompany vehicle transfers, CKD/SKD kit supply, shared services, or royalty charges. Captures intercompany invoice number, issuing entity, receiving entity, transaction type (vehicle_transfer/CKD_supply/SKD_supply/royalty/management_fee/shared_service), transfer price basis (cost_plus/market_price/arm_length), amount, currency, applicable transfer pricing policy reference, tax treatment, and SAP FI intercompany document reference. Supports global intercompany reconciliation and transfer pricing compliance.'
}

Table billing.parts_service_charge {
  parts_service_charge_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the parts and service charge record. Primary key for this transaction.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to billing.billing_account. Business justification: Service charges are billed to a customer\'s account; linking enables service revenue attribution and account statements.']
  approver_employee_id varchar [note: 'User identifier of the person who approved this charge. Used for audit trail and approval workflow tracking.']
  invoice_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to billing.invoice. Business justification: Parts service charges are billed on an invoice; adding invoice_id creates the proper parent link.']
  parts_employee_id varchar [note: 'User identifier of the person who approved this charge. Used for audit trail and approval workflow tracking.']
  recall_campaign_id varchar [note: 'Identifier for the recall campaign if this charge is related to recall parts or repair work. Links charge to manufacturer recall program.']
  vin_registry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to vehicle.vin_registry. Business justification: Service charges are billed per vehicle; FK enables service history reconciliation and compliance with warranty regulations.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date when the charge was approved for billing. Marks transition from pending to approved status in the charge lifecycle.']
  billing_account_number varchar [note: 'Account number to which this charge is billed. May reference dealer account, retail customer account, or fleet account depending on charge type.']
  billing_date date [note: 'Date when the charge was formally billed to the customer or dealer. Marks the date of invoice generation.']
  cdk_repair_order_number varchar [note: 'CDK Global DMS repair order reference number. Source system identifier linking charge to the dealer management system repair order.']
  charge_date date [note: 'Date when the parts and service charge was created or incurred. Principal business event timestamp for this transaction.']
  charge_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the parts and service charge. Externally-known unique charge reference number used in billing statements and dispute resolution.']
  charge_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the charge in the billing workflow. Tracks progression from draft through approval, billing, payment, or dispute resolution. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|pending_approval|approved|billed|paid|disputed|cancelled — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  charge_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the charge based on service context. Distinguishes between warranty claims, retail service, fleet maintenance, recall campaigns, and Technical Service Bulletin (TSB) repairs. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: warranty_parts|warranty_labor|retail_parts|retail_labor|fleet_maintenance|recall_parts|tsb_repair — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  cost_center varchar [note: 'Cost center code for internal accounting allocation. Used for profitability analysis and departmental cost tracking.']
  created_by_user varchar [note: 'User identifier of the person or system that created this charge record. Audit field for accountability and traceability.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this charge record was first created in the system. Audit field for record creation tracking.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary amounts in this charge record. Ensures proper currency handling in multi-currency environments.']
  dealer_code varchar [note: 'Unique identifier for the dealership that performed the service and raised the charge. Links charge to the servicing dealer location.']
  discount_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total discount applied to the charge. May include promotional discounts, warranty coverage adjustments, or goodwill credits.']
  dispute_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this charge is currently under dispute. True if customer or dealer has contested the charge.']
  dispute_reason varchar [note: 'Reason code or description for charge dispute. Captures customer or dealer objection to the charge for resolution tracking.']
  fiscal_period varchar [note: 'Fiscal period (month) within the fiscal year for financial reporting. Enables monthly revenue tracking and variance analysis.']
  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'Fiscal year in which the charge was recognized for financial reporting. Used for period-based revenue analysis.']
  gl_account_code varchar [note: 'General ledger account code for revenue recognition. Maps charge to appropriate revenue account in the chart of accounts.']
  labor_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total monetary value of labor charged in this transaction before tax and adjustments. Represents the gross labor cost component.']
  labor_hours decimal(6,2) [note: 'Total labor hours charged for the service work. Used for labor cost calculation and technician productivity tracking.']
  labor_operation_code varchar [note: 'Standardized labor operation code identifying the type of service work performed. Used for labor time estimation and billing calculation.']
  labor_rate decimal(10,2) [note: 'Hourly labor rate applied to this charge. May vary by service type, warranty status, or dealer pricing policy.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or comments related to the charge. Captures additional context, special circumstances, or billing instructions.']
  part_number varchar [note: 'OEM (Original Equipment Manufacturer) part number for the primary part charged. References the parts catalog for the component installed or replaced.']
  parts_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total monetary value of parts charged in this transaction before tax and adjustments. Represents the gross parts cost component.']
  parts_quantity decimal(10,3) [note: 'Quantity of parts charged in this transaction. Supports fractional quantities for fluids, lubricants, and bulk materials.']
  profit_center varchar [note: 'Profit center code for internal management reporting. Enables P&L analysis by business unit or service line.']
  sap_billing_document_number varchar [note: 'SAP SD billing document number generated when this charge is invoiced. Links charge to the formal invoice in the ERP system.']
  service_date date [note: 'Date when the actual service work was performed. May differ from charge date or billing date depending on workflow timing.']
  service_order_number varchar [note: 'Reference to the originating service order or repair order that generated this charge. Links charge to the after-sales service event.']
  tax_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total tax amount applied to the parts and labor charges. Includes sales tax, VAT, or other applicable taxes based on jurisdiction.']
  total_charge_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Net total charge amount after applying parts, labor, tax, and discounts. Final billable amount for this charge record.']
  tsb_number varchar [note: 'Technical Service Bulletin number if this charge is related to a TSB repair. References manufacturer-issued service bulletin for known issues.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for parts quantity. Common values include EA (each), L (liter), KG (kilogram), M (meter).']
  updated_by_user varchar [note: 'User identifier of the person or system that last modified this charge record. Audit field for change accountability.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this charge record was last modified. Audit field for change tracking and data lineage.']
  warranty_claim_number varchar [note: 'Reference to the warranty claim if this charge is associated with warranty parts or labor. Links charge to warranty reimbursement process.']

  Note: 'Billing record for after-sales parts and service charges raised against dealer warranty claims, retail service invoices, or fleet maintenance billing. Captures charge number, charge type (warranty_parts/warranty_labor/retail_parts/retail_labor/fleet_maintenance/recall_parts/TSB_repair), service order reference, VIN reference, part numbers charged, labor operation codes, parts amount, labor amount, total charge, billing account, charge status, and CDK Global DMS repair order reference. Bridges aftersales service operations with billing settlement.'
}

Table billing.advance_payment {
  advance_payment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the advance payment record. Primary key.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'User ID or username of the person who created this advance payment record in the system.']
  invoice_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to billing.invoice. Business justification: Advance payments are often applied to a specific invoice; linking provides traceability.']
  payer_account_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the account making the advance payment. May reference dealer, fleet customer, or retail customer account.']
  vin_registry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to vehicle.vin_registry. Business justification: Advance payments are tied to a specific vehicle order; linking supports finance tracking and downstream invoicing.']
  advance_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total amount of the advance payment received from the payer.']
  advance_payment_number varchar [note: 'Business-facing unique reference number for the advance payment, used for external communication and tracking.']
  advance_type varchar [note: 'Type of advance payment: vehicle deposit, fleet prepayment, dealer advance, booking fee, order deposit, or down payment.']
  application_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the advance payment application: open (not yet applied), partially applied (some amount applied), fully applied (entire amount applied), refunded (returned to payer), or expired (no longer valid).']
  applied_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Cumulative amount of the advance payment that has been applied to invoices or final billing documents.']
  bank_reference_number varchar [note: 'Bank transaction reference or confirmation number for the advance payment, used for reconciliation with bank statements.']
  business_area varchar [note: 'Business area or division within the company that the advance payment is attributed to for internal reporting.']
  company_code varchar [note: 'SAP company code representing the legal entity that received the advance payment.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this advance payment record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO currency code for the advance payment amount (e.g., USD, EUR, JPY).']
  dealer_code varchar [note: 'Unique code identifying the dealership associated with this advance payment, applicable when payer is a dealer or when payment is for dealer inventory.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date after which the advance payment is no longer valid and may be subject to refund or forfeiture per contract terms.']
  fiscal_period varchar [note: 'Fiscal period (month) within the fiscal year when the advance payment was received.']
  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'Fiscal year in which the advance payment was received, used for financial period reporting.']
  gl_account_code varchar [note: 'General ledger account code where the advance payment liability is recorded, typically a special GL account for customer down payments.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or comments regarding the advance payment, including special instructions, exceptions, or additional context.']
  payer_entity_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the entity making the advance payment: dealer, fleet customer, retail customer, or corporate account.']
  payment_date date [note: 'Date when the advance payment was received from the payer.']
  payment_method varchar [note: 'Method used to make the advance payment: wire transfer, credit card, debit card, check, cash, ACH, or electronic funds transfer. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: wire_transfer|credit_card|debit_card|check|cash|ach|electronic_funds_transfer — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  payment_reference_number varchar [note: 'External reference number from the payment processor, bank, or payer system for traceability and reconciliation.']
  payment_terms_code varchar [note: 'Payment terms code applicable to the transaction for which the advance was received, defining due dates and discount conditions.']
  payment_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise timestamp when the advance payment transaction was recorded in the system.']
  posting_date date [note: 'Date when the advance payment was posted to the general ledger in the financial system.']
  profit_center varchar [note: 'Profit center responsible for the revenue associated with this advance payment, used for profitability analysis.']
  reconciliation_date date [note: 'Date when the advance payment was successfully reconciled with bank statements and confirmed in the financial system.']
  reconciliation_status varchar [note: 'Status of the advance payment reconciliation with bank statements and financial records: pending, reconciled, discrepancy, or under review.']
  refund_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Amount of the advance payment that has been refunded to the payer due to order cancellation or overpayment.']
  refund_date date [note: 'Date when the advance payment (or portion thereof) was refunded to the payer.']
  refund_reason_code varchar [note: 'Code indicating the reason for refunding the advance payment, such as order cancellation, overpayment, or customer request.']
  sales_order_number varchar [note: 'Reference to the sales order document for which this advance payment was received. Links the advance to the vehicle or parts order.']
  sales_region_code varchar [note: 'Geographic sales region code where the advance payment originated, used for regional revenue tracking.']
  sap_clearing_document varchar [note: 'SAP FI clearing document number used when the advance payment is applied against an invoice, clearing the down payment liability.']
  sap_down_payment_document varchar [note: 'SAP FI down payment document number (transaction F-29) that records this advance payment as a special GL transaction.']
  unapplied_balance decimal(18,2) [note: 'Remaining balance of the advance payment that has not yet been applied to invoices or refunded. Calculated as advance_amount minus applied_amount minus refund_amount.']
  updated_by_user varchar [note: 'User ID or username of the person who last modified this advance payment record in the system.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this advance payment record was last modified in the system.']
  value_date date [note: 'Date when the funds became available in the company bank account, used for cash flow and treasury management.']
  vehicle_order_number varchar [note: 'Specific vehicle order reference number associated with this advance payment, used for vehicle deposits and booking fees.']

  Note: 'Record of advance or deposit payments received from dealers or customers prior to invoice issuance, such as vehicle order deposits, fleet pre-payments, or dealer down payments. Captures advance payment date, payer account, advance type (vehicle_deposit/fleet_prepayment/dealer_advance/booking_fee), advance amount, currency, linked sales order or vehicle order reference, application status (open/partially_applied/fully_applied/refunded), applied amount, refund amount, and SAP FI down payment document reference (F-29). Ensures accurate revenue recognition and liability tracking.'
}

Table billing.block {
  block_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the billing block record. Primary key.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to billing.account. Business justification: Blocks can be applied at the account level; adding account_id creates a direct relationship and removes no redundant columns.']
  block_customer_party_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the customer account associated with the blocked billing document.']
  block_dealer_dealership_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the dealer associated with the blocked billing document, if applicable to dealer transactions.']
  block_employee_id varchar [note: 'User ID or system identifier of the person or automated process that applied the billing block.']
  block_party_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the customer account associated with the blocked billing document.']
  block_released_by_user_employee_id varchar [note: 'User ID or system identifier of the person or automated process that released the billing block.']
  dispute_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the customer dispute record associated with this billing block, if the block is dispute-related.']
  primary_block_employee_id varchar [note: 'User ID or system identifier of the person or automated process that applied the billing block.']
  recall_campaign_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.compliance_recall_campaign. Business justification: Billing blocks must reference active recall campaigns to prevent invoicing for recalled vehicles.']
  vin_registry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to vehicle.vin_registry. Business justification: Billing blocks are often placed on a particular vehicle for compliance or credit issues; FK allows block status reporting per VIN.']
  aging_days varchar [note: 'Number of days the billing block has been active since application, used for aging analysis and escalation triggers.']
  applied_by_user_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the user who applied the billing block, for business user reference and audit purposes.']
  applied_date date [note: 'Date on which the billing block was applied to the document, preventing invoice generation.']
  applied_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise timestamp when the billing block was applied, including time-of-day for audit and tracking purposes.']
  block_number varchar [note: 'Business-facing unique identifier or reference number for the billing block, used for tracking and communication purposes.']
  block_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the billing block. Active blocks prevent invoicing; released blocks have been cleared.']
  block_type varchar [note: 'Category of billing block applied. Indicates the reason category for preventing invoice generation.']
  blocked_document_number varchar [note: 'Reference number of the sales order, delivery, or billing account that is blocked from invoicing.']
  blocked_document_type varchar [note: 'Type of document to which the billing block is applied (e.g., sales order, delivery, billing account).']
  company_code varchar [note: 'SAP company code representing the legal entity to which the blocked billing document belongs.']
  compliance_hold_reason varchar [note: 'Specific compliance or regulatory reason for the billing hold, such as export control, sanctions screening, or regulatory investigation.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this billing block record was first created in the system, for audit trail and data lineage purposes.']
  credit_exposure_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total credit exposure amount for the customer at the time the billing block was applied, used in credit limit evaluation.']
  credit_limit_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Approved credit limit for the customer at the time of billing block application, used for credit hold decisions.']
  credit_limit_exceeded_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the billing block was triggered due to the customer exceeding their approved credit limit.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for credit exposure and limit amounts.']
  delivery_number varchar [note: 'Delivery document number associated with the billing block, if the block is applied at the delivery level.']
  distribution_channel varchar [note: 'SAP distribution channel through which the blocked billing document was processed (e.g., retail, fleet, wholesale).']
  division varchar [note: 'SAP division or product line associated with the blocked billing document (e.g., passenger vehicles, commercial vehicles, parts).']
  escalation_date date [note: 'Date on which the billing block was escalated for higher-level review or resolution.']
  escalation_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the billing block has been escalated to management or a specialized resolution team due to age or complexity.']
  expected_resolution_date date [note: 'Target date by which the billing block is expected to be resolved and released, used for SLA tracking and customer communication.']
  fiscal_period varchar [note: 'Fiscal period (month or quarter) within the fiscal year when the billing block was applied.']
  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'Fiscal year in which the billing block was applied, used for financial period reporting and analysis.']
  legal_hold_case_number varchar [note: 'Case or matter number for legal holds, linking the billing block to a specific legal proceeding or investigation.']
  notes varchar [note: 'General free-text notes or comments related to the billing block, capturing additional context, communications, or resolution actions.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Priority level assigned to the billing block for resolution workflow and escalation management.']
  reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code representing the specific reason for the billing block. Maps to SAP SD billing block reason master data.']
  reason_description varchar [note: 'Detailed textual description of the reason the billing block was applied, providing context for resolution.']
  release_date date [note: 'Date on which the billing block was released or cleared, allowing invoice generation to proceed.']
  release_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or comments entered by the user when releasing the billing block, documenting resolution actions taken.']
  release_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise timestamp when the billing block was released, including time-of-day for audit and SLA tracking.']
  released_by_user_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the user who released the billing block, for business user reference and audit purposes.']
  sales_order_number varchar [note: 'Sales order number associated with the blocked document, if applicable. Links the block to the originating sales transaction.']
  sales_organization varchar [note: 'SAP sales organization code responsible for the blocked billing document.']
  sap_billing_block_code varchar [note: 'SAP SD system billing block code reference. Maps to SAP configuration table for billing block types and controls.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this billing block record was last modified, for audit trail and change tracking purposes.']

  Note: 'Record of a billing block applied to a sales order, delivery, or billing account that prevents invoice generation until the block is resolved. Captures block type (credit_limit_exceeded/pricing_incomplete/legal_hold/compliance_hold/dispute_hold/manual_hold), blocked document reference, block applied date, block applied by, block reason description, release date, released by, and SAP SD billing block code reference. Supports credit management and compliance-driven invoice holds.'
}

Table billing.payment_plan {
  payment_plan_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for payment_plan']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to billing.account. Business justification: Accounts may have a default payment plan; adding the FK enables direct lookup.']
  superseded_payment_plan_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on payment_plan (superseded_payment_plan_id)']
  auto_renewal boolean [note: 'If true, the plan automatically renews at the end of its term.']
  billing_cycle varchar [note: 'Definition of the billing period used for invoicing.']
  billing_day_of_month varchar [note: 'Day of month on which recurring invoices are generated.']
  collection_status varchar [note: 'Current status of debt collection activities.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the payment plan record was first created.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO code of the currency used for the plan.']
  default_date date [note: 'Date when the plan entered default status.']
  defaulted_flag boolean [note: 'True if the plan is in default due to missed payments.']
  discount_rate decimal(5,4) [note: 'Percentage discount applied to the total amount.']
  down_payment_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Initial lump‑sum payment required at plan inception.']
  early_termination_fee decimal(12,2) [note: 'Fee charged if the plan is terminated before its effective end date.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when the payment plan terminates; null for open‑ended plans.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when the payment plan becomes binding.']
  eligibility_criteria varchar [note: 'Business rules that determine which customers may enroll in the plan.']
  grace_period_days varchar [note: 'Number of days after due date before penalties apply.']
  installment_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Amount due for each regular installment.']
  interest_rate decimal(5,4) [note: 'Annual percentage rate applied to the outstanding balance.']
  last_payment_date date [note: 'Date when the most recent payment was received.']
  late_fee_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Fixed fee applied when a payment is received after the grace period.']
  next_payment_due_date date [note: 'Date of the next scheduled payment.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or special instructions.']
  number_of_installments varchar [note: 'Total number of installments defined in the plan.']
  outstanding_balance decimal(12,2) [note: 'Remaining amount due under the plan.']
  payment_channel varchar [note: 'Channel through which the payment is initiated.']
  payment_frequency varchar [note: 'Regular interval at which payments are scheduled.']
  payment_method varchar [note: 'Instrument used to settle payments.']
  payment_plan_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the plan, including purpose and key terms.']
  payment_plan_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the payment plan.']
  penalty_rate decimal(5,4) [note: 'Percentage applied to overdue balances as a penalty.']
  plan_code varchar [note: 'External business code used to reference the payment plan in contracts and invoices.']
  plan_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the payment plan.']
  plan_type varchar [note: 'Category of the payment plan indicating the financing model.']
  promotional_code varchar [note: 'Code representing a marketing promotion linked to the plan.']
  recurring_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Amount that recurs each payment period after down payment.']
  renewal_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the plan is eligible for renewal.']
  tax_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Calculated tax amount based on tax_rate and taxable amount.']
  tax_exempt_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the plan is exempt from sales tax.']
  tax_rate decimal(5,4) [note: 'Applicable tax rate for the plan (if not tax‑exempt).']
  total_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Overall amount to be paid over the life of the plan.']
  total_paid_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Sum of all payments received to date.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the payment plan record.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for payment_plan. Referenced by payment_plan_id.'
}

Table billing.billing_period {
  billing_period_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for billing_period']
  previous_billing_period_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on billing_period (previous_billing_period_id)']
  billing_cycle_code varchar [note: 'Code representing the billing cycle associated with the period.']
  billing_frequency varchar [note: 'Frequency at which billing is generated for this period.']
  billing_period_description varchar [note: 'Free‑form description or notes about the period.']
  billing_period_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the period.']
  close_date date [note: 'Date on which the period was closed, if applicable.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the period record was first created.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code applicable to the period\'s financial amounts.']
  days_in_period varchar [note: 'Number of calendar days covered by the period.']
  fiscal_quarter varchar [note: 'Fiscal quarter identifier for the period.']
  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'Fiscal year to which the period belongs.']
  is_closed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the period has been closed for posting.']
  period_end_date date [note: 'Last calendar date of the billing period.']
  period_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the billing period, e.g., "2023‑04".']
  period_start_date date [note: 'First calendar date of the billing period.']
  period_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the period (e.g., regular monthly, quarterly, adjustment, forecast).']
  period_version varchar [note: 'Version number for the period definition, used when revisions occur.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the period record.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for billing_period. Referenced by billing_period_id.'
}

Table mobility.connected_vehicle {
  connected_vehicle_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for the connected vehicle record.']
  connected_fleet_mobility_fleet_account_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the fleet to which the vehicle belongs, if applicable.']
  connected_mobility_fleet_account_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the fleet to which the vehicle belongs, if applicable.']
  dealership_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to dealer.dealership. Business justification: Sales Attribution Report links each connected vehicle to the dealer that sold it, enabling dealer‑level warranty and service responsibility.']
  driver_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Fleet driver assignment report requires linking each vehicle to its current driver employee for compliance and usage tracking.']
  equipment_registry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to asset.equipment_registry. Business justification: Vehicle Asset Management report requires linking each connected vehicle to its equipment registry record for warranty, maintenance, and depreciation tracking.']
  homologation_record_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.homologation_record. Business justification: Regulatory Homologation Report requires linking each vehicle to its homologation record for market approval tracking.']
  nameplate_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to aftersales.nameplate. Business justification: OTA campaign planning requires linking each connected vehicle to its product nameplate to apply correct software version and compliance reporting.']
  owner_party_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.party. Business justification: Needed to identify the legal owner for billing, data‑privacy compliance, and service entitlement of each connected vehicle.']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Each vehicle contributes to a profit center for margin analysis; required for the Vehicle Profitability Report.']
  software_version_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to mobility.software_version. Business justification: Storing the software version as a FK enables consistent version management and eliminates duplicated version strings on the vehicle record.']
  telematics_device_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the telematics hardware installed in the vehicle.']
  vehicle_vin_registry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to vehicle.vin_registry. Business justification: Required for warranty, recall and OTA management linking each connected vehicle to the official VIN registry record.']
  activation_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the device activation.']
  activation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the device was first activated.']
  battery_health_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Estimated health of the battery relative to its original capacity.']
  battery_state_of_charge_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Current state of charge of the vehicle\'s battery expressed as a percentage.']
  connectivity_tier varchar [note: 'Service tier defining data allowance and priority for the vehicle.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the connected vehicle record was first created in the lakehouse.']
  data_plan varchar [note: 'Subscription plan governing cellular data usage.']
  data_usage_gb decimal(10,2) [note: 'Cumulative cellular data consumed by the vehicle in the current billing cycle.']
  data_usage_last_reset date [note: 'Date when the data usage counter was last reset.']
  deactivation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the device was deactivated or retired.']
  device_type varchar [note: 'Model or family of the connected telematics device.']
  diagnostic_status varchar [note: 'Overall health status derived from the latest diagnostic data.']
  firmware_version varchar [note: 'Current firmware version installed on the telematics device.']
  geographic_region varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO country code representing the primary market of the vehicle.']
  last_diagnostic_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent vehicle diagnostic event.']
  last_error_code varchar [note: 'Most recent Diagnostic Trouble Code reported by the vehicle.']
  last_ota_update_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent Over‑The‑Air software update.']
  last_tpms_update_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent TPMS firmware or configuration update.']
  last_v2x_update_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent V2X software update.']
  manufacturer varchar [note: 'Original Equipment Manufacturer of the vehicle.']
  mileage_km decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total distance traveled by the vehicle, reported in kilometers.']
  mileage_last_update timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent mileage reading.']
  model_name varchar [note: 'Marketing name of the vehicle model.']
  model_year varchar [note: 'Model year of the vehicle (calendar year).']
  ota_capability boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the vehicle supports Over-The-Air updates.']
  powertrain_type varchar [note: 'Primary propulsion technology of the vehicle.']
  registration_date date [note: 'Date the vehicle was enrolled in the connected mobility program.']
  registration_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the vehicle\'s connectivity service registration.']
  sim_iccid varchar [note: 'Integrated Circuit Card Identifier of the SIM/eSIM used for connectivity.']
  sim_imsi varchar [note: 'International Mobile Subscriber Identity associated with the SIM.']
  software_version varchar [note: 'Version of the vehicle\'s on‑board software platform.']
  subscription_active_flag boolean
  subscription_end_date date [note: 'Date when the current subscription plan expires (null if open‑ended).']
  subscription_plan varchar [note: 'Service plan governing feature entitlements for the connected vehicle.']
  subscription_start_date date [note: 'Date when the current subscription plan became effective.']
  tpms_capability boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the vehicle is equipped with Tire Pressure Monitoring System telemetry.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the connected vehicle record.']
  v2x_capability boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the vehicle can communicate Vehicle‑to‑Everything.']
  vehicle_type varchar [note: 'Broad category of the vehicle.']
  vin varchar [note: 'Globally unique identifier assigned to each vehicle by the manufacturer.']
  warranty_expiration_date date [note: 'Date when the vehicle\'s warranty coverage ends.']
  warranty_status varchar [note: 'Current warranty coverage status of the vehicle.']

  Note: 'Master registry of all connected vehicles enrolled in mobility and telematics services. Owns the connected device identity per VIN, connectivity hardware profile (Geotab/Bosch IoT device), SIM/eSIM identifiers, connectivity tier, activation status, OTA capability flags, V2X capability flags, and TPMS sensor registration. This is the SSOT for connected vehicle device identity within the mobility domain, distinct from the vehicle master in the vehicle domain which owns VIN-level manufacturing identity. Links to telematics_device for hardware asset details.'
}

Table mobility.telematics_device {
  telematics_device_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for each telematics control unit (TCU) record.']
  equipment_registry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to asset.equipment_registry. Business justification: Device Installation Management process tracks which equipment asset each telematics device is mounted on for service contracts and calibration compliance.']
  installed_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Device installation/maintenance logs need the installer employee to support warranty validation and service cost allocation.']
  supply_supplier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.supply_supplier. Business justification: Supports hardware warranty and recall process linking each telematics device to its manufacturing supplier for defect tracking.']
  battery_level_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Current battery charge level expressed as a percentage.']
  calibration_status boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the device has passed factory calibration (true) or not (false).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the telematics device record was first created in the data lake.']
  data_plan_expiration date [note: 'Date when the current data plan expires.']
  data_plan_type varchar [note: 'Type of cellular data plan assigned to the device.']
  device_make varchar [note: 'Name of the manufacturer that produced the telematics device.']
  device_model varchar [note: 'Model designation of the telematics device as defined by the manufacturer.']
  device_status varchar [note: 'Overall lifecycle status of the device.']
  firmware_version varchar [note: 'Current firmware version installed on the telematics device.']
  gps_latitude double [note: 'Most recent latitude coordinate reported by the device.']
  gps_longitude double [note: 'Most recent longitude coordinate reported by the device.']
  hardware_generation varchar [note: 'Generation identifier for the hardware platform of the device (e.g., Gen1, Gen2).']
  iccid varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the SIM card embedded in the telematics device.']
  imei varchar [note: 'Globally unique identifier for the device\'s cellular modem.']
  installation_date date [note: 'Date the telematics device was installed in the vehicle.']
  last_firmware_update timestamp [note: 'When the device last received a firmware OTA update.']
  last_heartbeat_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent health‑check ping received from the device.']
  last_maintenance_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent scheduled maintenance performed on the device.']
  maintenance_cycle_months varchar [note: 'Planned interval in months between routine maintenance events.']
  odometer_km decimal(10,2) [note: 'Cumulative distance traveled as reported by the device, in kilometers.']
  operational_status varchar [note: 'Current operational state of the device within the fleet.']
  ota_update_capability boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the device supports over‑the‑air firmware updates.']
  owner_type varchar [note: 'Entity type that owns the device.']
  provisioning_date date [note: 'Date the device was provisioned and linked to the fleet management system.']
  signal_strength_dbm varchar [note: 'Cellular signal strength measured in decibel‑milliwatts.']
  subscription_context_flag boolean
  temperature_c double [note: 'Internal temperature of the telematics unit in degrees Celsius.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the telematics device record.']
  v2x_enabled boolean [note: 'Flag indicating if vehicle‑to‑everything communication is enabled on the device.']
  vehicle_vin varchar [note: 'VIN of the vehicle in which the device is installed.']
  warranty_expiration_date date [note: 'Date when the device\'s manufacturer warranty ends.']

  Note: 'Master record for each physical or embedded telematics control unit (TCU) installed in a vehicle. Tracks device make, model, firmware version, IMEI, ICCID, hardware generation, installation date, calibration status, and operational health. Sourced from Geotab and Bosch IoT device registries. Distinct from connected_vehicle which is the vehicle-level enrollment record; telematics_device is the hardware asset record.'
}

Table mobility.service {
  service_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the mobility service offering.']
  manager_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Service management dashboards track which employee manages each mobility service for SLA compliance and accountability.']
  nameplate_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to aftersales.nameplate. Business justification: Service eligibility matrix links services to specific vehicle nameplates for subscription eligibility and regulatory reporting.']
  service_tier_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the pricing tier applicable to the service.']
  supply_supplier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.supply_supplier. Business justification: Needed for Service Supplier Management report that tracks which external supplier provides each mobility service for billing, compliance, and warranty.']
  billing_cycle varchar [note: 'Frequency of billing for the service.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the service record was first created.']
  currency varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 currency code for the service price.']
  data_governance_ref varchar [note: 'Reference to data governance catalog entry for lineage and ownership tracking']
  data_privacy_level varchar [note: 'Classifies the privacy level of data collected by the service.']
  data_retention_period_days varchar [note: 'Number of days the service usage data is retained.']
  device_compatibility varchar [note: 'List of compatible telematics devices or platforms (e.g., Bosch, Geotab).']
  eligibility_rules varchar [note: 'Business rules that determine which customers or vehicles may subscribe to the service.']
  end_of_service_date date [note: 'Date when the service is retired or no longer offered.']
  field_service_enabled_flag boolean [note: 'Whether this mobility service supports field service dispatch integration']
  field_service_info varchar [note: 'Field service related information, consolidating field service concepts.']
  is_premium boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the service is classified as a premium offering.']
  launch_date date [note: 'Date when the service became available to customers.']
  max_simultaneous_devices varchar [note: 'Maximum number of vehicle devices that can be linked to a single subscription.']
  ota_update_capability boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the service includes over‑the‑air software updates.']
  predictive_maintenance_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the service provides predictive maintenance alerts.']
  price decimal(10,2) [note: 'Base price of the service before taxes or discounts.']
  provider varchar [note: 'Internal business unit or external partner delivering the service.']
  regulatory_approval_date date [note: 'Date when regulatory approval was granted.']
  regulatory_approval_status varchar [note: 'Regulatory compliance status of the service.']
  remote_diagnostics_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether remote vehicle health diagnostics are part of the service.']
  service_category varchar [note: 'Broad classification of the service (e.g., remote diagnostics, OTA updates, V2X). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: remote_diagnostics|predictive_maintenance|ota_update|tpms_monitoring|v2x|fleet_telematics|autonomous_feature_package|usage_based_insurance — promote to reference product]']
  service_code varchar [note: 'External business code used to reference the service in contracts and catalogs.']
  service_description varchar [note: 'Detailed textual description of the service offering.']
  service_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the mobility service.']
  service_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the service offering.']
  service_type varchar
  sla_description varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the service level agreement terms.']
  sla_hours varchar [note: 'Target response time in hours for service‑related incidents.']
  subscription_context_flag boolean
  subscription_type varchar [note: 'Billing model for the service.']
  supported_vehicle_models varchar [note: 'List or pattern of vehicle models (by platform or VIN prefix) that can use the service.']
  tax_included boolean [note: 'Indicates whether tax is included in the listed price.']
  tax_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Applicable tax rate as a percentage when tax is not included.']
  terms_url varchar [note: 'Link to the legal terms and conditions governing the service.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the service record.']
  usage_based_insurance_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the service enables usage‑based insurance functionality.']
  usage_limit decimal(10,2) [note: 'Maximum allowed usage quantity (e.g., miles, hours) per billing period.']
  usage_metric varchar [note: 'Metric used to measure consumption for usage‑based billing (e.g., data_volume, event_count).']
  usage_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the usage limit.']
  v2x_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the service provides vehicle‑to‑everything communication features.']
  version varchar [note: 'Version identifier for the service definition.']
  version_release_date date [note: 'Date when the current service version was released.']

  Note: 'Catalog of all connected mobility service offerings available for subscription or entitlement, including remote diagnostics, predictive maintenance alerts, OTA update service, TPMS monitoring, V2X communication, fleet telematics, autonomous driving feature packages, and usage-based insurance enablement. Defines service name, category, eligibility rules, supported vehicle platforms, pricing tier reference, and service lifecycle (launch date, EOP date). This is the SSOT for mobility service definitions.'
}

Table mobility.service_subscription {
  service_subscription_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for each mobility service subscription record.']
  connected_vehicle_id varchar
  dealership_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to dealer.dealership. Business justification: Subscription Sales Attribution Report attributes each service subscription to the dealer that sold it for revenue tracking.']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Subscription fees are booked to a revenue GL account; needed for the Subscription Billing and Revenue Recognition process.']
  sales_rep_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Sales commission reports require linking each subscription to the sales representative employee who closed the deal.']
  service_customer_party_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the customer who owns the subscription.']
  service_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific connected mobility service offering.']
  service_party_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the customer who owns the subscription.']
  vehicle_vin_registry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to vehicle.vin_registry. Business justification: Needed to tie subscription eligibility, billing and compliance directly to the vehicle master record for accurate invoicing and regulatory reporting.']
  auto_renewal_flag boolean [note: 'True if the subscription is set to renew automatically at the end of the term.']
  billing_amount decimal(10,2) [note: 'Base amount charged per billing cycle before taxes, fees, or discounts.']
  billing_cycle varchar [note: 'Frequency at which the subscription is billed.']
  cancellation_date date [note: 'Date when the subscription was cancelled by the customer or provider.']
  cancellation_reason varchar [note: 'Reason provided for terminating the subscription.']
  compliance_gdpr_consent boolean [note: 'True if the subscriber has provided consent for data processing under GDPR.']
  contract_terms varchar [note: 'Free‑text description of the contractual obligations, service level agreements, and special conditions.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the subscription record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three‑letter currency code for the billing amount.']
  data_governance_ref varchar [note: 'Reference to data governance catalog entry for lineage and ownership tracking']
  data_sharing_opt_in boolean [note: 'True if the subscriber agrees to share usage data with third‑party partners.']
  end_date date [note: 'Date when the subscription terminates or expires; null for open‑ended agreements.']
  entitlement_tier varchar [note: 'Level of service features and data allowances granted to the subscriber.']
  last_modified_by varchar [note: 'User identifier of the person who performed the most recent update.']
  last_payment_date date [note: 'Date when the most recent subscription payment was received.']
  next_payment_due date [note: 'Scheduled date for the upcoming subscription payment.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional free‑form comments or remarks about the subscription.']
  overage_amount decimal(10,2) [note: 'Monetary charge applied for usage beyond the allocated limit.']
  overage_fee_applied boolean [note: 'True if usage exceeded the limit and an overage fee was charged.']
  payment_method varchar [note: 'Instrument used for subscription billing.']
  payment_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the most recent payment transaction.']
  price_amount decimal(18,2)
  privacy_policy_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the privacy policy accepted at subscription time.']
  promo_discount_amount decimal(10,2) [note: 'Monetary value of the discount granted by the promotional code.']
  promo_end_date date [note: 'Date when the promotional discount expires.']
  promo_start_date date [note: 'Date when the promotional discount becomes effective.']
  promotional_code varchar [note: 'Code applied to the subscription for a discount or special offer.']
  renewal_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next automatic renewal, if auto‑renewal is enabled.']
  service_subscription_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the subscription.']
  start_date date [note: 'Date when the subscription becomes effective.']
  subscription_context_flag boolean
  subscription_number varchar [note: 'External reference number assigned to the subscription agreement, used in billing and customer communications.']
  subscription_status varchar
  subscription_type varchar [note: 'Category of the connected mobility service (e.g., telematics, infotainment, remote diagnostics).']
  trial_end_date date [note: 'Date when the trial period expires and regular billing begins.']
  trial_flag boolean [note: 'True if the subscription is currently in a free‑trial period.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the most recent modification to the subscription record.']
  usage_limit decimal(10,2) [note: 'Maximum allowed usage (e.g., data, minutes) per billing cycle.']
  usage_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for usage (e.g., gigabytes, minutes).']
  usage_used decimal(10,2) [note: 'Actual usage recorded for the current billing cycle.']

  Note: 'Active and historical mobility service subscription agreements between a customer and a connected mobility service. Captures subscription start/end dates, billing cycle, entitlement tier, auto-renewal flag, cancellation reason, trial period flag, and subscription status. Links customer identity (from customer domain) to a mobility_service. This is the SSOT for mobility service entitlements and active agreements per vehicle/customer.'
}

Table mobility.telemetry_event {
  telemetry_event_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for each telemetry event record.']
  connected_vehicle_id varchar
  equipment_registry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to asset.equipment_registry. Business justification: Telemetry Data Integration report joins raw telemetry events with equipment assets for reliability analysis and warranty claim validation.']
  individual_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the driver associated with the vehicle at event time.']
  trip_id varchar [note: 'Identifier linking the telemetry event to a specific vehicle trip or route.']
  vehicle_vin_registry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to vehicle.vin_registry. Business justification: Allows raw telemetry events to be associated with the official vehicle record for diagnostics, safety reporting and emissions compliance.']
  altitude double [note: 'Elevation above sea level of the vehicle at event time, in meters.']
  battery_soc_percent double [note: 'Current electric battery charge level for EV/HEV vehicles, expressed as a percentage.']
  battery_voltage_volt double [note: 'Instantaneous voltage of the vehicle\'s high‑voltage battery, expressed in volts.']
  charging_status boolean [note: 'True if the vehicle is currently connected to a charger and charging, otherwise false.']
  connectivity_status boolean [note: 'True if the telematics device had an active network connection at event time.']
  engine_rpm varchar [note: 'Engine speed measured in revolutions per minute at the time of the event.']
  engine_temperature_c double [note: 'Measured temperature of the engine coolant at event time, in degrees Celsius.']
  event_sequence varchar [note: 'Monotonically increasing sequence number for events from the same vehicle, used for ordering.']
  event_source varchar [note: 'Identifier of the source system that produced the telemetry record (e.g., Geotab, Bosch).']
  event_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the telemetry event was generated by the vehicle sensor.']
  event_type varchar
  event_type_code varchar [note: 'Code that categorizes the type of telemetry event (e.g., speed, harsh_brake, idle).']
  firmware_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the telematics device firmware that generated the event.']
  fuel_level_percent double [note: 'Remaining fuel level expressed as a percentage of tank capacity.']
  gps_accuracy_m double [note: 'Estimated horizontal accuracy of the GPS position in meters.']
  heading_degrees double [note: 'Compass direction the vehicle is facing at event time, expressed in degrees from true north.']
  ignition_state boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the vehicle ignition is on (true) or off (false) at the moment of the event.']
  latitude double [note: 'Geographic latitude of the vehicle at event time, expressed in decimal degrees.']
  latitude_accuracy double [note: 'Estimated vertical accuracy of the latitude measurement.']
  location_city varchar [note: 'City name derived from the GPS coordinates at event time.']
  location_country varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO country code derived from the GPS coordinates at event time.']
  location_state varchar [note: 'State or province name derived from the GPS coordinates at event time.']
  longitude double [note: 'Geographic longitude of the vehicle at event time, expressed in decimal degrees.']
  longitude_accuracy double [note: 'Estimated vertical accuracy of the longitude measurement.']
  odometer_km double [note: 'Cumulative distance traveled by the vehicle, recorded in kilometers.']
  raw_payload varchar [note: 'Original JSON payload received from the telematics device before any transformation.']
  record_audit_created timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the telemetry record was first ingested into the silver layer.']
  record_audit_updated timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the telemetry record in the lakehouse.']
  signal_quality varchar [note: 'Qualitative assessment of the telemetry signal (e.g., good, moderate, poor).']
  speed_kmh decimal(6,2)
  speed_kph double [note: 'Instantaneous vehicle speed at event time, measured in kilometers per hour.']
  subscription_context_flag boolean
  tire_pressure_front_left_psi double [note: 'Air pressure of the front‑left tire measured in pounds per square inch.']
  tire_pressure_front_right_psi double [note: 'Air pressure of the front‑right tire measured in pounds per square inch.']
  tire_pressure_rear_left_psi double [note: 'Air pressure of the rear‑left tire measured in pounds per square inch.']
  tire_pressure_rear_right_psi double [note: 'Air pressure of the rear‑right tire measured in pounds per square inch.']

  Note: '[FIELD SERVICES COVERAGE] connected-vehicle telemetry building block feeding proactive field service triggers | High-frequency transactional record of raw and processed telematics events streamed from connected vehicles via Geotab or Bosch IoT. Each record captures event timestamp, VIN reference, GPS coordinates (latitude, longitude, altitude), vehicle speed, heading, ignition state, odometer reading, fuel level, battery state of charge (for EV/HEV), engine RPM, and event type code. Silver layer record represents cleansed and deduplicated stream from the bronze ingestion layer.'
}

Table mobility.trip {
  trip_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for the trip record.']
  connected_vehicle_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the vehicle that performed the trip.']
